--
-- Name: X_parse_json_rules_to_sql_to_be_deleted(jsonb, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."X_parse_json_rules_to_sql_to_be_deleted"(p_conditions jsonb, p_entity_alias text) RETURNS text
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_type TEXT;
    v_rules JSONB;
    v_rule JSONB;
    v_sql_parts TEXT[] := ARRAY[]::TEXT[];
    v_sql TEXT := '';
    v_field TEXT;
    v_operator TEXT;
    v_value JSONB;
    v_field_parts TEXT[];
    v_field_alias TEXT;
    v_column_name TEXT;
    v_sql_field TEXT;
    v_sql_value TEXT;
    v_value_str TEXT;
    v_is_numeric BOOLEAN;
    v_is_timestamp_field BOOLEAN;
BEGIN
    v_type := p_conditions->>'type';
    v_rules := p_conditions->'rules';

    IF v_rules IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_rules) = 0 THEN
        RETURN ''; -- No conditions
    END IF;

    FOR v_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_rules)
    LOOP
        IF v_rule->'type' IS NOT NULL THEN
            -- Nested group
            v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, '( ' || automation.parse_json_rules_to_sql(v_rule, p_entity_alias) || ' )');
        ELSE
            -- Single condition
            v_field := v_rule->>'field';
            v_operator := v_rule->>'operator';
            v_value := v_rule->'value';
            v_value_str := v_value->>0; -- Get string representation
            
            -- FIX: Correct numeric detection
            v_is_numeric := jsonb_typeof(v_value) = 'number';

            -- FIX: Detect timestamp fields
            v_is_timestamp_field := (
                v_field LIKE '%created_at%' OR 
                v_field LIKE '%updated_at%' OR 
                v_field LIKE '%due_date%' OR 
                v_field LIKE '%event_%_at%' OR
                v_field LIKE '%scheduled_%_time%' OR
                v_field LIKE '%actual_%_time%'
            );

            -- 1. Determine the SQL for the FIELD
            v_field_alias := split_part(v_field, '.', 1);
            v_column_name := split_part(v_field, '.', 2);

            IF v_column_name LIKE '%->>%' THEN
                -- It's a JSONB field, e.g., "entity.details->>scheduled_start_time"
                v_field_parts := string_to_array(v_column_name, '->>');
                v_sql_field := format('%I.%I->>%L', v_field_alias, v_field_parts[1], v_field_parts[2]);
                
                -- FIX: JSONB extracted fields are TEXT, need explicit casting for timestamps
                IF v_is_timestamp_field THEN
                    v_sql_field := v_sql_field || '::timestamptz';
                END IF;
            ELSE
                -- It's a regular column, e.g., "esm.updated_at" or "entity.escalation_level"
                v_sql_field := format('%I.%I', v_field_alias, v_column_name);
                
                -- FIX: Add timestamp casting for regular timestamp columns
                IF v_is_timestamp_field AND v_operator NOT IN ('is_null', 'is_not_null') THEN
                    v_sql_field := v_sql_field || '::timestamptz';
                END IF;
            END IF;

            -- 2. Determine the SQL for the VALUE
            IF v_value::text = '"NOW()"' THEN
                v_sql_value := 'NOW()::timestamptz'; -- FIX: Cast NOW() to timestamptz
            ELSIF v_value_str IN ('due_date', 'created_at', 'updated_at', 'scheduled_start_time', 'scheduled_end_time', 'actual_start_time', 'actual_end_time') THEN
                -- This is a column reference, needs alias and casting
                v_sql_value := format('%I.%I::timestamptz', v_field_alias, v_value_str);
            ELSIF v_is_numeric THEN
                v_sql_value := v_value#>>'{}'; -- Extract numeric value as string
            ELSE
                v_sql_value := quote_literal(v_value_str); -- String literal
                
                -- FIX: If comparing timestamp field with string literal, cast the value too
                IF v_is_timestamp_field AND v_operator NOT IN ('is_null', 'is_not_null') THEN
                    v_sql_value := v_sql_value || '::timestamptz';
                END IF;
            END IF;

            -- 3. Build the SQL statement
            CASE v_operator
                WHEN 'equals' THEN
                    v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s = %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                WHEN 'not_equals' THEN
                    v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s != %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                WHEN 'less_than' THEN
                    IF v_field LIKE '%escalation_level%' THEN
                        v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('COALESCE(%s::int, 0) < %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                    ELSE
                        v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s < %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                    END IF;
                WHEN 'greater_than' THEN
                     IF v_field LIKE '%escalation_level%' THEN
                        v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('COALESCE(%s::int, 0) > %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                    ELSE
                        v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s > %s', v_sql_field, v_sql_value));
                    END IF;
                WHEN 'is_null' THEN
                    v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s IS NULL', v_sql_field));
                WHEN 'is_not_null' THEN
                    v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s IS NOT NULL', v_sql_field));
                WHEN 'hours_ago' THEN
                     v_sql_parts := array_append(v_sql_parts, format('%s < (NOW() - interval %L)', v_sql_field, v_value_str || ' hours'));
                ELSE
                    RAISE WARNING 'Unknown operator %', v_operator;
            END CASE;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    v_sql := array_to_string(v_sql_parts, ' ' || v_type || ' ');
    
    IF v_sql != '' THEN
        RETURN ' AND (' || v_sql || ')';
    ELSE
        RETURN '';
    END IF;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: _resolve_assignment(jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation._resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$
DECLARE
    v_assignment_rule TEXT := p_assignment_config->>'rule';
    v_sources JSONB := p_assignment_config->'sources';
    v_pool_logic TEXT := COALESCE(p_assignment_config->>'pool_logic', 'OR');
    v_source JSONB;
    v_resolved_user_id UUID; -- The final user ID to return
    v_candidate_users JSONB[]; -- Array of JSONB objects like [{"id": "uuid", "last_assigned_at_for_source": "timestamp"}]
    v_user_resolver_config JSONB;
    v_rr_source_id UUID; -- The ID of the team/role used for round-robin tracking
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate config
    IF v_assignment_rule IS NULL OR v_sources IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_sources) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(v_sources) = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'Assignment config must include a "rule" and a non-empty "sources" array.';
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Handle 'direct' assignment immediately (only makes sense if it's the only source or logic is OR)
    IF v_pool_logic = 'OR' THEN
        FOR v_source IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_sources) LOOP
            IF v_source->>'type' = 'direct' THEN
                DECLARE v_user_id_text TEXT := p_flattened_data->>trim(both '{{}}' FROM (v_source->>'expression'));
                BEGIN
                    IF v_user_id_text IS NOT NULL THEN 
                        RETURN v_user_id_text::UUID; -- Return immediately if direct user found
                    END IF;
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Direct assignment expression did not resolve to a valid UUID: %', v_user_id_text;
                    -- Continue loop to check other sources
                END;
            END IF;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    -- 3. If not direct or logic is AND, resolve the pool of users using _util_resolve_users
    -- We need the user ID and the last_assigned_at specific to the source for round-robin.
    -- This requires a more complex `select_columns` for _util_resolve_users.
    v_user_resolver_config := jsonb_build_object(
        'pool_logic', v_pool_logic,
        -- Select user ID and source-specific last_assigned_at
        'select_columns', $$
            id, 
            COALESCE(
                (SELECT last_assigned_at_for_source FROM (
                    SELECT user_id, last_assigned_at AS last_assigned_at_for_source, team_id AS source_id FROM identity.user_teams
                    UNION ALL 
                    SELECT user_id, last_assigned_at AS last_assigned_at_for_source, role_id AS source_id FROM identity.user_roles
                ) AS rr_data WHERE rr_data.user_id = u.id ORDER BY last_assigned_at_for_source ASC NULLS FIRST LIMIT 1),
                'infinity'::timestamptz -- Treat users never assigned as lowest priority
            ) AS last_assigned_at_for_source,
            (SELECT source_id FROM (
                    SELECT user_id, team_id AS source_id FROM identity.user_teams
                    UNION ALL 
                    SELECT user_id, role_id AS source_id FROM identity.user_roles
                ) AS rr_data WHERE rr_data.user_id = u.id ORDER BY last_assigned_at_for_source ASC NULLS FIRST LIMIT 1
            ) AS rr_source_id -- Also get the source ID for updating state
        $$,
        'sources', v_sources -- Pass the original sources
    );

    v_candidate_users := automation._util_resolve_users(v_user_resolver_config, p_flattened_data);

    IF v_candidate_users IS NULL OR array_length(v_candidate_users, 1) = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'No eligible users found for assignment based on sources.';
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    -- 4. Apply assignment rule (only round_robin implemented here)
    IF v_assignment_rule = 'round_robin' THEN
        DECLARE 
            v_best_candidate JSONB;
            v_last_assigned_user_id UUID;
        BEGIN
            -- Sort candidates by last_assigned_at (nulls first), then pick the top one
            SELECT elem INTO v_best_candidate 
            FROM unnest(v_candidate_users) elem
            ORDER BY (elem->>'last_assigned_at_for_source')::timestamptz ASC NULLS FIRST 
            LIMIT 1;

            IF v_best_candidate IS NULL THEN RETURN NULL; END IF; -- Should not happen if array wasn't empty

            v_resolved_user_id := (v_best_candidate->>'id')::UUID;
            v_rr_source_id     := (v_best_candidate->>'rr_source_id')::UUID; -- Get source ID for state update

            -- Update the timestamp for the specific source (team/role) if applicable
            IF v_rr_source_id IS NOT NULL THEN
                 -- This uses the NEW state table automation.ut_rr_assignment_state
                 INSERT INTO automation.ut_rr_assignment_state (source_id, last_assigned_user_id)
                 VALUES (v_rr_source_id, v_resolved_user_id)
                 ON CONFLICT (source_id) DO UPDATE SET
                    last_assigned_user_id = EXCLUDED.last_assigned_user_id; 
                    -- updated_at is handled by trigger
            ELSE
                 RAISE WARNING 'Round-robin selected user % with no trackable source_id (likely direct or user type)', v_resolved_user_id;
            END IF;
        END;
    ELSE
        RAISE WARNING 'Unsupported assignment rule: %', v_assignment_rule;
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    RETURN v_resolved_user_id;
END;
$_$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION _resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation._resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb) IS 'Resolves a user ID for assignment based on configuration (team, role, direct) and rules (round-robin). Uses _util_resolve_users. Internal helper function.';


--
-- Name: _resolve_assignment(jsonb, jsonb, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation._resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$
DECLARE
    v_assignment_rule TEXT := p_assignment_config->>'rule';
    v_sources JSONB := p_assignment_config->'sources';
    v_pool_logic TEXT := COALESCE(p_assignment_config->>'pool_logic', 'OR');
    v_source JSONB;
    v_resolved_user_id UUID;
    v_candidate_users JSONB[]; -- Array of [{"id": "uuid", "last_assigned_at_for_source": "ts", "rr_source_id": "uuid", "rr_source_type": "text"}]
    v_user_resolver_config JSONB;
    v_rr_source_id UUID;
    v_rr_source_type TEXT; -- Added source type
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate config
    IF v_assignment_rule IS NULL OR v_sources IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_sources) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(v_sources) = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING '[_resolve_assignment] Config must include "rule" and non-empty "sources".';
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Handle 'direct' assignment immediately if OR logic
    IF v_pool_logic = 'OR' THEN
        FOR v_source IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_sources) LOOP
            IF v_source->>'type' = 'direct' THEN
                DECLARE v_user_id_text TEXT := p_flattened_data->>trim(both '{{}}' FROM (v_source->>'expression'));
                BEGIN
                    IF v_user_id_text IS NOT NULL THEN RETURN v_user_id_text::UUID; END IF;
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN RAISE WARNING '[_resolve_assignment] Direct expr did not resolve to UUID: %', v_user_id_text; END;
            END IF;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Resolve user pool via _util_resolve_users
    v_user_resolver_config := jsonb_build_object(
        'pool_logic', v_pool_logic,
        -- Select ID, source-specific last_assigned_at, source_id, and source_type
        'select_columns', $$
            id,
            COALESCE(rr_data.last_assigned_at_for_source, '-infinity'::timestamptz) AS last_assigned_at_for_source, -- Use -infinity for NULLS FIRST
            rr_data.source_id AS rr_source_id,
            rr_data.source_type AS rr_source_type
        $$,
         -- Subquery to get last assigned time PER SOURCE for each user
        'join_subquery', $$
            LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
                SELECT
                    state.last_assigned_user_id,
                    state.updated_at as last_assigned_at_for_source,
                    state.source_id,
                    state.source_type
                FROM automation.ut_rr_assignment_state state
                WHERE state.organization_id = $1 -- Pass org_id here
                  AND state.last_assigned_user_id = u.id -- Correlate with outer user
                ORDER BY state.updated_at DESC -- Get the most recent if multiple sources map to same user
                LIMIT 1
            ) rr_data ON true
        $$,
        'sources', v_sources
    );

    -- Pass p_organization_id to the user resolver IF it uses the join_subquery parameter
    v_candidate_users := automation._util_resolve_users(v_user_resolver_config, p_flattened_data, p_organization_id); -- Assumes _util_resolve_users accepts org_id

    IF v_candidate_users IS NULL OR array_length(v_candidate_users, 1) = 0 THEN
        RAISE WARNING '[_resolve_assignment] No eligible users found for assignment.';
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    -- 4. Apply assignment rule (round_robin)
    IF v_assignment_rule = 'round_robin' THEN
        DECLARE
            v_best_candidate JSONB;
        BEGIN
            -- Sort candidates by last_assigned_at (nulls/-infinity first)
            SELECT elem INTO v_best_candidate
            FROM unnest(v_candidate_users) elem
            ORDER BY (elem->>'last_assigned_at_for_source')::timestamptz ASC NULLS FIRST -- NULLS FIRST handles -infinity too
            LIMIT 1;

            IF v_best_candidate IS NULL THEN RETURN NULL; END IF;

            v_resolved_user_id := (v_best_candidate->>'id')::UUID;
            v_rr_source_id     := (v_best_candidate->>'rr_source_id')::UUID;
            v_rr_source_type   := v_best_candidate->>'rr_source_type'; -- Get source type

            -- Update state table using composite key
            IF v_rr_source_id IS NOT NULL AND v_rr_source_type IS NOT NULL THEN
                 INSERT INTO automation.ut_rr_assignment_state (organization_id, source_type, source_id, last_assigned_user_id)
                 VALUES (p_organization_id, v_rr_source_type, v_rr_source_id, v_resolved_user_id)
                 ON CONFLICT (organization_id, source_type, source_id) DO UPDATE SET
                    last_assigned_user_id = EXCLUDED.last_assigned_user_id;
                    -- updated_at is handled by trigger
            ELSE
                 RAISE WARNING '[_resolve_assignment] Round-robin selected user % with no trackable source_id/type', v_resolved_user_id;
            END IF;
        END;
    ELSE
        RAISE WARNING '[_resolve_assignment] Unsupported assignment rule: %', v_assignment_rule;
        RETURN NULL;
    END IF;

    RETURN v_resolved_user_id;
END;
$_$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION _resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb, p_organization_id uuid); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation._resolve_assignment(p_assignment_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb, p_organization_id uuid) IS 'Resolves a user ID for assignment based on configuration (team, role, direct) and rules (round-robin). Uses _util_resolve_users and ut_rr_assignment_state. Internal helper function.';


--
-- Name: _resolve_recipient(jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation._resolve_recipient(p_recipient_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb) RETURNS text[]
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$
DECLARE
    v_all_emails TEXT[] := ARRAY[]::TEXT[];
    v_sources JSONB;
    v_pool_logic TEXT;
    v_source JSONB;
    v_user_sources JSONB[] := ARRAY[]::JSONB[]; -- For collecting team/role sources
BEGIN
    IF p_recipient_config IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_recipient_config) != 'object' OR NOT (p_recipient_config ? 'sources') THEN
        RETURN v_all_emails;
    END IF;

    v_sources := p_recipient_config->'sources';
    IF v_sources IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_sources) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(v_sources) = 0 THEN
        RETURN v_all_emails;
    END IF;

    v_pool_logic := COALESCE(p_recipient_config->>'pool_logic', 'OR');

    FOR v_source IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_sources) LOOP
        IF v_source->>'type' = 'direct' THEN
            DECLARE
                v_direct_emails TEXT[] := ARRAY[]::TEXT[];
                v_expr_path TEXT;
                v_resolved_val JSONB;
            BEGIN
                IF v_source->'expressions' IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_source->'expressions') != 'array' THEN
                    CONTINUE;
                END IF;
                FOR v_expr_path IN SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(v_source->'expressions') LOOP
                    v_expr_path := regexp_replace(v_expr_path, '[{}]', '', 'g');
                    v_resolved_val := p_flattened_data -> v_expr_path;
                    IF v_resolved_val IS NOT NULL THEN
                        IF jsonb_typeof(v_resolved_val) = 'array' THEN
                            v_direct_emails := v_direct_emails || ARRAY(SELECT value FROM jsonb_array_elements_text(v_resolved_val) WHERE value IS NOT NULL AND value <> '');
                        ELSIF jsonb_typeof(v_resolved_val) = 'string' THEN
                            IF v_resolved_val#>>'{}' <> '' THEN
                                v_direct_emails := array_append(v_direct_emails, v_resolved_val#>>'{}');
                            END IF;
                        END IF;
                    END IF;
                END LOOP;
                v_all_emails := v_all_emails || v_direct_emails;
            END;
        ELSIF v_source->>'type' IN ('team', 'role', 'user') THEN
             v_user_sources := array_append(v_user_sources, v_source);
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF array_length(v_user_sources, 1) > 0 THEN
        DECLARE
            v_user_resolver_config JSONB;
            -- ▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼
            v_resolved_user_emails_jsonb JSONB[]; -- Variable to hold the JSONB array from the mock
            v_resolved_user_emails_text TEXT[];  -- Variable to hold the converted TEXT array
            -- ▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲
        BEGIN
            v_user_resolver_config := jsonb_build_object(
                'pool_logic', v_pool_logic,
                'select_columns', $$details->>'email'$$, -- Still ask for email text
                'sources', to_jsonb(v_user_sources)
            );

            -- Call the user resolver (mock returns JSONB[] containing JSONB strings)
            v_resolved_user_emails_jsonb := automation._util_resolve_users(v_user_resolver_config, p_flattened_data, NULL); -- Pass NULL org_id for mock

            -- ▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼
            -- FIX: Convert the JSONB array of strings ["email1", "email2"] into a TEXT array {'email1', 'email2'}
            SELECT array_agg(elem #>> '{}')
            INTO v_resolved_user_emails_text
            FROM unnest(COALESCE(v_resolved_user_emails_jsonb, ARRAY[]::JSONB[])) elem;
            -- ▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲

            -- Concatenate the resulting TEXT array
            v_all_emails := v_all_emails || COALESCE(v_resolved_user_emails_text, ARRAY[]::TEXT[]);
        END;
    END IF;

    RETURN ARRAY(SELECT DISTINCT email FROM unnest(COALESCE(v_all_emails, ARRAY[]::TEXT[])) AS email WHERE email IS NOT NULL AND email <> '');
END;
$_$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION _resolve_recipient(p_recipient_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation._resolve_recipient(p_recipient_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb) IS '[v2] Resolves email addresses based on config. Uses _util_resolve_users. Handles mock JSONB[] return. Internal helper.';


--
-- Name: _util_flatten_jsonb(jsonb, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation._util_flatten_jsonb(p_data jsonb, p_prefix text DEFAULT ''::text) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_result JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_rec RECORD;
    v_new_key TEXT;
BEGIN
    IF p_data IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_data) != 'object' THEN
        RETURN v_result;
    END IF;
    FOR v_rec IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_data) LOOP
        v_new_key := p_prefix || v_rec.key;
        IF jsonb_typeof(v_rec.value) = 'object' THEN
            v_result := v_result || automation._util_flatten_jsonb(v_rec.value, v_new_key || '.');
        ELSE
            v_result := jsonb_set(v_result, ARRAY[v_new_key], v_rec.value);
        END IF;
    END LOOP;
    RETURN v_result;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION _util_flatten_jsonb(p_data jsonb, p_prefix text); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation._util_flatten_jsonb(p_data jsonb, p_prefix text) IS 'Recursively flattens a JSONB object into a single level with dot-separated keys. Internal helper function.';


--
-- Name: bp_deactivate(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.bp_deactivate(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_org_id UUID;
    v_entity_schema TEXT;
    v_entity_type TEXT;
    v_name TEXT;
    v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family UUID[];
    -- ▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼▼
    v_deactivated_job_status TEXT := 'pending'; -- FIX: Use allowed 'pending' status
    -- ▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲▲
BEGIN
    -- Step 1: Find the identifying characteristics
    SELECT organization_id, entity_schema, entity_type, name
    INTO v_org_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_name
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;
    IF NOT FOUND THEN RAISE WARNING 'Blueprint with ID % not found...', p_blueprint_id; RETURN; END IF;

    -- Step 2: Aggregate all version IDs in this family
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE organization_id = v_org_id AND entity_schema = v_entity_schema AND entity_type = v_entity_type AND name = v_name;

    -- Step 3: Deactivate blueprint versions
    UPDATE automation.bp_process_blueprints SET is_active = false, updated_at = now()
    WHERE id = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family);

    -- Step 4: Deactivate ESM definitions
    UPDATE automation.esm_definitions SET is_active = false, updated_at = now()
    WHERE (metadata->>'blueprint_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family);

    -- Step 5: Deactivate rules
    UPDATE automation.wf_rules SET is_active = false, updated_at = now()
    WHERE (metadata->>'blueprint_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family);

    -- Step 6: Set scheduled jobs status (using allowed value)
    UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
    SET status = v_deactivated_job_status, -- Use 'pending'
        updated_at = now()
    WHERE (metadata->>'blueprint_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_in_family);
    -- Rely on scheduler checking wf_rules.is_active = false

    RAISE NOTICE 'Deactivated blueprint family (Name: %, Entity: %.%) and associated artifacts.', v_name, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: bp_recompile_active(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.bp_recompile_active(p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_blueprint RECORD;
BEGIN
    FOR v_blueprint IN 
        SELECT id FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
        WHERE is_active = true 
          AND (organization_id = p_organization_id OR p_organization_id IS NULL)
    LOOP
        PERFORM automation.comp_core_compile(v_blueprint.id, p_organization_id);
    END LOOP;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: bp_resolve_modular_definition(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.bp_resolve_modular_definition(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_resolved jsonb := p_definition;
    v_template_key text;
    v_template_def jsonb;
    v_sla_rules jsonb := '[]'::jsonb;
    v_sla RECORD;
    v_frag_def jsonb;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Handle Template Inheritance
    v_template_key := v_resolved->>'template_key';
    IF v_template_key IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT definition INTO v_template_def FROM automation.bp_templates WHERE key = v_template_key;
        IF FOUND THEN
            -- Merge template into definition (definition takes precedence for existing keys)
            v_resolved := v_template_def || v_resolved;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Resolve SLA Fragments
    IF v_resolved ? 'sla_rules' THEN
        FOR v_sla IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_resolved->'sla_rules') LOOP
            IF v_sla.value ? 'use_fragment' THEN
                SELECT definition INTO v_frag_def FROM automation.bp_fragments WHERE key = v_sla.value->>'use_fragment';
                IF FOUND THEN
                    -- Overlay any local overrides from the blueprint
                    v_sla_rules := v_sla_rules || (v_frag_def || v_sla.value);
                ELSE
                    v_sla_rules := v_sla_rules || v_sla.value;
                END IF;
            ELSE
                v_sla_rules := v_sla_rules || v_sla.value;
            END IF;
        END LOOP;
        v_resolved := jsonb_set(v_resolved, '{sla_rules}', v_sla_rules);
    END IF;

    RETURN v_resolved;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_artifacts_esm(uuid, jsonb, integer, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_artifacts_esm(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_definition jsonb, p_version integer, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_esm_def_id UUID; 
    v_esm_definitions_json JSONB; 
    v_stage JSONB; 
    v_stages JSONB[] := ARRAY[]::JSONB[];
    v_seq INT := 1; 
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema'; 
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type';
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_metadata JSONB;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT := COALESCE(p_definition->>'blueprint_type', 'lifecycle');
BEGIN
    FOR v_stage IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') LOOP
        v_stage := v_stage || jsonb_build_object(
            'on_entry_event_name', format('%s.%s.stage.%s.entered', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage->>'id'),
            'on_exit_event_name', format('%s.%s.stage.%s.exited', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage->>'id'),
            'sequence', v_seq,
            'category', v_stage->>'category'
        );
        v_stages := array_append(v_stages, v_stage);
        v_seq := v_seq + 1;
    END LOOP;
    v_esm_definitions_json := jsonb_build_object(
        'name', p_definition->>'name', 
        'entity_type', v_entity_type, 
        'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
        'startStateId', p_definition->'lifecycle'->>'start_stage', 
        'stages', to_jsonb(v_stages),
        'transitions', p_definition->'lifecycle'->'transitions'
    );
    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
        p_blueprint_id, 
        p_version, 
        NULL, 
        NULL, 
        jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
            'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type
        )
    );
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_definitions (
        organization_id, name, entity_schema, entity_type, definitions, version, 
        is_active, blueprint_id, metadata
    )
    VALUES (
        p_organization_id, 
        p_definition->>'name' || ' - ESM v' || p_version::TEXT, 
        v_entity_schema, 
        v_entity_type,
        v_esm_definitions_json, 
        p_version, 
        true, 
        p_blueprint_id, 
        v_metadata
    )
    RETURNING id INTO v_esm_def_id;
    RETURN v_esm_def_id;
END;$$;

--                         -- Create Action using utility function

--                             v_final_action_config,

--                             p_blueprint_id,
--                             v_metadata,
--                             COALESCE(v_action->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
--                             v_action_conditions
--                         );
--                         v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                        
--                     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--                         RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create action "%" for stage %: %', 
--                             v_action->>'name', v_stage_name, SQLERRM;
--                     END;
--                 END LOOP;
--             END LOOP;
--         END IF;
        
--     END IF; -- End of lifecycle/approval block

--     -- ** C. PROCESS ON_RELATED_EVENT AUTOMATIONS (ALL TYPES) **
--     IF v_automations IS NOT NULL AND v_automations->'on_related_event' IS NOT NULL THEN
--         v_event_automations := v_automations->'on_related_event';
--         FOR v_event_name, v_event_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(v_event_automations) LOOP
--             v_actions := v_event_config->'actions';

--             v_trigger_schema := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_schema'; 
--             v_trigger_table  := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_type'; 
--             v_trigger_type   := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'trigger_type';
            
--             -- Validate listen_to structure
--             IF v_trigger_schema IS NULL OR v_trigger_table IS NULL OR v_trigger_type IS NULL THEN 
--                 RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid "listen_to" structure for on_related_event "%".', v_event_name; 
--             END IF;
            
--             v_conditions := v_event_config->'conditions';
--             IF v_conditions IS NULL OR v_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR v_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN 
--                 v_conditions := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb;
--             ELSIF jsonb_typeof(v_conditions) != 'object' OR v_conditions->>'type' IS NULL THEN 
--                 RAISE EXCEPTION 'Condition for on_related_event "%" must be a valid JSONB object.', v_event_name; 
--             END IF;
            
--             -- Build standardized metadata
--             v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
--                 p_blueprint_id, 
--                 p_version, 
--                 p_esm_def_id,
--                 'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
--                 jsonb_build_object(
--                     'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
--                     'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type
--                 )
--             );
            
--             -- Create Rule using utility function
--             v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
--                 p_organization_id, 
--                 v_rule_name, 
--                 'Auto-generated from blueprint for related event', 
--                 v_trigger_schema, 
--                 v_trigger_table, 
--                 v_trigger_type, 
--                 v_conditions, 
--                 p_version, 
--                 10, -- priority
--                 p_blueprint_id, 
--                 v_metadata
--             );
--             v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            
--             -- Create Actions for Related Event with optimized utility functions
--             FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
--                 v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_action->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
--                 BEGIN
--                     -- Use utility function for condition extraction
--                     v_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_action);
                    
--                     -- Use utility function for template variable merging
--                     v_final_action_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
--                         COALESCE(v_action->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
--                         v_blueprint_name,
--                         p_version
--                     );
                    
--                     -- Build action metadata with condition source tracking
--                     v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
--                         p_blueprint_id, 
--                         p_version, 
--                         p_esm_def_id,
--                         'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
--                         jsonb_build_object(
--                             'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
--                             'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,
--                             'blueprint_action_name', v_action->>'name', 
--                             'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
--                             'condition_source', CASE 
--                                 WHEN v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
--                                 WHEN v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
--                                 WHEN v_action->'applies_to_categories' IS NOT NULL THEN 'category_conversion'
--                                 ELSE 'none'

--                     -- Create Action using utility function
--                     PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
--                         v_rule_id,

--                         v_action_priority,

--                         p_blueprint_id,
--                         v_metadata,

--                         v_action_conditions

--                     v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    
--                 EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN

--                         v_action->>'name', v_event_name, SQLERRM;
--                 END;
--             END LOOP;
--         END LOOP;
--     END IF;
    
--     RAISE NOTICE 'Created % rules and % actions for blueprint % (type: %)', v_rule_count, v_action_count, p_blueprint_id, v_blueprint_type;
--     RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count);
-- END;

-- ABOVE TRIAL FOR NON STANDARD APPROVAL

DECLARE
    v_automations JSONB; 
    v_stage_automations JSONB; 
    v_event_automations JSONB; 
    v_stage_name TEXT; 
    v_stage_config JSONB; 
    v_actions JSONB; 
    v_action JSONB; 
    v_rule_id UUID;
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema'; 
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type'; 
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_trigger_schema TEXT; 
    v_trigger_table TEXT; 
    v_trigger_type TEXT; 
    v_rule_name TEXT;
    v_conditions JSONB; 
    v_condition_type TEXT; 
    v_rule_count INT := 0; 
    v_action_count INT := 0; 
    v_event_name TEXT; 
    v_event_config JSONB; 
    v_action_priority INT;
    v_metadata JSONB;
    v_action_conditions JSONB;
    v_final_action_config JSONB;
    -- MINIMAL FIX: Add blueprint_type for metadata only
    v_blueprint_type TEXT := COALESCE(p_definition->>'blueprint_type', 'lifecycle');
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Compiles rules and actions from blueprint automations with heavy optimization
    -- 2. Uses utility functions for condition extraction, template merging, and metadata creation
    -- 3. Eliminates 100+ lines of duplicated code from original implementation
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile
    -- 5. MINIMAL FIX: Works for both lifecycle and approval blueprints
    --    - If approval blueprint has automations, they'll be processed
    --    - If approval blueprint has no automations, returns early (rules_created: 0, actions_created: 0)
    
    v_automations := p_definition->'automations';
    IF v_automations IS NULL THEN 
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', 0, 'actions_created', 0); 
    END IF;
    
    -- Process Stage Entry Automations (works for both lifecycle and approval)
    v_stage_automations := v_automations->'on_stage_entry';
    IF v_stage_automations IS NOT NULL THEN
        FOR v_stage_name, v_stage_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(v_stage_automations) LOOP
            v_actions := v_stage_config->'actions'; 
            v_conditions := v_stage_config->'conditions';
            v_rule_name := 'Blueprint: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - Stage ' || v_stage_name || ' Entry';
            v_trigger_type := format('%s.%s.stage.%s.entered', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage_name);
            
            -- Enhanced condition handling using standardized approach
            IF v_conditions IS NULL OR v_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR v_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN 
                v_conditions := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb; 
            END IF;
            
            -- Build standardized metadata (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
            v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                p_blueprint_id, 
                p_version, 
                p_esm_def_id,
                'on_stage_entry.' || v_stage_name,
                jsonb_build_object(
                    'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                    'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                )
            );
            
            -- Create Rule using utility function
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                'Auto-generated from blueprint for stage entry', 
                v_entity_schema, 
                v_entity_type, 
                v_trigger_type, 
                v_conditions, 
                p_version, 
                10, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            
            -- Create Actions for Stage Entry with optimized utility functions
            FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_action->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_action);
                    
                    -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                    v_final_action_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                        COALESCE(v_action->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                        v_blueprint_name,
                        p_version
                    );
                    
                    -- Build action metadata with condition source tracking (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        'on_stage_entry.' || v_stage_name,
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                            'blueprint_action_name', v_action->>'name', 
                            'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                WHEN v_action->'applies_to_categories' IS NOT NULL THEN 'category_conversion'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create Action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'Unnamed Action'), 
                        v_action->>'action_type', 
                        v_final_action_config,
                        v_action_priority, 
                        COALESCE((v_action->>'run_asynchronous')::BOOLEAN, false),
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        COALESCE(v_action->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                        v_action_conditions
                    );
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create action "%" for stage %: %', 
                        v_action->>'name', v_stage_name, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END LOOP;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    -- Process Related Event Automations with same optimized approach (works for all types)
    v_event_automations := v_automations->'on_related_event';
    IF v_event_automations IS NOT NULL THEN
        FOR v_event_name, v_event_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(v_event_automations) LOOP
            v_actions := v_event_config->'actions'; 
            v_rule_name := 'Blueprint: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - Event: ' || v_event_name;
            v_trigger_schema := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_schema'; 
            v_trigger_table  := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_type'; 
            v_trigger_type   := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'trigger_type';
            
            -- Validate listen_to structure
            IF v_trigger_schema IS NULL OR v_trigger_table IS NULL OR v_trigger_type IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid "listen_to" structure for on_related_event "%".', v_event_name; 
            END IF;
            
            v_conditions := v_event_config->'conditions';
            IF v_conditions IS NULL OR v_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR v_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN 
                v_conditions := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb;
            ELSIF jsonb_typeof(v_conditions) != 'object' OR v_conditions->>'type' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Condition for on_related_event "%" must be a valid JSONB object.', v_event_name; 
            END IF;
            
            -- Build standardized metadata (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
            v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                p_blueprint_id, 
                p_version, 
                p_esm_def_id,
                'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
                jsonb_build_object(
                    'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                    'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                )
            );
            
            -- Create Rule using utility function
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                'Auto-generated from blueprint for related event', 
                v_trigger_schema, 
                v_trigger_table, 
                v_trigger_type, 
                v_conditions, 
                p_version, 
                10, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            
            -- Create Actions for Related Event with optimized utility functions
            FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_action->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_action);
                    
                    -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                    v_final_action_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                        COALESCE(v_action->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                        v_blueprint_name,
                        p_version
                    );
                    
                    -- Build action metadata with condition source tracking (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                            'blueprint_action_name', v_action->>'name', 
                            'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                WHEN v_action->'applies_to_categories' IS NOT NULL THEN 'category_conversion'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create Action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'Unnamed Action'), 
                        v_action->>'action_type', 
                        v_final_action_config,
                        v_action_priority, 
                        COALESCE((v_action->>'run_asynchronous')::BOOLEAN, false),
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        COALESCE(v_action->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                        v_action_conditions
                    );
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create action "%" for event %: %', 
                        v_action->>'name', v_event_name, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END LOOP;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Created % rules and % actions for blueprint % (type: %)', 
        v_rule_count, v_action_count, p_blueprint_id, v_blueprint_type;
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count);
END;$$;

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_definition jsonb, p_version integer, p_esm_def_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- -- ***** WORKED B4 BP TYPES - tested dec 9 ******** --

--         IF v_monitored_stages IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_monitored_stages) = 0 THEN

--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create rule for SLA %: %', v_sla_rule_id, SQLERRM;
--             CONTINUE;
--         END;

--         -- Create EMAIL action with optimized utility functions
--         IF v_sla_rule->'action' IS NOT NULL AND v_sla_rule->'action' != 'null'::jsonb THEN
--             BEGIN
--                 v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_sla_rule->'action'->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
--                 -- Use utility function for template variable merging
--                 v_email_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
--                     COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
--                     v_blueprint_name,

--                 );
                
--                 -- Add SLA-specific template variables
--                 v_email_config := jsonb_set(
--                     v_email_config,
--                     '{template_variables}',
--                     COALESCE(v_email_config->'template_variables', '{}'::jsonb) || 
--                     jsonb_build_object(
--                         'entity_id', '{{new.display_id}}',
--                         'entity_type', v_entity_type,
--                         'escalation_level', v_escalation_level,
--                         'sla_rule_name', v_sla_rule_id,
--                         'time_threshold', v_time_threshold_hours || ' hours'
--                     )
--                 );
                
--                 -- Use utility function for condition extraction
--                 v_sla_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_sla_rule->'action');
                
--                 -- Build action metadata
--                 v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
--                     p_blueprint_id, 
--                     p_version, 
--                     p_esm_def_id,
--                     NULL, -- no specific context
--                     jsonb_build_object(
--                         'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
--                         'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
--                         'blueprint_action_name', v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name',
--                         'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
--                         'condition_source', CASE 
--                             WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
--                             WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
