--                         END
--                     )
--                 );
                
--                 -- Create action using utility function
--                 PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
--                     v_rule_id, 
--                     p_organization_id, 
--                     COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name', 'SLA Action for ' || v_sla_rule_id),
--                     v_sla_rule->'action'->>'type', 
--                     v_email_config, 
--                     v_action_priority, 
--                     false, -- run_asynchronous
--                     p_blueprint_id,
--                     v_metadata,
--                     COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
--                     v_sla_action_conditions
--                 );
                
--                 v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
--                 RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created SLA email action with conditions: %', 
--                     CASE WHEN v_sla_action_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
--             EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--                 RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create email action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
--             END;
--         END IF;

--         -- Create ON_BREACH update_entity action with optimized utility functions
--         IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config != 'null'::jsonb THEN
--             IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
--                 BEGIN
--                     -- Use utility function for condition extraction
--                     v_on_breach_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_on_breach_config->'update_entity');
                    
--                     -- Build action metadata
--                     v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
--                         p_blueprint_id, 
--                         p_version, 
--                         p_esm_def_id,
--                         NULL, -- no specific context
--                         jsonb_build_object(
--                             'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
--                             'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
--                             'is_on_breach_action', true,
--                             'condition_source', CASE 
--                                 WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
--                                 WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
--                                 ELSE 'none'
--                             END
--                         )
--                     );
                    
--                     -- Create action using utility function
--                     PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
--                         v_rule_id, 
--                         p_organization_id, 
--                         'Update Entity for SLA: ' || v_sla_rule_id,
--                         'update_entity', 
--                         jsonb_build_object(
--                             'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
--                             'entity_name', v_entity_type,
--                             'payload', jsonb_build_object(
--                                 (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->>'field'), 
--                                 (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'value')
--                             )
--                         ),
--                         COALESCE(v_action_priority, 0) + 1,
--                         false, -- run_asynchronous
--                         p_blueprint_id,
--                         v_metadata,
--                         '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb,
--                         v_on_breach_conditions
--                     );
                    
--                     v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
--                     RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created on_breach action with conditions: %', 
--                         CASE WHEN v_on_breach_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
--                 EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--                     RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create on_breach action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
--                 END;
--             END IF;
--         END IF;

--         -- Create scheduled job
--         BEGIN
--             INSERT INTO automation.wf_scheduled_jobs (
--                 organization_id, rule_id, job_type, cron_expression, 
--                 next_run, status, blueprint_id, metadata,
--                 is_active
--             )
--             VALUES (
--                 p_organization_id, v_rule_id, 'SLA_MONITOR', 
--                 COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'check_frequency', '0 */6 * * *'),
--                 NOW(), 'pending', p_blueprint_id, 
--                 automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
--                     p_blueprint_id, 
--                     p_version, 
--                     p_esm_def_id,
--                     NULL, -- no specific context
--                     jsonb_build_object(
--                         'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
--                         'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id
--                     )
--                 ),
--                 true
--             );
            
--             v_job_count := v_job_count + 1;
--             RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created scheduled job for rule %', v_rule_id;
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create scheduled job for rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
--         END;

--     END LOOP;

--     RAISE NOTICE '=== Completed comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs ===';
--     RAISE NOTICE 'Rules: %, Actions: %, Jobs: %', v_rule_count, v_action_count, v_job_count;
    
--     RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count, 'jobs_created', v_job_count);
-- END;

DECLARE
    v_sla_rules JSONB;
    v_sla_rule JSONB;
    v_rule_id UUID;
    v_rule_name TEXT;
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema';
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type';
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_monitored_stages JSONB;
    v_time_threshold_hours TEXT;
    v_conditions_jsonb JSONB;
    v_rule_conditions_payload JSONB;
    v_rule_count INT := 0;
    v_action_count INT := 0;
    v_job_count INT := 0;
    v_action_priority INT;
    v_sla_rule_id TEXT;
    v_escalation_level INT;
    v_on_breach_config JSONB;
    v_email_config JSONB;
    v_metadata JSONB;
    v_sla_action_conditions JSONB;
    v_on_breach_conditions JSONB;
    -- MINIMAL FIX: Add blueprint_type for metadata
    v_blueprint_type TEXT := COALESCE(p_definition->>'blueprint_type', 'lifecycle');
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Compiles SLA rules, actions, and scheduled jobs with heavy optimization
    -- 2. Uses utility functions for condition extraction, template merging, and metadata creation
    -- 3. Eliminates 80+ lines of duplicated code from original implementation
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile
    -- 5. MINIMAL FIX: Works for both lifecycle and approval blueprints with SLA rules
    
    RAISE NOTICE '=== Starting comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs ===';
    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint: % (type: %), Version: %, Organization: %', 
        p_blueprint_id, v_blueprint_type, p_version, p_organization_id;
    
    v_sla_rules := p_definition->'sla_rules';
    
    IF v_sla_rules IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_sla_rules) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(v_sla_rules) = 0 THEN
        RAISE NOTICE 'No SLA rules to process, returning early';
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', 0, 'actions_created', 0, 'jobs_created', 0);
    END IF;

    FOR v_sla_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_sla_rules) 
    LOOP
        v_sla_rule_id := v_sla_rule->>'id';
        v_rule_name := 'Blueprint SLA: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - ' || v_sla_rule_id;
        v_monitored_stages := v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages';
        v_time_threshold_hours := COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'time_threshold_hours', '0');
        v_conditions_jsonb := v_sla_rule->'conditions';
        
        RAISE NOTICE 'Processing SLA rule: % (type: %)', v_sla_rule_id, v_blueprint_type;

        -- Validate monitored_stages
        IF v_monitored_stages IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_monitored_stages) = 0 THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Skipping SLA rule %: `monitored_stages` is missing or empty.', v_sla_rule_id;
            CONTINUE;
        END IF;
        
        -- Handle empty conditions
        IF v_conditions_jsonb IS NULL OR v_conditions_jsonb = 'null'::jsonb THEN
            v_conditions_jsonb := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb;
        END IF;

        -- Build rule conditions payload
        v_rule_conditions_payload := jsonb_build_object(
            'monitored_stages', v_monitored_stages,
            'time_threshold_hours', v_time_threshold_hours,
            'base_conditions', v_conditions_jsonb,
            'sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id
        );

        -- Calculate escalation level from on_breach configuration
        v_escalation_level := 1;
        v_on_breach_config := v_sla_rule->'on_breach';
        IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_escalation_level := COALESCE(
                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->>'value')::INT,
                1
            );
        END IF;

        -- Build standardized metadata (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
        v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            p_version, 
            p_esm_def_id,
            NULL, -- no specific context
            jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                'target_escalation_level', v_escalation_level
            )
        );

        -- Create the rule using utility function
        BEGIN
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'description', 'SLA rule for ' || v_sla_rule_id),
                v_entity_schema, 
                v_entity_type,
                'scheduled', 
                v_rule_conditions_payload,
                p_version, 
                20, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created rule: % (ID: %)', v_rule_name, v_rule_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create rule for SLA %: %', v_sla_rule_id, SQLERRM;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        -- Create EMAIL action with optimized utility functions
        IF v_sla_rule->'action' IS NOT NULL AND v_sla_rule->'action' != 'null'::jsonb THEN
            BEGIN
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_sla_rule->'action'->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                v_email_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                    v_blueprint_name,
                    p_version
                );
                
                -- Add SLA-specific template variables
                v_email_config := jsonb_set(
                    v_email_config,
                    '{template_variables}',
                    COALESCE(v_email_config->'template_variables', '{}'::jsonb) || 
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'entity_id', '{{new.display_id}}',
                        'entity_type', v_entity_type,
                        'escalation_level', v_escalation_level,
                        'sla_rule_name', v_sla_rule_id,
                        'time_threshold', v_time_threshold_hours || ' hours'
                    )
                );
                
                -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                v_sla_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_sla_rule->'action');
                
                -- Build action metadata (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
                v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                    p_blueprint_id, 
                    p_version, 
                    p_esm_def_id,
                    NULL, -- no specific context
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                        'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                        'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                        'blueprint_action_name', v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name',
                        'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                        'condition_source', CASE 
                            WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                            WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                            ELSE 'none'
                        END
                    )
                );
                
                -- Create action using utility function
                PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                    v_rule_id, 
                    p_organization_id, 
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name', 'SLA Action for ' || v_sla_rule_id),
                    v_sla_rule->'action'->>'type', 
                    v_email_config, 
                    v_action_priority, 
                    false, -- run_asynchronous
                    p_blueprint_id,
                    v_metadata,
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                    v_sla_action_conditions
                );
                
                v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created SLA email action with conditions: %', 
                    CASE WHEN v_sla_action_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
            EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create email action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
            END;
        END IF;

        -- Create ON_BREACH update_entity action with optimized utility functions
        IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config != 'null'::jsonb THEN
            IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_on_breach_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_on_breach_config->'update_entity');
                    
                    -- Build action metadata (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type)
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        NULL, -- no specific context
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                            'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                            'is_on_breach_action', true,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        'Update Entity for SLA: ' || v_sla_rule_id,
                        'update_entity', 
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
                            'entity_name', v_entity_type,
                            'payload', jsonb_build_object(
                                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->>'field'), 
                                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'value')
                            )
                        ),
                        COALESCE(v_action_priority, 0) + 1,
                        false, -- run_asynchronous
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb,
                        v_on_breach_conditions
                    );
                    
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created on_breach action with conditions: %', 
                        CASE WHEN v_on_breach_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create on_breach action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        -- Create scheduled job (MINIMAL FIX: add blueprint_type to job metadata)
        BEGIN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_scheduled_jobs (
                organization_id, rule_id, job_type, cron_expression, 
                next_run, status, blueprint_id, metadata,
                is_active
            )
            VALUES (
                p_organization_id, v_rule_id, 'SLA_MONITOR', 
                COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'check_frequency', '0 */6 * * *'),
                NOW(), 'pending', p_blueprint_id, 
                automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                    p_blueprint_id, 
                    p_version, 
                    p_esm_def_id,
                    NULL, -- no specific context
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                        'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,  -- MINIMAL FIX: Added
                        'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id
                    )
                ),
                true
            );
            
            v_job_count := v_job_count + 1;
            RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created scheduled job for rule %', v_rule_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create scheduled job for rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
        END;

    END LOOP;

    RAISE NOTICE '=== Completed comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs ===';
    RAISE NOTICE 'Rules: %, Actions: %, Jobs: % (type: %)', 
        v_rule_count, v_action_count, v_job_count, v_blueprint_type;
    
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count, 'jobs_created', v_job_count);
END;$$;


--
-- Name: comp_core_activate(uuid, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_core_activate(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_version integer) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_org_id UUID;
    v_entity_schema TEXT;
    v_entity_type TEXT;
    v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity UUID[];
    v_activation_count INT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Enhanced activation logic with comprehensive artifact management
    -- 2. Implements sunset strategy for smooth transitions between versions
    -- 3. Maintains in-flight entity processing while activating new version
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile_and_activate
    
    -- Step 1: Find the key characteristics (org, schema, type)
    SELECT organization_id, entity_schema, entity_type
    INTO v_org_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id AND version = p_version;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint version to activate (ID: %, Version: %) not found.', p_blueprint_id, p_version;
    END IF;
    
    -- Step 2: Get all blueprint IDs (all versions) for this entity type
    SELECT array_agg(id)
    INTO v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE organization_id = v_org_id
      AND entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND entity_type = v_entity_type;
      
    IF v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity IS NULL THEN
        v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity := ARRAY[]::UUID[];
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE '[Sunset Activate] Deactivating all old blueprints and ESM definitions for entity %.%', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;

    -- Step 3: Deactivate ALL old blueprints AND esm_definitions for this entity type
    -- This ensures esm_orchestration_before_insert only finds the new active one.
    UPDATE automation.bp_process_blueprints
    SET is_active = false, updated_at = now()
    WHERE id = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity);

    UPDATE automation.esm_definitions
    SET is_active = false, updated_at = now()
    WHERE blueprint_id = ANY(v_all_blueprint_ids_for_entity);

    -- *** SUNSET LOGIC ***
    -- We deliberately DO NOT touch wf_rules, wf_actions, or wf_scheduled_jobs.
    -- Their `is_active` status will be managed separately.
    -- Old rules/jobs remain active to process in-flight entities.

    -- Step 4: Activate the TARGET blueprint version
    UPDATE automation.bp_process_blueprints
    SET is_active = true, updated_at = now()
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id AND version = p_version;

    -- Step 5: Activate the compiled artifacts for ONLY the TARGET version
    RAISE NOTICE '[Sunset Activate] Activating target artifacts for blueprint %, version %', p_blueprint_id, p_version;
    
    -- Activate ESM definitions for target version
    UPDATE automation.esm_definitions
    SET is_active = true, updated_at = now()
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;

    -- Ensure all rules/actions/jobs for the NEW version are active/enabled
    UPDATE automation.wf_rules
    SET is_active = true, updated_at = now()
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;

    UPDATE automation.wf_actions
    SET is_enabled = true, updated_at = now()
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;
    
    UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
    SET is_active = true, status = 'pending', updated_at = now()
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;

    -- Get activation counts for reporting
    GET DIAGNOSTICS v_activation_count = ROW_COUNT;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Activated blueprint version (ID: %, Version: %) and deactivated other blueprint/ESM definitions for entity type %.%', 
        p_blueprint_id, p_version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;
        
    RAISE NOTICE 'Activation summary: % rules, % actions, % jobs, % ESM definitions activated',
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM automation.wf_rules WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version AND is_active = true),
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM automation.wf_actions WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version AND is_enabled = true),
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version AND is_active = true),
        (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM automation.esm_definitions WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version AND is_active = true);

END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_core_compile(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_core_compile(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_blueprint RECORD; 
    v_is_valid BOOLEAN; 
    v_esm_def_id UUID; 
    v_compilation_id UUID := gen_random_uuid();
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp(); 
    v_rules_actions_result JSONB; 
    v_scheduled_jobs_result JSONB;
    v_final_details JSONB; 
    v_compiled_by UUID;
    v_cleanup_result JSONB;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT;
    v_definition JSONB;
BEGIN
    SELECT 
        bp.*,
        COALESCE(bp.blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_fixed
    INTO STRICT v_blueprint 
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
    WHERE bp.id = p_blueprint_id;
    v_definition := automation.bp_resolve_modular_definition(v_blueprint.definition);
    v_blueprint_type := v_blueprint.blueprint_type_fixed; 

    -- V4.7 Hybrid Normalization: Support legacy top-level stages/transitions (Backward Compatibility)
    IF v_definition ? 'stages' AND NOT (v_definition ? 'lifecycle') THEN
        v_definition := jsonb_set(
            v_definition, 
            '{lifecycle}', 
            jsonb_build_object(
                'stages', v_definition->'stages',
                'transitions', COALESCE(v_definition->'transitions', '[]'::jsonb),
                'start_stage', COALESCE(v_definition->>'start_stage', v_definition->>'startStateId')
            )
        );
        RAISE NOTICE 'V4.7 Hybrid Warning: Applied Auto-Normalization for Blueprint %. Moving stages/transitions to lifecycle block.', p_blueprint_id;
    ELSIF v_definition ? 'lifecycle' THEN
        -- Internal Lifecycle consistency (startStateId -> start_stage)
        IF NOT (v_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'start_stage') AND (v_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'startStateId') THEN
            v_definition := jsonb_set(
                v_definition,
                '{lifecycle,start_stage}',
                v_definition->'lifecycle'->'startStateId'
            );
            RAISE NOTICE 'V4.7 Hybrid Warning: Applied Internal Normalization for Blueprint %. Mapping startStateId to start_stage.', p_blueprint_id;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Starting structural validation (type: %)...', v_blueprint_type;
    PERFORM automation.comp_validate_structure(v_definition);
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleaning up artifacts for Blueprint ID %, Version % (type: %)...', 
        p_blueprint_id, v_blueprint.version, v_blueprint_type;
    v_cleanup_result := automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(
        p_blueprint_id, 
        v_blueprint.version, 
        ARRAY['jobs', 'actions', 'rules', 'esm']
    );
    BEGIN
        v_is_valid := automation.comp_validate_definition(v_definition);
        IF v_blueprint_type IN ('lifecycle', 'approval') THEN
             v_esm_def_id := automation.comp_artifacts_esm(
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_definition,
                v_blueprint.version, 
                v_blueprint.organization_id
            );
        END IF;
        v_rules_actions_result := automation.comp_artifacts_rules_actions(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_definition,
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_esm_def_id, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id
        );
        v_scheduled_jobs_result := automation.comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_definition,
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_esm_def_id, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id
        );
        v_final_details := jsonb_build_object(
            'message', 'Blueprint compiled successfully (Modular Resolution Applied).',
            'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,
            'artifacts', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_definition_id', v_esm_def_id, 
                'rules_created', v_rules_actions_result->'rules_created',
                'actions_created', v_rules_actions_result->'actions_created', 
                'jobs_created', v_scheduled_jobs_result->'jobs_created'
            )
        );
        PERFORM automation.comp_utils_log(
            v_compilation_id, 
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id, 
            'success', 
            v_final_details, 
            v_start_time, 
            clock_timestamp(), 
            v_compiled_by
        );
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'COMPILATION ERROR: %', SQLERRM;
    END;
    RETURN v_compilation_id;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: comp_core_compile_and_activate(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_core_compile_and_activate(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- -- -- ***** WORKED B4 BP TYPES - tested dec 9 ******** --
-- DECLARE
--     v_compilation_id UUID;
--     v_version INT;
--     v_blueprint_name TEXT;
-- BEGIN
--     -- DEV NOTES:
--     -- 1. Main entry point for blueprint compilation and activation
--     -- 2. Orchestrates the complete compilation-to-activation pipeline
--     -- 3. Enhanced error handling and validation throughout the process
--     -- 4. Called by: External applications and APIs
    
--     -- Step 0: Validate blueprint exists and is valid
--     IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id) THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint with ID % not found.', p_blueprint_id;
--     END IF;

--     -- Retrieve blueprint details for logging
--     SELECT version, definition->>'name'
--     INTO v_version, v_blueprint_name
--     FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
--     WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;

--     RAISE NOTICE 'Starting compilation and activation for blueprint: % (ID: %, Version: %)', 
--         v_blueprint_name, p_blueprint_id, v_version;

--     -- Step 1: Enhanced cleanup using utility function
--     PERFORM automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

--     -- Step 2: Compile with enhanced validation and optimization
--     v_compilation_id := automation.comp_core_compile(p_blueprint_id);

--     -- Step 3: Activate with improved conflict handling
--     PERFORM automation.comp_core_activate(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

--     RAISE NOTICE 'Successfully compiled and activated blueprint: % (Compilation ID: %)', 
--         v_blueprint_name, v_compilation_id;

--     -- Return compilation ID for tracking
--     RETURN v_compilation_id;
    
-- EXCEPTION
--     WHEN OTHERS THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint compilation and activation failed for ID %: %', p_blueprint_id, SQLERRM;
-- END;

-- -- DECLARE
-- --     v_compilation_id UUID;
-- --     v_version INT;
-- --     v_blueprint_name TEXT;
-- --     v_blueprint_type TEXT;
-- -- BEGIN
-- --     -- Step 0: Validate blueprint exists and is valid
-- --     IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id) THEN
-- --         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint with ID % not found.', p_blueprint_id;
-- --     END IF;

-- --     -- Retrieve blueprint details for logging
-- --     SELECT version, definition->>'name', definition->>'blueprint_type'
-- --     INTO v_version, v_blueprint_name, v_blueprint_type
-- --     FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
-- --     WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;

-- --     RAISE NOTICE 'Starting compilation and activation for blueprint: % (ID: %, Version: %, Type: %)', 
-- --         v_blueprint_name, p_blueprint_id, v_version, v_blueprint_type;

-- --     -- Step 1: Enhanced cleanup using utility function
-- --     PERFORM automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

-- --     -- Step 2: Compile with enhanced validation and optimization
-- --     v_compilation_id := automation.comp_core_compile(p_blueprint_id);

-- --     -- Step 3: Activate with improved conflict handling
-- --     PERFORM automation.comp_core_activate(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

-- --     RAISE NOTICE 'Successfully compiled and activated blueprint: % (Compilation ID: %, Type: %)', 
-- --         v_blueprint_name, v_compilation_id, v_blueprint_type;

-- --     -- Return compilation ID for tracking
-- --     RETURN v_compilation_id;
    
-- -- EXCEPTION
-- --     WHEN OTHERS THEN
-- --         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint compilation and activation failed for ID %: %', p_blueprint_id, SQLERRM;
-- -- END;


DECLARE
    v_compilation_id UUID;
    v_version INT;
    v_blueprint_name TEXT;
    -- Variables to capture error details
    v_error_message TEXT;
    v_error_context TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- Step 0: Validate blueprint exists and is valid
    IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint with ID % not found.', p_blueprint_id;
    END IF;

    -- Retrieve blueprint details for logging
    SELECT version, definition->>'name'
    INTO v_version, v_blueprint_name
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Starting compilation and activation for blueprint: % (ID: %, Version: %)', 
        v_blueprint_name, p_blueprint_id, v_version;

    -- Step 1: Enhanced cleanup using utility function
    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

    -- Step 2: Compile with enhanced validation and optimization
    v_compilation_id := automation.comp_core_compile(p_blueprint_id);

    -- Step 3: Activate with improved conflict handling
    PERFORM automation.comp_core_activate(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

    RAISE NOTICE 'Successfully compiled and activated blueprint: % (Compilation ID: %)', 
        v_blueprint_name, v_compilation_id;

    -- Return compilation ID for tracking (Successful Path)
    RETURN v_compilation_id;
    
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        -- Capture the actual error message and context
        GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS
            v_error_message = MESSAGE_TEXT,
            v_error_context = PG_EXCEPTION_CONTEXT;

        -- Log the failure to the PostgreSQL server log
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'COMPILATION FAILED: Blueprint ID % (v%): %', p_blueprint_id, v_version, v_error_message
        USING HINT = v_error_context;

        -- We must return a value here, even though RAISE EXCEPTION technically exits.
        -- In most practical environments, the outer calling function catches the exception 
        -- and the database transaction aborts. We return NULL here to satisfy the function
        -- signature if the RAISExxx fails or to be compliant, though the RAISE is the primary goal.
        RETURN NULL; 
END;$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_build_metadata(uuid, integer, uuid, text, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_version integer, p_esm_def_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_context text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_extra_fields jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_base_metadata JSONB;
    v_final_metadata JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Standardized metadata creation for all blueprint artifacts
    -- 2. Ensures consistent field names and structure across rules, actions, jobs, ESM
    -- 3. Supports optional context and extra fields for specific use cases
    -- 4. Used by: comp_artifacts_esm, comp_artifacts_rules_actions, comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs
    
    -- Build base metadata structure
    v_base_metadata := jsonb_build_object(
        'blueprint_version', p_version,
        'blueprint_name', 'TBD' -- Will be filled by calling functions
    );
    
    -- Add ESM definition ID if provided
    IF p_esm_def_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_base_metadata := v_base_metadata || jsonb_build_object('esm_definition_id', p_esm_def_id);
    END IF;
    
    -- Add context if provided (e.g., 'on_stage_entry.stage_name', 'on_related_event.event_name')
    IF p_context IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_base_metadata := v_base_metadata || jsonb_build_object('blueprint_listener_id', p_context);
    END IF;
    
    -- Merge with any extra fields provided
    IF p_extra_fields IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_final_metadata := v_base_metadata || p_extra_fields;
    ELSE
        v_final_metadata := v_base_metadata;
    END IF;
    
    RETURN v_final_metadata;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_clean_artifacts(uuid, integer, text[]); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_version integer, p_artifact_types text[] DEFAULT NULL::text[]) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_artifact_types TEXT[];
    v_deleted_counts JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_count INT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Centralized cleanup for blueprint artifacts with dependency order awareness
    -- 2. Supports selective cleanup by artifact type or full cleanup
    -- 3. Returns deletion counts for monitoring and debugging
    -- 4. Used by: comp_core_compile, comp_utils_cleanup (if needed)
    
    -- Default to cleaning all artifact types if none specified
    v_artifact_types := COALESCE(p_artifact_types, ARRAY['jobs', 'actions', 'rules', 'esm']);
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleaning artifacts for Blueprint %, Version %. Types: %', 
        p_blueprint_id, p_version, v_artifact_types;
    
    -- Clean in dependency order to avoid FK constraints
    
    -- 1. Scheduled Jobs (depend on rules)
    IF 'jobs' = ANY(v_artifact_types) THEN
        DELETE FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs 
        WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
          AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_count = ROW_COUNT;
        v_deleted_counts := v_deleted_counts || jsonb_build_object('jobs', v_count);
        RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % scheduled jobs', v_count;
    END IF;
    
    -- 2. Actions (depend on rules)  
    IF 'actions' = ANY(v_artifact_types) THEN
        DELETE FROM automation.wf_actions 
        WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
          AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_count = ROW_COUNT;
        v_deleted_counts := v_deleted_counts || jsonb_build_object('actions', v_count);
        RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % actions', v_count;
    END IF;
    
    -- 3. Rules (depend on nothing)
    IF 'rules' = ANY(v_artifact_types) THEN
        DELETE FROM automation.wf_rules 
        WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
          AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_count = ROW_COUNT;
        v_deleted_counts := v_deleted_counts || jsonb_build_object('rules', v_count);
        RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % rules', v_count;
    END IF;
    
    -- 4. ESM Definitions (depend on nothing)
    IF 'esm' = ANY(v_artifact_types) THEN
        DELETE FROM automation.esm_definitions 
        WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
          AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = p_version;
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_count = ROW_COUNT;
        v_deleted_counts := v_deleted_counts || jsonb_build_object('esm_definitions', v_count);
        RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % ESM definitions', v_count;
    END IF;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleanup completed for Blueprint %', p_blueprint_id;
    
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('deleted_counts', v_deleted_counts);
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_create_action(uuid, uuid, text, text, jsonb, integer, boolean, uuid, jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_create_action(p_rule_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid, p_action_name text, p_action_type text, p_configuration jsonb, p_action_order integer DEFAULT 0, p_run_asynchronous boolean DEFAULT false, p_blueprint_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_metadata jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb, p_retry_policy jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb, p_conditions jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_action_id UUID;
    v_condition_type TEXT;
    v_final_retry_policy JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Standardized action creation with consistent field handling
    -- 2. Automatically determines condition_type based on conditions content  
    -- 3. Provides default retry policy if none specified
    -- 4. Used by: comp_artifacts_rules_actions, comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs
    
    -- Determine condition type
    IF p_conditions IS NULL OR p_conditions = 'null'::jsonb THEN
        v_condition_type := NULL;
    ELSE
        v_condition_type := 'jsonb';
    END IF;
    
    -- Set default retry policy if not provided
    v_final_retry_policy := COALESCE(p_retry_policy, '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb);
    
    -- Create the action
    INSERT INTO automation.wf_actions (
        rule_id, organization_id, name, action_type, configuration,
        action_order, is_enabled, run_asynchronous, blueprint_id,
        metadata, retry_policy, conditions, condition_type
    )
    VALUES (
        p_rule_id,
        p_organization_id,
        p_action_name,
        p_action_type,
        p_configuration,
        p_action_order,
        true,
        p_run_asynchronous,
        p_blueprint_id,
        p_metadata,
        v_final_retry_policy,
        p_conditions,
        v_condition_type
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_action_id;
    
    RETURN v_action_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_create_rule(uuid, text, text, text, text, text, jsonb, integer, integer, uuid, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_create_rule(p_organization_id uuid, p_rule_name text, p_description text, p_trigger_schema text, p_trigger_table text, p_trigger_type text, p_conditions jsonb, p_version integer, p_priority integer DEFAULT 10, p_blueprint_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_metadata jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_rule_id UUID;
    v_condition_type TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Standardized rule creation with consistent field handling
    -- 2. Automatically determines condition_type based on conditions content
    -- 3. Handles metadata merging and blueprint association
    -- 4. Used by: comp_artifacts_rules_actions, comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs
    
    -- Determine condition type
    IF p_conditions IS NULL OR p_conditions = '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb THEN
        v_condition_type := NULL;
    ELSE
        v_condition_type := 'jsonb';
    END IF;
    
    -- Create the rule
    INSERT INTO automation.wf_rules (
        organization_id, name, description, trigger_schema, trigger_table,
        trigger_type, condition_type, conditions, version, is_active,
        priority, blueprint_id, metadata
    )
    VALUES (
        p_organization_id,
        p_rule_name,
        p_description,
        p_trigger_schema,
        p_trigger_table,
        p_trigger_type,
        v_condition_type,
        p_conditions,
        p_version,
        true,
        p_priority,
        p_blueprint_id,
        p_metadata
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_rule_id;
    
    RETURN v_rule_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_create_rule(uuid, text, text, text, text, text, jsonb, text, integer, uuid, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_create_rule(p_organization_id uuid, p_rule_name text, p_description text, p_trigger_schema text, p_trigger_table text, p_trigger_type text, p_conditions jsonb, p_version text, p_priority integer, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_metadata jsonb) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
 DECLARE
     v_rule_id UUID;
     v_condition_type TEXT;
 BEGIN
     -- Determine condition type: Always 'jsonb' if conditions are provided
     IF p_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN
         v_condition_type := 'jsonb';
     ELSE
         v_condition_type := NULL;
     END IF;

     -- Create the rule
     INSERT INTO automation.wf_rules (
         organization_id, name, description, trigger_schema, trigger_table,
         trigger_type, condition_type, conditions, version, is_active,
         priority, blueprint_id, metadata
     )
     VALUES (
         p_organization_id,
         p_rule_name,
         p_description,
         p_trigger_schema,
         p_trigger_table,
         p_trigger_type,
         v_condition_type,
         p_conditions,
         p_version,
         true,
         p_priority,
         p_blueprint_id,
         p_metadata
     ) RETURNING id INTO v_rule_id;

     RETURN v_rule_id;
 END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_extract_conditions(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(p_action_jsonb jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_extracted_conditions JSONB;
    v_applies_to_categories JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Unified condition extraction from multiple possible locations in action JSON
    -- 2. Handles both top-level and nested config conditions
    -- 3. Converts applies_to_categories to condition format when no explicit conditions
    -- 4. Used by: comp_artifacts_rules_actions, comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs
    
    -- Step 1: Extract from primary locations (top-level takes precedence over nested)
    v_extracted_conditions := COALESCE(
        p_action_jsonb->'conditions',
        p_action_jsonb->'config'->'conditions'
    );
    
    -- Step 2: If no explicit conditions, check for applies_to_categories conversion
    IF v_extracted_conditions IS NULL OR v_extracted_conditions = 'null'::jsonb THEN
        v_applies_to_categories := p_action_jsonb->'applies_to_categories';
        
        IF v_applies_to_categories IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_extracted_conditions := jsonb_build_object(
                'type', 'AND',
                'rules', jsonb_build_array(
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'field', 'new.category_id',
                        'operator', 'in',
                        'value', v_applies_to_categories
                    )
                )
            );
        END IF;
    END IF;
    
    RETURN v_extracted_conditions;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_log(uuid, uuid, integer, uuid, text, jsonb, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_log(p_compilation_id uuid, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_blueprint_version integer, p_organization_id uuid, p_status text, p_details jsonb, p_started_at timestamp with time zone, p_finished_at timestamp with time zone, p_compiled_by uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Centralized compilation logging with structured details
    -- 2. Captures both success and failure scenarios comprehensively
    -- 3. Provides audit trail for compilation operations
    -- 4. Used by: comp_core_compile
    
    INSERT INTO automation.comp_blueprint_compilation_logs (
        id, blueprint_id, organization_id, blueprint_version, status,
        started_at, finished_at, error_message, artifacts_generated, compiled_by,
        metadata
    ) VALUES (
        p_compilation_id, 
        p_blueprint_id, 
        p_organization_id, 
        p_blueprint_version, 
        p_status,
        p_started_at, 
        p_finished_at,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'failed' THEN p_details->>'error' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'success' THEN p_details->'artifacts' ELSE NULL END,
        p_compiled_by,
        jsonb_build_object(
            'compilation_function', 'comp_core_compile',
            'error_context', p_details->>'context',
            'log_version', 'v3_optimized',
            'details', p_details
        )
    );
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Compilation log recorded: % for blueprint % (Status: %)', 
        p_compilation_id, p_blueprint_id, p_status;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_utils_merge_template_vars(jsonb, text, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(p_original_config jsonb, p_blueprint_name text, p_version integer) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_merged_config JSONB;
    v_existing_template_vars JSONB;
    v_blueprint_template_vars JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Centralized template variable merging for consistent behavior across all compilation functions
    -- 2. Preserves existing template variables while adding blueprint metadata
    -- 3. Only modifies template_variables if they exist in original config (non-destructive)
    -- 4. Used by: comp_artifacts_rules_actions, comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs
    
    -- Start with original config
    v_merged_config := COALESCE(p_original_config, '{}'::jsonb);
    
    -- Only proceed if template_variables exist in original config
    IF v_merged_config ? 'template_variables' THEN
        -- Extract existing template variables
        v_existing_template_vars := COALESCE(v_merged_config->'template_variables', '{}'::jsonb);
        
        -- Create blueprint metadata template variables (new values don't overwrite existing)
        v_blueprint_template_vars := jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_name', p_blueprint_name,
            'blueprint_version', p_version::text
        );
        
        -- Merge existing with blueprint vars (existing take priority)
        v_merged_config := jsonb_set(
            v_merged_config,
            '{template_variables}',
            v_existing_template_vars || v_blueprint_template_vars
        );
    END IF;
    
    -- Return merged config (unchanged if no template_variables existed)
    RETURN v_merged_config;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_validate_actions(jsonb, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_validate_actions(p_actions jsonb, p_path text) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE 
    v_action JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Validates action array structure and required fields
    -- 2. Ensures all actions have name and action_type
    -- 3. Provides detailed error context for debugging
    -- 4. Used by: comp_validate_definition
    
    -- Must be a non-empty array
    IF p_actions IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_actions) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(p_actions) = 0 THEN 
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid actions block: Must be a non-empty array. Path: %', p_path; 
    END IF;
    
    -- Validate each action in the array
    FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_actions) LOOP
        -- Every action must have a name
        IF v_action->>'name' IS NULL THEN 
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid action: Action is missing "name". Path: %', p_path; 
        END IF;
        
        -- Every action must have an action_type
        IF v_action->>'action_type' IS NULL THEN 
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid action: Action "%" is missing "action_type".', v_action->>'name'; 
        END IF;
    END LOOP;
    
    RETURN TRUE;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_validate_conditions(jsonb, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_validate_conditions(p_conditions jsonb, p_path text) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql IMMUTABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_rule JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Validates condition block structure for AND/OR/NOT logic
    -- 2. Supports nested condition groups and field-based rules
    -- 3. Provides detailed error messages with context paths
    -- 4. Used by: comp_validate_definition, comp_validate_actions
    
    -- Allow empty/null conditions (valid case)
    IF p_conditions IS NULL OR p_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR p_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN
        RETURN TRUE;
    END IF;

    -- Must be a JSON object
    IF jsonb_typeof(p_conditions) != 'object' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid conditions block: Must be an object. Path: %', p_path;
    END IF;

    -- Must have valid type (AND, OR, NOT)
    IF p_conditions->>'type' IS NULL OR NOT (p_conditions->>'type' IN ('AND', 'OR', 'NOT')) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid conditions block: Missing or invalid "type" (must be AND, OR, NOT). Path: %', p_path;
    END IF;

    -- Rules must be an array
    IF jsonb_typeof(p_conditions->'rules') != 'array' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid conditions block: "rules" must be an array. Path: %', p_path;
    END IF;

    -- Validate each rule in the array
    FOR v_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_conditions->'rules')
    LOOP
        -- Each rule must have either a 'field' (for field-based) or 'type' (for nested group)
        IF v_rule->>'field' IS NULL AND v_rule->>'type' IS NULL THEN
             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid rule: Each rule must have a "field" or be a nested "type" group. Path: %', p_path || '->rules';
        END IF;
        
        -- Field-based rules must have an operator
        IF v_rule->>'field' IS NOT NULL AND v_rule->>'operator' IS NULL THEN
             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid rule: Rule with "field" is missing "operator". Path: %', p_path || '->rules';
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN TRUE;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: comp_validate_definition(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_validate_definition(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql

-- Modified basic validation to be type-specific
-- Lifecycle blueprints still require entity_schema and entity_type in JSON
-- Approval blueprints only require name in JSON
-- All other validation logic remains unchanged
DECLARE
    v_stage_key TEXT;
    v_stage_config JSONB;
    v_event_key TEXT;
    v_event_config JSONB;
    v_sla_rule JSONB;
    v_sla_conditions JSONB;
    v_sla_conditions_type TEXT;
    v_rule_index INT := 0;
    v_sla_id TEXT;
    v_action JSONB;
    v_actions JSONB;
    v_on_breach_config JSONB;
    -- MINIMAL FIX: Add blueprint_type variable for logging and conditional checks
    v_blueprint_type TEXT := COALESCE(p_definition->>'blueprint_type', 'lifecycle');
BEGIN
    -- Basic required fields validation (No change needed here; it passes for both types)
    IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name' AND p_definition ? 'lifecycle' AND p_definition ? 'entity_schema' AND p_definition ? 'entity_type') THEN 
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing required keys: name, lifecycle, entity_schema, entity_type (type: %)', v_blueprint_type; 
    END IF;

    -- Lifecycle structure validation (No change needed here; both types use this structure)
    IF NOT (p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'start_stage' AND p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'stages' 
             AND jsonb_typeof(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') = 'array' 
             AND jsonb_array_length(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') > 0) THEN 
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint lifecycle invalid: missing start_stage or stages array or stages array is empty (type: %)', v_blueprint_type; 
    END IF;

    -- Validate automations section if present (Logic remains the same as it correctly calls sub-validation functions)
    IF p_definition->'automations' IS NOT NULL THEN
        -- Validate on_stage_entry automations
        IF p_definition->'automations'->'on_stage_entry' IS NOT NULL THEN
            FOR v_stage_key, v_stage_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_definition->'automations'->'on_stage_entry') LOOP
                -- Validate stage conditions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_stage_config->'conditions', 
                    'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->conditions'
                );
                
                -- Validate stage actions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_actions(
                    v_stage_config->'actions', 
                    'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions'
                );

                -- Enhanced: Validate action-level condition structure conflicts
                v_actions := v_stage_config->'actions';
                IF v_actions IS NOT NULL THEN
                    FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                        -- Detect conflicting condition definitions
                        IF (v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL AND v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL) THEN
                            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Action "%" in on_stage_entry.% has conditions defined in both top-level and config. Choose one location.', 
                                COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed'), v_stage_key;
                        END IF;
                        
                        -- Validate condition structure if present in top-level
                        IF v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                        
                        -- Validate condition structure if present in nested config
                        IF v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'config'->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->config->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
        
        -- Validate on_related_event automations
        IF p_definition->'automations'->'on_related_event' IS NOT NULL THEN
            FOR v_event_key, v_event_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_definition->'automations'->'on_related_event') LOOP
                -- Validate listen_to structure
                IF v_event_config->'listen_to' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_schema' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_type' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'trigger_type' IS NULL THEN 
                    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid listen_to structure for event "%": missing entity_schema, entity_type, or trigger_type', v_event_key; 
                END IF;

                -- Validate event conditions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_event_config->'conditions', 
                    'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->conditions'
                );
                
                -- Validate event actions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_actions(
                    v_event_config->'actions', 
                    'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions'
                );

                -- Enhanced: Validate action-level condition structure conflicts
                v_actions := v_event_config->'actions';
                IF v_actions IS NOT NULL THEN
                    FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                        IF (v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL AND v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL) THEN
                            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Action "%" in on_related_event.% has conditions defined in both top-level and config. Choose one location.', 
                                COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed'), v_event_key;
                        END IF;
                        
                        IF v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                        
                        IF v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'config'->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->config->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- Enhanced: Validate sla_rules with action condition validation
    IF p_definition->'sla_rules' IS NOT NULL THEN
        IF jsonb_typeof(p_definition->'sla_rules') != 'array' THEN 
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'sla_rules must be an array.'; 
        END IF;
        
        v_rule_index := 0; 
        FOR v_sla_rule IN SELECT value FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'sla_rules') LOOP
            v_sla_id := COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'id', 'UNKNOWN'); 

            -- Basic SLA rule structure validation
            IF jsonb_typeof(v_sla_rule) != 'object' THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule element at index % is not a JSON object!', v_rule_index; 
            END IF;
            
            IF v_sla_rule->>'id' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->>'check_frequency' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->>'time_threshold_hours' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule at index % (ID: %) is missing required key(s): id, check_frequency, or time_threshold_hours', v_rule_index, v_sla_id; 
            END IF;

            -- Validate monitored_stages
            IF v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages' IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages') = 0 THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % has missing or empty monitored_stages array', v_sla_id, v_rule_index;
            END IF;

            -- Validate SLA conditions
            v_sla_conditions := v_sla_rule->'conditions'; 
            v_sla_conditions_type := jsonb_typeof(v_sla_conditions);
            
            IF v_sla_conditions_type != 'object' AND v_sla_conditions_type != 'null' THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % has invalid "conditions" type. Expected object/null, got %.', v_sla_id, v_rule_index, v_sla_conditions_type;
            END IF;

            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                v_sla_conditions, 
                'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->conditions'
            ); 

            -- Enhanced: Validate SLA action conditions
            IF v_sla_rule->'action' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->'action'->>'type' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % is missing "action" or "action.type"', v_sla_id, v_rule_index; 
            END IF;

            -- Validate SLA action conditions if present
            IF v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions',
                    'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->action->conditions'
                );
            END IF;

            -- Enhanced: Validate on_breach action conditions if present
            v_on_breach_config := v_sla_rule->'on_breach';
            IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config != 'null'::jsonb THEN
                IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
                    IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                        PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                            v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions',
                            'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->on_breach->update_entity->conditions'
                        );
                    END IF;
                END IF;
            END IF;

            v_rule_index := v_rule_index + 1; 
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint validation passed for: % (type: %)', p_definition->>'name', v_blueprint_type;
    RETURN TRUE;

EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint validation failed: %', SQLERRM;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: comp_validate_structure(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.comp_validate_structure(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- -- ***** WORKED B4 BP TYPES - tested dec 9 - REVERTED BACK ******** --
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Structural validation beyond JSON schema - checks business logic constraints
    -- 2. Validates stage ID uniqueness and required field presence
    -- 3. Early detection of blueprint structural issues before compilation
    -- 4. Used by: comp_core_compile
    
    -- Check for required top-level fields
    IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name' AND p_definition ? 'entity_schema' AND p_definition ? 'entity_type') THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing required fields: name, entity_schema, entity_type';
    END IF;
    
    -- Validate lifecycle structure exists
    IF p_definition->'lifecycle' IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing lifecycle definition';
    END IF;
    
    -- Check for stage ID conflicts (no duplicates)
    IF EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM (
            SELECT value->>'id' as stage_id, COUNT(*)
            FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages')
            GROUP BY value->>'id'
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
        ) duplicates
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Duplicate stage IDs found in lifecycle stages';
    END IF;
    
    -- Validate template variable usage in actions (placeholder for future enhancement)
    -- This can be expanded later for template variable validation
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint structure validation passed for: %', p_definition->>'name';
    RETURN TRUE;
    
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint structure validation failed: %', SQLERRM;
END;

-- -- UPDATE DID NOT WORK PROPERLY
-- DECLARE
--     v_blueprint_type TEXT := COALESCE(p_definition->>'blueprint_type', 'lifecycle');
-- BEGIN
--     -- DEV NOTES:
--     -- 1. Validates basic blueprint structure
--     -- 2. Called early in compilation pipeline
--     -- 3. Ensures required fields are present
--     -- 4. MINIMAL FIX: Added blueprint_type awareness
    
--     -- Basic required fields validation - TYPE SPECIFIC
--     IF v_blueprint_type = 'lifecycle' THEN
--         -- Lifecycle requires all fields
--         IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name' AND p_definition ? 'entity_schema' AND p_definition ? 'entity_type') THEN 
--             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Lifecycle blueprint missing required fields: name, entity_schema, entity_type'; 
--         END IF;
--     ELSIF v_blueprint_type = 'approval' THEN
--         -- Approval only requires name
--         IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name') THEN 
--             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Approval blueprint missing required field: name'; 
--         END IF;
--         -- Entity info should already be in definition for approval blueprints
--     ELSIF v_blueprint_type = 'orchestration' THEN
--         -- Orchestration only requires name
--         IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name') THEN 
--             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Orchestration blueprint missing required field: name'; 
--         END IF;
--     ELSE
--         -- Default validation for unknown types
--         IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name') THEN 
--             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing required field: name'; 
--         END IF;
--     END IF;
    
--     RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint structure validation passed for: % (type: %)', p_definition->>'name', v_blueprint_type;
-- END;$$;


--
-- Name: dispatch_action(uuid, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.dispatch_action(p_action_id uuid, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- This is a shim to route to the optimized dispatcher
    PERFORM automation.run_dispatch_action_optimized(p_action_id, p_trigger_data, p_context);
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: esm_orchestration_after_update(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.esm_orchestration_after_update() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_entity_schema  TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type    TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_on_exit_event  TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT;
 
    -- OTEL telemetry (3 new variables — trace_id = entity's shared UUID)
    v_trace_id   UUID := NEW.id;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp();
    v_events     JSONB := '[]'::JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- Only run if the stage_id actually changed and an instance ID exists
    IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id AND NEW.automation_esm_instance_id IS NOT NULL THEN
 
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'stage_change_detected', jsonb_build_object(
            'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
            'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
            'esm_instance_id', NEW.automation_esm_instance_id
        ));
 
        -- 1. Update the stage in the esm_instance table
        UPDATE automation.esm_instances
        SET stage_id = NEW.stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
        WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;
 
        -- 2. Find the ESM definition for this instance
        SELECT esm.*, (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_type') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_esm_definition
        FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
        JOIN automation.esm_instances inst ON inst.esm_definition_id = esm.id
        WHERE inst.id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;
 
        IF FOUND THEN
            v_blueprint_type := COALESCE(v_esm_definition.blueprint_type_safe, 'lifecycle');
 
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'esm_definition_resolved', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
                'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type
            ));
 
            -- FIXED: Fire events for ALL blueprint types that have them defined
            -- 3. Fire the EXIT event for the OLD stage
            SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
            FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
            WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;
 
            IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                    (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, 
                     context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                VALUES
                    (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, 
                     jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), 
                     v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
            END IF;
 
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exit_event', jsonb_build_object(
                'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
                'event_name', v_on_exit_event,
                'fired', v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL
            ));
 
            -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
            SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
            FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
            WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
 
            IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                    (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, 
                     context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                VALUES
                    (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, 
                     jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), 
                     v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
            END IF;
 
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'entry_event', jsonb_build_object(
                'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
                'event_name', v_on_entry_event,
                'fired', v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL
            ));
 
        ELSE
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_esm_definition', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_instance_id', NEW.automation_esm_instance_id
            ));
        END IF;
 
        -- Flush telemetry: 1 write for the full stage transition
        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_after_update', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object(
                'outcome', 'stage_transition',
                'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
                'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
                'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,
                'exit_event_fired', v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL,
                'entry_event_fired', v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL,
                'exit_event_name', v_on_exit_event,
                'entry_event_name', v_on_entry_event
            ), v_events);
 
    END IF;
 
    -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
    RETURN NULL;
 
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exception', jsonb_build_object(
        'sqlstate', SQLSTATE,
        'sqlerrm', SQLERRM
    ));
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_after_update', v_start_time, 'ERROR',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'exception', 'old_stage', OLD.stage_id, 'new_stage', NEW.stage_id),
        v_events, SQLERRM);
    RAISE;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: esm_orchestration_before_insert(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.esm_orchestration_before_insert() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
-- CHANGES FROM ORIGINAL:
--   1. Added 3 variables: v_trace_id, v_start_time, v_events
--   2. Replaced every INSERT INTO ut_debug_logs with v_events := automation.tel_event(...)
--   3. Added 1 PERFORM automation.tel_flush(...) at end  in EXCEPTION
--   4. Business logic is 100% unchanged
--   5. [019a] Removed NEW.leave_type_id from telemetry — not generic across entities
-- ============================================================================
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type   TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint     RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_found_blueprint BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_match          text[];
    v_filter_matches BOOLEAN;
    v_stage_category TEXT;
    v_record_intent  TEXT;
    v_blueprint_count INT;
    v_blueprint_checked TEXT := '';
    -- OTEL telemetry (3 new variables — trace_id set after shared UUID anchor)
    v_trace_id   UUID;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp();
    v_events     JSONB := '[]'::JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- [SHARED UUID ANCHOR]
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); END IF;
    v_trace_id := NEW.id;  -- trace_id = entity's shared UUID

    -- Log start [019a: removed leave_type_id — not present on all entity tables]
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'start', jsonb_build_object(
        'organization_id', NEW.organization_id,
        'stage_id', NEW.stage_id,
        'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
        'entity_type', v_entity_type
    ));

    -- [AXIS 1: RESOLVE RECORD PILLAR]
    v_record_intent := COALESCE(to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent', to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent_category', '');

    -- Count available blueprints
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_blueprint_count
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND entity_type = v_entity_type
      AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
      AND is_active = TRUE;

    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'blueprints_available', jsonb_build_object(
        'count', v_blueprint_count,
        'organization_id', NEW.organization_id
    ));

    -- [DUAL-AXIS MATCHER]: Universal Resolution
    FOR v_blueprint IN
        SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
        WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema
          AND entity_type = v_entity_type
          AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY organization_id NULLS LAST, version DESC
    LOOP
        v_filter_matches := TRUE;

        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'checking_blueprint', jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
            'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version,
            'partition_filter', v_blueprint.partition_filter,
            'intent', v_blueprint.intent
        ));

        -- Axis 1: Pillar Match
        IF v_record_intent != '' AND v_blueprint.intent IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.intent != '' THEN
            IF v_blueprint.intent != v_record_intent THEN
                v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'intent_mismatch', jsonb_build_object(
                    'record_intent', v_record_intent,
                    'blueprint_intent', v_blueprint.intent
                ));
            END IF;
        END IF;

        -- Axis 2: Discriminator Match (High-Performance Column Matcher)
        IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.partition_filter != '' THEN
            -- Standard Equality Matcher
            FOR v_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*=\s*''([^'']+)''', 'gi')) LOOP
                DECLARE
                    v_actual_value TEXT := to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_match[1];
                    v_matches BOOLEAN := v_actual_value = v_match[2];
                BEGIN
                    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'equality_filter', jsonb_build_object(
                        'column', v_match[1],
                        'expected', v_match[2],
                        'actual', v_actual_value,
                        'matches', v_matches
                    ));
                    IF NOT v_matches THEN
                        v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                        EXIT;
                    END IF;
                END;
            END LOOP;

            -- Generic IN Clause Matcher
            IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter ~* 'IN\s*\(' THEN
                DECLARE
                    v_in_match record;
                BEGIN
                    FOR v_in_match IN
                        SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[1] as col,
                               (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[2] as vals
                    LOOP
                        DECLARE
                            v_actual_value TEXT := to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_in_match.col;
                            v_allowed_values TEXT[];
                        BEGIN
                            v_allowed_values := ARRAY(
                                SELECT trim(both '''' from x)
