                                FROM unnest(string_to_array(replace(v_in_match.vals, ' ', ''), ',')) x
                            );
                            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'in_filter', jsonb_build_object(
                                'column', v_in_match.col,
                                'allowed_values', v_allowed_values,
                                'actual', v_actual_value,
                                'matches', v_actual_value = ANY(v_allowed_values)
                            ));
                            IF NOT (v_actual_value = ANY(v_allowed_values)) THEN
                                v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                                EXIT;
                            END IF;
                        END;
                    END LOOP;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        IF v_filter_matches THEN
            v_found_blueprint := TRUE;
            v_blueprint_checked := v_blueprint.name;
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'blueprint_matched', jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
                'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
                'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version
            ));
            EXIT;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF NOT v_found_blueprint THEN
        -- [019a: removed leave_type_id — not present on all entity tables]
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_blueprint_found', jsonb_build_object(
            'organization_id', NEW.organization_id,
            'blueprints_checked', v_blueprint_checked
        ));

        -- Flush telemetry: 1 write, status = OK (graceful skip)
        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'no_blueprint'), v_events);
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- [ESM PROVISIONING]
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
      AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version LIMIT 1;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_esm_definition', jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version
        ));

        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'no_esm_definition'), v_events);
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'esm_definition_found', jsonb_build_object(
        'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
        'esm_version', v_esm_definition.version
    ));

    -- Provision Instances
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = 'ACTIVE';

    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_stage_category
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id, stage_category)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id, v_stage_category)
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET stage_id = EXCLUDED.stage_id, stage_category = EXCLUDED.stage_category;

    -- [WF EVENTS]
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id, status)
        VALUES (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'pending')
        ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    END IF;

    -- [BINDING]
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN NULL; END;

    -- Flush telemetry: 1 write, full trace
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'success',
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
            'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
            'bp_instance_id', NEW.id,
            'esm_instance_id', NEW.id,
            'final_stage_id', v_final_stage_id
        ), v_events);

    RETURN NEW;

EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    -- Flush telemetry even on error: 1 write
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exception', jsonb_build_object(
        'sqlstate', SQLSTATE,
        'sqlerrm', SQLERRM
    ));
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'ERROR',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'exception'), v_events, SQLERRM);
    RAISE;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: esm_trigger_stage_change(uuid, text, text, uuid, text, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.esm_trigger_stage_change(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_entity_id uuid, p_old_stage_id text, p_new_stage_id text) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$
DECLARE
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_on_exit_event TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT;
    v_esm_instance_id UUID;
    v_new_record JSONB;
    v_old_record JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- 0. Get the instance ID (Linked by entity ID)
    -- We prioritize the most recent instance for this entity
    SELECT id INTO v_esm_instance_id 
    FROM automation.esm_instances 
    WHERE entity_id = p_entity_id AND entity_schema = p_entity_schema 
    ORDER BY created_at DESC
    LIMIT 1;

    IF v_esm_instance_id IS NULL THEN RETURN; END IF;

    -- 1. Update Instance Stage
    UPDATE automation.esm_instances
    SET stage_id = p_new_stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
    WHERE id = v_esm_instance_id;

    -- 2. Get ESM
    SELECT esm.*, (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_type') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.id = (SELECT esm_definition_id FROM automation.esm_instances WHERE id = v_esm_instance_id);

    IF NOT FOUND THEN RETURN; END IF;

    v_blueprint_type := COALESCE(v_esm_definition.blueprint_type_safe, 'lifecycle');

    -- 3. Fetch current records
    EXECUTE format('SELECT to_jsonb(t) FROM %I.%I t WHERE id = $1', p_entity_schema, p_entity_type)
    INTO v_new_record USING p_entity_id;
    v_old_record := v_new_record || jsonb_build_object('stage_id', p_old_stage_id);

    -- 4. Fire Events (Allowed for lifecycle and approval types)
    IF v_blueprint_type IN ('lifecycle', 'approval') THEN
        -- Exit Event
        SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
        FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
        WHERE s->>'id' = p_old_stage_id;

        IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
                organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, 
                trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id
            ) VALUES (
                p_organization_id, p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, 
                jsonb_build_object('new', v_new_record, 'old', v_old_record), 
                p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id
            ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
        END IF;

        -- Entry Event
        SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
        FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
        WHERE s->>'id' = p_new_stage_id;

        IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
                organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, 
                trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id
            ) VALUES (
                p_organization_id, p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, 
                jsonb_build_object('new', v_new_record, 'old', v_old_record), 
                p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id
            ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
        END IF;
    END IF;
END;
$_$;


--
-- Name: fix_ctrm_backfill(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.fix_ctrm_backfill() RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_schema TEXT; v_table TEXT; v_blueprint_id UUID; v_esm_definition RECORD; v_row RECORD;
BEGIN
    FOR v_schema, v_table, v_blueprint_id IN 
        VALUES ('ctrm', 'trades', 'a1b2c3d4-e5f6-47a8-9b10-111213141516'::UUID),
               ('ctrm', 'shipments', 'b2c3d4e5-f6a7-48b9-10c1-121314151617'::UUID),
               ('ctrm', 'contracts', 'c3d4e5f6-a7b8-49c9-10d1-121314151618'::UUID)
    LOOP
        SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition FROM automation.esm_definitions 
        WHERE blueprint_id = v_blueprint_id LIMIT 1;
        
        FOR v_row IN EXECUTE format('SELECT id, organization_id, stage_id FROM %I.%I WHERE automation_esm_instance_id IS NULL', v_schema, v_table)
        LOOP
            INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances
                (organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id)
            VALUES
                (v_row.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_esm_definition.version, v_schema, v_table, v_row.id, COALESCE(v_row.stage_id, v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId'), 'Active', v_blueprint_id)
            RETURNING id INTO v_row.automation_esm_instance_id;
            INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances
                (organization_id, blueprint_id, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
            VALUES
                (v_row.organization_id, v_blueprint_id, v_schema, v_table, v_row.id, 'Active')
            RETURNING id INTO v_row.automation_bp_instance_id;
            EXECUTE format('UPDATE %I.%I SET automation_esm_instance_id = , automation_bp_instance_id =  WHERE id = ', v_schema, v_table)
            USING v_row.automation_esm_instance_id, v_row.automation_bp_instance_id, v_row.id;
        END LOOP;
    END LOOP;
END; $$;


--
-- Name: get_automation_logs_v3(uuid, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.get_automation_logs_v3(p_ticket_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_org_id UUID := p_organization_id;
    
    -- ID Arrays for tracing
    v_ticket_ids UUID[] := ARRAY[p_ticket_id];
    v_task_ids UUID[];
    v_sr_ids UUID[];
    v_invoice_ids UUID[];
    v_all_entity_ids UUID[];
    v_esm_instance_ids UUID[];
    v_event_ids UUID[];
    v_blueprint_ids UUID[];

    -- Final JSONB data holders
    v_ticket_data JSONB;
    v_related_tasks JSONB;
    v_related_service_reports JSONB;
    v_related_invoices JSONB;
    v_esm_instances JSONB;
    v_esm_logs JSONB;
    v_bp_instances JSONB;
    v_wf_events JSONB;
    v_wf_logs JSONB;
    v_sla_breaches JSONB;
    v_scheduled_jobs JSONB;
    v_scheduled_job_logs JSONB;
    v_automation_summary JSONB;

BEGIN
    -- 1. Get Organization ID if not provided
    IF v_org_id IS NULL THEN
        SELECT organization_id INTO v_org_id
        FROM blueprint.tickets
        WHERE id = p_ticket_id;
        
        IF NOT FOUND THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Ticket not found: %', p_ticket_id;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Build the "Family Tree" of related entity IDs - ONLY FOR THIS TICKET
    -- Get tasks directly linked to this ticket
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_task_ids
    FROM blueprint.tasks
    WHERE parent_entity_id = p_ticket_id
      AND parent_entity_type = 'tickets'
      AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- Get service reports linked to this ticket OR its tasks
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_sr_ids
    FROM blueprint.service_reports
    WHERE organization_id = v_org_id
      AND (
          (parent_entity_id = p_ticket_id AND parent_entity_type = 'tickets') 
          OR 
          (task_id IN (SELECT unnest(COALESCE(v_task_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))))
      );

    -- Get invoices linked to this ticket OR its service reports
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_invoice_ids
    FROM blueprint.invoices
    WHERE organization_id = v_org_id
      AND (
          (parent_entity_id = p_ticket_id AND parent_entity_type = 'tickets')
          OR
          (parent_entity_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_sr_ids, '{}'::UUID[])) AND parent_entity_type = 'service_reports')
      );

    -- Combine all IDs into one pool - ONLY entities connected to our ticket
    v_all_entity_ids := v_ticket_ids 
                     || COALESCE(v_task_ids, '{}'::UUID[]) 
                     || COALESCE(v_sr_ids, '{}'::UUID[]) 
                     || COALESCE(v_invoice_ids, '{}'::UUID[]);

    RAISE NOTICE 'Entities traced: %', v_all_entity_ids;

    -- 3. Get blueprint IDs ONLY for this entity family
    SELECT array_agg(DISTINCT blueprint_id) INTO v_blueprint_ids
    FROM automation.bp_instances
    WHERE entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint IDs: %', v_blueprint_ids;

    -- 4. Get all related data objects for the UI - ONLY FOR OUR TICKET
    SELECT row_to_json(t) INTO v_ticket_data 
    FROM blueprint.tickets t 
    WHERE id = p_ticket_id;

    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(t) ORDER BY created_at), '[]') INTO v_related_tasks 
    FROM blueprint.tasks t 
    WHERE id = ANY(COALESCE(v_task_ids, '{}'::UUID[]));

    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(sr) ORDER BY created_at), '[]') INTO v_related_service_reports 
    FROM blueprint.service_reports sr 
    WHERE id = ANY(COALESCE(v_sr_ids, '{}'::UUID[]));

    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(i) ORDER BY created_at), '[]') INTO v_related_invoices 
    FROM blueprint.invoices i 
    WHERE id = ANY(COALESCE(v_invoice_ids, '{}'::UUID[]));

    -- 5. Get automation instances ONLY for this family of entities
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(bp) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_bp_instances
    FROM automation.bp_instances bp
    WHERE bp.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND bp.organization_id = v_org_id;

    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(esm) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_esm_instances
    FROM automation.esm_instances esm
    WHERE esm.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND esm.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- Get the IDs of the ESM instances for the next step
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_esm_instance_ids
    FROM automation.esm_instances
    WHERE entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 6. Get state transition logs ONLY for this family
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(esml) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_esm_logs
    FROM automation.esm_logs esml
    WHERE esml.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND esml.organization_id = v_org_id;
    
    -- 7. Get events fired ONLY by this family
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_event_ids
    FROM automation.wf_events
    WHERE context_entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Event IDs: %', v_event_ids;

    -- 8. Get the events themselves, enriched with rules - ONLY FOR OUR ENTITIES
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'event', e,
            'matched_rules', (
                SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'rule', r,
                        'actions', (
                            SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(a ORDER BY a.action_order), '[]')
                            FROM automation.wf_actions a
                            WHERE a.rule_id = r.id
                            AND a.organization_id = v_org_id
                        )
                    )
                ), '[]')
                FROM automation.wf_rules r
                WHERE r.organization_id = e.organization_id
                  AND r.trigger_schema = e.trigger_schema
                  AND r.trigger_table = e.trigger_table
                  AND r.trigger_type = e.trigger_type
                  AND (r.blueprint_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_blueprint_ids, '{}'::UUID[])))
            )
        )
    ORDER BY e.created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_wf_events
    FROM automation.wf_events e
    WHERE e.id = ANY(COALESCE(v_event_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND e.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 9. CRITICAL FIX: Get workflow logs ONLY for our specific entities and events
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(l ORDER BY l.executed_at), '[]')
    INTO v_wf_logs
    FROM automation.wf_logs l
    WHERE l.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND (
        -- Logs from events triggered by our entities
        l.event_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_event_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
        OR
        -- Logs that directly mention our entities in context
        (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR
        -- Logs that mention our entities in created_entity_id
        (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR
        -- Logs that mention our entities in updated_entity_id  
        (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    );

    RAISE NOTICE 'Workflow logs found: %', (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_wf_logs));

    -- 10. Get scheduled jobs ONLY if they have run for our entities
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'job', sj,
            'rule', r
        ) ORDER BY sj.created_at
    ), '[]')
    INTO v_scheduled_jobs
    FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs sj
    JOIN automation.wf_rules r ON sj.rule_id = r.id
    WHERE r.blueprint_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_blueprint_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND sj.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM automation.wf_logs l 
        WHERE l.scheduled_job_id = sj.id 
        AND l.organization_id = v_org_id
        AND (
            (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
            OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
            OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        )
    );

    -- 11. Get scheduled job logs ONLY for jobs that affected our entities
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(l ORDER BY l.executed_at), '[]')
    INTO v_scheduled_job_logs
    FROM automation.wf_logs l
    WHERE l.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND l.scheduled_job_id IS NOT NULL
    AND (
        (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    );

    -- 12. Get SLA breaches ONLY for our instances
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(sla ORDER BY sla.breached_at), '[]')
    INTO v_sla_breaches
    FROM automation.sla_breaches sla
    WHERE sla.esm_instance_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_esm_instance_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND sla.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 13. Create automation summary
    v_automation_summary := jsonb_build_object(
        'total_tasks', jsonb_array_length(v_related_tasks),
        'total_service_reports', jsonb_array_length(v_related_service_reports),
        'total_invoices', jsonb_array_length(v_related_invoices),
        'total_bp_instances', jsonb_array_length(v_bp_instances),
        'total_esm_instances', jsonb_array_length(v_esm_instances),
        'total_esm_logs', jsonb_array_length(v_esm_logs),
        'total_wf_events', jsonb_array_length(v_wf_events),
        'total_wf_logs', jsonb_array_length(v_wf_logs),
        'total_sla_breaches', jsonb_array_length(v_sla_breaches),
        'total_scheduled_jobs', jsonb_array_length(v_scheduled_jobs)
    );

    -- 14. Return the final composite object
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'ticket', v_ticket_data,
        'related_objects', jsonb_build_object(
            'tasks', v_related_tasks,
            'service_reports', v_related_service_reports,
            'invoices', v_related_invoices
        ),
        'automation_trace', jsonb_build_object(
            'bp_instances', v_bp_instances,
            'esm_instances', v_esm_instances,
            'esm_logs', v_esm_logs,
            'wf_events_and_rules', v_wf_events,
            'wf_logs', v_wf_logs,
            'sla_breaches', v_sla_breaches,
            'scheduled_jobs', v_scheduled_jobs,
            'scheduled_job_logs', v_scheduled_job_logs
        ),
        'summary', v_automation_summary,
        'metadata', jsonb_build_object(
            'ticket_id', p_ticket_id,
            'organization_id', v_org_id,
            'generated_at', NOW(),
            'entity_count', array_length(v_all_entity_ids, 1),
            'entities_traced', v_all_entity_ids,
            'blueprint_ids', v_blueprint_ids
        )
    );

EXCEPTION
    WHEN others THEN
        RAISE NOTICE 'Error gathering automation trace for ticket %: %', p_ticket_id, SQLERRM;
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'error', SQLERRM,
            'error_code', SQLSTATE,
            'ticket_id', p_ticket_id,
            'organization_id', v_org_id
        );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: get_automation_logs_v4(uuid, text, text, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.get_automation_logs_v4(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_entity_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_org_id UUID := p_organization_id;

    v_primary_entity_id UUID := p_entity_id;
    v_all_entity_ids UUID[] := ARRAY[p_entity_id];
    v_child_entity_ids UUID[];
    v_new_children_found BOOLEAN := TRUE;

    v_esm_instance_ids UUID[];
    v_event_ids UUID[];
    v_blueprint_ids UUID[];

    v_primary_entity_data JSONB;
    v_related_objects JSONB := '{}'::JSONB;
    v_esm_instances JSONB;
    v_esm_logs JSONB;
    v_bp_instances JSONB;
    v_wf_events JSONB;
    v_wf_logs JSONB;
    v_sla_breaches JSONB;
    v_scheduled_jobs JSONB;
    v_scheduled_job_logs JSONB;
    v_automation_summary JSONB;

    -- [019b] temp array for merging child IDs from multiple sources
    v_temp_ids UUID[];
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate and fetch primary entity data
    EXECUTE format('SELECT row_to_json(t) FROM %I.%I t WHERE id = %L', p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id)
    INTO v_primary_entity_data;

    IF v_primary_entity_data IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Primary entity (%s.%s with ID: %) not found.', p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Build the "Family Tree" of related entity IDs (Recursive Traversal)
    -- [019b] Now searches BOTH blueprint.* AND esm entities via wf_logs
    WHILE v_new_children_found LOOP
        v_new_children_found := FALSE;
        v_child_entity_ids := '{}'::UUID[];

        -- Find child tasks (blueprint.tasks)
        SELECT array_agg(t.id) INTO v_temp_ids
        FROM blueprint.tasks t
        WHERE t.parent_entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
          AND t.organization_id = v_org_id
          AND t.id <> ALL(v_all_entity_ids);
        IF v_temp_ids IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_child_entity_ids := array_cat(v_child_entity_ids, v_temp_ids);
        END IF;

        -- Find child service reports (blueprint.service_reports)
        SELECT array_agg(sr.id) INTO v_temp_ids
        FROM blueprint.service_reports sr
        WHERE (sr.parent_entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids) OR sr.task_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
          AND sr.organization_id = v_org_id
          AND sr.id <> ALL(v_all_entity_ids);
        IF v_temp_ids IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_child_entity_ids := array_cat(v_child_entity_ids, v_temp_ids);
        END IF;

        -- Find child invoices (blueprint.invoices)
        SELECT array_agg(i.id) INTO v_temp_ids
        FROM blueprint.invoices i
        WHERE i.parent_entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
          AND i.organization_id = v_org_id
          AND i.id <> ALL(v_all_entity_ids);
        IF v_temp_ids IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_child_entity_ids := array_cat(v_child_entity_ids, v_temp_ids);
        END IF;

        -- [019b] Find ESM child entities via wf_logs orchestration links
        SELECT array_agg(ei.entity_id) INTO v_temp_ids
        FROM automation.esm_instances ei
        WHERE ei.organization_id = v_org_id
          AND ei.entity_id <> ALL(v_all_entity_ids)
          AND EXISTS (
              SELECT 1 FROM automation.wf_logs wl
              WHERE wl.organization_id = v_org_id
                AND (wl.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
                AND (wl.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ei.entity_id
          );
        IF v_temp_ids IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_child_entity_ids := array_cat(v_child_entity_ids, v_temp_ids);
        END IF;

        IF array_length(v_child_entity_ids, 1) > 0 THEN
            v_all_entity_ids := array_cat(v_all_entity_ids, v_child_entity_ids);
            v_new_children_found := TRUE;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Entities traced: %', v_all_entity_ids;

    -- 3. Get blueprint IDs
    SELECT array_agg(DISTINCT blueprint_id) INTO v_blueprint_ids
    FROM automation.bp_instances
    WHERE entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 4. Get related data objects
    v_related_objects := jsonb_set(v_related_objects, '{tasks}',
        (SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(t) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
         FROM blueprint.tasks t
         WHERE t.id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    , true);

    v_related_objects := jsonb_set(v_related_objects, '{service_reports}',
        (SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(sr) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
         FROM blueprint.service_reports sr
         WHERE sr.id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    , true);

    -- [019b] Fixed: removed duplicate invoices line
    v_related_objects := jsonb_set(v_related_objects, '{invoices}',
        (SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(i) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
         FROM blueprint.invoices i
         WHERE i.id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    , true);

    -- 5. Get automation instances
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(bp) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_bp_instances
    FROM automation.bp_instances bp
    WHERE bp.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND bp.organization_id = v_org_id;

    SELECT
        coalesce(array_agg(esm.id), '{}'::UUID[]),
        coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(esm) ORDER BY esm.created_at), '[]'::JSONB)
    INTO
        v_esm_instance_ids,
        v_esm_instances
    FROM automation.esm_instances esm
    WHERE esm.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
      AND esm.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 6. Get state transition logs
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(row_to_json(esml) ORDER BY created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_esm_logs
    FROM automation.esm_logs esml
    WHERE esml.entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND esml.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 7. Get events fired
    SELECT array_agg(id) INTO v_event_ids
    FROM automation.wf_events
    WHERE context_entity_id = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    AND organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 8. Get events enriched with rules
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'event', e,
            'matched_rules', (
                SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'rule', r,
                        'actions', (
                            SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(a ORDER BY a.action_order), '[]')
                            FROM automation.wf_actions a
                            WHERE a.rule_id = r.id
                            AND a.organization_id = v_org_id
                        )
                    )
                ), '[]')
                FROM automation.wf_rules r
                WHERE r.organization_id = e.organization_id
                  AND r.trigger_schema = e.trigger_schema
                  AND r.trigger_table = e.trigger_table
                  AND r.trigger_type = e.trigger_type
                  AND (r.blueprint_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_blueprint_ids, '{}'::UUID[])))
            )
        )
    ORDER BY e.created_at), '[]')
    INTO v_wf_events
    FROM automation.wf_events e
    WHERE e.id = ANY(COALESCE(v_event_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND e.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 9. Get workflow logs
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(l ORDER BY l.executed_at), '[]')
    INTO v_wf_logs
    FROM automation.wf_logs l
    WHERE l.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND (
        l.event_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_event_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
        OR (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
        OR (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
        OR (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids)
    );

    -- 10. Get scheduled jobs
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'job', sj,
            'rule', r
        ) ORDER BY sj.created_at
    ), '[]')
    INTO v_scheduled_jobs
    FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs sj
    JOIN automation.wf_rules r ON sj.rule_id = r.id
    WHERE r.blueprint_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_blueprint_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND sj.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM automation.wf_logs l
        WHERE l.scheduled_job_id = sj.id
        AND l.organization_id = v_org_id
        AND (
            (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
            OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
            OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        )
    );

    -- 11. Get scheduled job logs
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(l ORDER BY l.executed_at), '[]')
    INTO v_scheduled_job_logs
    FROM automation.wf_logs l
    WHERE l.organization_id = v_org_id
    AND l.scheduled_job_id IS NOT NULL
    AND (
        (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'created_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
        OR (l.context IS NOT NULL AND (l.context->>'updated_entity_id')::UUID = ANY(v_all_entity_ids))
    );

    -- 12. Get SLA breaches
    SELECT coalesce(jsonb_agg(sla ORDER BY sla.breached_at), '[]')
    INTO v_sla_breaches
    FROM automation.sla_breaches sla
    WHERE sla.esm_instance_id = ANY(COALESCE(v_esm_instance_ids, '{}'::UUID[]))
    AND sla.organization_id = v_org_id;

    -- 13. Create automation summary
    v_automation_summary := jsonb_build_object(
        'total_entities_traced', array_length(v_all_entity_ids, 1),
        'total_bp_instances', jsonb_array_length(v_bp_instances),
        'total_esm_instances', jsonb_array_length(v_esm_instances),
        'total_wf_logs', jsonb_array_length(v_wf_logs)
    );

    -- 14. Return the final composite object
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'primary_entity', v_primary_entity_data,
        'related_objects', v_related_objects,
        'automation_trace', jsonb_build_object(
            'bp_instances', v_bp_instances,
            'esm_instances', v_esm_instances,
            'esm_logs', v_esm_logs,
            'wf_events_and_rules', v_wf_events,
            'wf_logs', v_wf_logs,
            'sla_breaches', v_sla_breaches,
            'scheduled_jobs', v_scheduled_jobs,
            'scheduled_job_logs', v_scheduled_job_logs
        ),
        'summary', v_automation_summary,
        'metadata', jsonb_build_object(
            'primary_entity_id', p_entity_id,
            'primary_entity_schema', p_entity_schema,
            'primary_entity_type', p_entity_type,
            'organization_id', v_org_id,
            'generated_at', NOW(),
            'entity_count', array_length(v_all_entity_ids, 1),
            'entities_traced', v_all_entity_ids,
            'blueprint_ids', v_blueprint_ids
        )
    );

EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE NOTICE 'Error gathering automation trace for entity %s.%s with ID %: %', p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id, SQLERRM;
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'error', SQLERRM,
            'error_code', SQLSTATE,
            'primary_entity_id', p_entity_id
        );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: log_compilation_error_bp(uuid, uuid, integer, uuid, text, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.log_compilation_error_bp(p_compilation_id uuid, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_blueprint_version integer, p_organization_id uuid, p_error_context text, p_error_message text) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    UPDATE automation.comp_blueprint_compilation_logs
    SET 
        status = 'failed',
        finished_at = NOW(),
        error_message = p_error_context || ': ' || p_error_message,
        artifacts_generated = jsonb_build_object(
            'error_context', p_error_context,
            'error_details', p_error_message
        )
    WHERE id = p_compilation_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: log_compilation_status_bp(uuid, uuid, integer, uuid, text, jsonb, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.log_compilation_status_bp(p_compilation_id uuid, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_blueprint_version integer, p_organization_id uuid, p_status text, p_details jsonb, p_started_at timestamp with time zone, p_finished_at timestamp with time zone, p_compiled_by uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO automation.comp_blueprint_compilation_logs (
        id, blueprint_id, organization_id, blueprint_version, status,
        started_at, finished_at, error_message, artifacts_generated, compiled_by
    ) VALUES (
        p_compilation_id, p_blueprint_id, p_organization_id, p_blueprint_version, p_status,
        p_started_at, p_finished_at,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'failed' THEN p_details->>'error' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'success' THEN p_details->'artifacts' ELSE NULL END,
        p_compiled_by
    );
END; $$;


--
-- Name: log_esm_initial_entry(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.log_esm_initial_entry() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Update instance category if not set
    IF NEW.stage_category IS NULL THEN
        NEW.stage_category := automation.util_get_stage_category(NEW.esm_definition_id, NEW.stage_id);
    END IF;

    INSERT INTO automation.esm_logs (
        organization_id, esm_instance_id, entity_id, entity_schema, entity_type,
        from_stage_id, to_stage_id, to_stage_category, transition_type, metadata, created_at
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.organization_id, NEW.id, NEW.entity_id, NEW.entity_schema, NEW.entity_type,
        NULL, NEW.stage_id, NEW.stage_category, 'entry',
        jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_id', NEW.blueprint_id,
            'blueprint_version', NEW.blueprint_version,
            'automation_trigger', 'initial_creation'
        ),
        NEW.created_at
    );
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: log_esm_transition(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.log_esm_transition() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_new_category text;
BEGIN
    IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id THEN
        v_new_category := automation.util_get_stage_category(NEW.esm_definition_id, NEW.stage_id);
        NEW.stage_category := v_new_category;

        INSERT INTO automation.esm_logs (
            organization_id, esm_instance_id, entity_id, entity_schema, entity_type,
            from_stage_id, to_stage_id, to_stage_category, transition_type, metadata
        ) VALUES (
            OLD.organization_id, OLD.id, OLD.entity_id, OLD.entity_schema, OLD.entity_type,
            OLD.stage_id, NEW.stage_id, v_new_category, 'transition',
            jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_id', OLD.blueprint_id,
                'blueprint_version', OLD.blueprint_version,
                'automation_trigger', 'stage_change'
            )
        )
        ON CONFLICT (esm_instance_id, from_stage_id, to_stage_id, created_at) 
        DO NOTHING;
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: n_bp_validate_definition(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.n_bp_validate_definition(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$BEGIN
    RETURN (
        (p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'start_stage' OR p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'startStateId') 
        AND p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'stages'
        AND jsonb_array_length(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') > 0
    );
END;$$;


--
-- Name: n_cleanup_blueprint_artifacts(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.n_cleanup_blueprint_artifacts(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_version INT;
    v_deleted_count INT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Centralized cleanup function for blueprint artifacts
    -- 2. Safer version-specific deletion to avoid race conditions
    -- 3. Better logging and count reporting
    -- 4. Used by both compilation and deactivation processes
    
    SELECT version INTO v_version 
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;
    
    IF v_version IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint not found: %', p_blueprint_id;
    END IF;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleaning up artifacts for Blueprint ID %, Version %', p_blueprint_id, v_version;
    
    -- Cleanup in dependency order (jobs -> actions -> rules -> esm)
    DELETE FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs 
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
      AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_version;
    GET DIAGNOSTICS v_deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % scheduled jobs', v_deleted_count;
    
    DELETE FROM automation.wf_actions 
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
      AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_version;
    GET DIAGNOSTICS v_deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % actions', v_deleted_count;
      
    DELETE FROM automation.wf_rules 
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
      AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_version;
    GET DIAGNOSTICS v_deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % rules', v_deleted_count;

    DELETE FROM automation.esm_definitions 
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id 
      AND (metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_version;
    GET DIAGNOSTICS v_deleted_count = ROW_COUNT;
    RAISE NOTICE '  Deleted % ESM definitions', v_deleted_count;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleanup completed for Blueprint ID %', p_blueprint_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: n_log_compilation_status_bp(uuid, uuid, integer, uuid, text, jsonb, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.n_log_compilation_status_bp(p_compilation_id uuid, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_blueprint_version integer, p_organization_id uuid, p_status text, p_details jsonb, p_started_at timestamp with time zone, p_finished_at timestamp with time zone, p_compiled_by uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Enhanced compilation logging with more detailed artifacts
    -- 2. Better error context and structured logging
    -- 3. Includes compilation function version for tracking
    -- 4. Maintains backward compatibility with existing logs
    
    INSERT INTO automation.comp_blueprint_compilation_logs (
        id, blueprint_id, organization_id, blueprint_version, status,
        started_at, finished_at, error_message, artifacts_generated, compiled_by,
        metadata
    ) VALUES (
        p_compilation_id, p_blueprint_id, p_organization_id, p_blueprint_version, p_status,
        p_started_at, p_finished_at,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'failed' THEN p_details->>'error' ELSE NULL END,
        CASE WHEN p_status = 'success' THEN p_details->'artifacts' ELSE NULL END,
        p_compiled_by,
        jsonb_build_object(
            'compilation_function', 'n_bp_compile',
            'error_context', p_details->>'context',
            'log_version', 'v2'
        )
    );
END;$$;


--
-- Name: notify_new_event(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.notify_new_event() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- Just notify that a new event exists - NO processing here
    PERFORM pg_notify('wf_events_new', NEW.id::text);
    
    -- Minimal: Just return the record
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: pm_detect_anomalies(uuid, text, text, integer, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_detect_anomalies(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_current_window_days integer DEFAULT 7, p_baseline_window_days integer DEFAULT 30) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_anomalies JSONB := '[]'::jsonb;
    v_current_avg NUMERIC;
    v_baseline_avg NUMERIC;
    v_current_breach_rate NUMERIC;
    v_baseline_breach_rate NUMERIC;
    v_deviation NUMERIC;
    r RECORD;
BEGIN
    -- Check each stage for dwell time anomalies
    FOR r IN
        SELECT
            stage_id,
            AVG(CASE WHEN entered_at > now() - (p_current_window_days || ' days')::interval
                THEN dwell_ms END) AS current_avg,
            AVG(CASE WHEN entered_at > now() - (p_baseline_window_days || ' days')::interval
                     AND entered_at <= now() - (p_current_window_days || ' days')::interval
                THEN dwell_ms END) AS baseline_avg
        FROM automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times
        WHERE organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND dwell_ms IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY stage_id
        HAVING COUNT(*) FILTER (WHERE entered_at > now() - (p_current_window_days || ' days')::interval) > 5
    LOOP
        IF r.baseline_avg IS NOT NULL AND r.baseline_avg > 0 THEN
            v_deviation := ((r.current_avg - r.baseline_avg) / r.baseline_avg) * 100;

            IF ABS(v_deviation) > 25 THEN
                v_anomalies := v_anomalies || jsonb_build_object(
                    'type', 'stage_dwell_deviation',
                    'stage_id', r.stage_id,
                    'current_avg_ms', ROUND(r.current_avg),
                    'baseline_avg_ms', ROUND(r.baseline_avg),
                    'deviation_percent', ROUND(v_deviation, 1),
                    'severity', CASE
                        WHEN ABS(v_deviation) > 100 THEN 'critical'
                        WHEN ABS(v_deviation) > 50 THEN 'high'
                        ELSE 'medium'
                    END,
                    'direction', CASE WHEN v_deviation > 0 THEN 'slower' ELSE 'faster' END
                );
            END IF;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'organization_id', p_organization_id,
        'entity_schema', p_entity_schema,
        'entity_type', p_entity_type,
        'analysis_timestamp', now(),
        'current_window_days', p_current_window_days,
        'baseline_window_days', p_baseline_window_days,
        'anomaly_count', jsonb_array_length(v_anomalies),
        'anomalies', v_anomalies
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_detect_anomalies(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_current_window_days integer, p_baseline_window_days integer); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_detect_anomalies(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_current_window_days integer, p_baseline_window_days integer) IS 'Compares current period stage dwell times against baseline to detect anomalies (>25% deviation).
Usage: SELECT automation.pm_detect_anomalies(org_id, ''esm'', ''tickets'', 7, 30);';


--
-- Name: pm_discover_process_patterns(uuid, text, text, timestamp with time zone, timestamp with time zone, uuid, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_discover_process_patterns(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_window_start timestamp with time zone DEFAULT (now() - '30 days'::interval), p_window_end timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(), p_blueprint_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_analysis_key text DEFAULT NULL::text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_mining_id UUID;
    v_discovered_steps JSONB;
    v_transitions JSONB;
    v_variants JSONB;
    v_bottlenecks JSONB;
    v_anomalies JSONB;
    v_instance_count INTEGER;
    v_avg_cycle BIGINT;
    v_median_cycle BIGINT;
    v_p95_cycle BIGINT;
    v_sla_breach_rate NUMERIC(5,4);
    v_total_instances INTEGER;
    v_breached_instances INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 1: Discover steps (unique stages visited) with frequency and dwell
    -- FIX: Alias COUNT(*) as freq, then ORDER BY freq
    -- ========================================================================
    SELECT jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'stage_id', s.stage_id,
            'stage_category', s.stage_category,
            'frequency', s.freq,
            'avg_dwell_ms', s.avg_dwell_ms
        ) ORDER BY s.freq DESC
    )
    INTO v_discovered_steps
    FROM (
        SELECT
            el.to_stage_id AS stage_id,
            el.to_stage_category AS stage_category,
            COUNT(*) AS freq,
            AVG(
                EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (
                    COALESCE(
                        (SELECT el2.created_at
                         FROM automation.esm_logs el2
                         WHERE el2.esm_instance_id = el.esm_instance_id
                           AND el2.created_at > el.created_at
                         ORDER BY el2.created_at LIMIT 1),
                        p_window_end
                    ) - el.created_at
                )) * 1000
            ) AS avg_dwell_ms
        FROM automation.esm_logs el
        WHERE el.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND el.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND el.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND el.created_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end
          AND el.to_stage_id IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY el.to_stage_id, el.to_stage_category
    ) s;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 2: Discover transitions (from → to with frequency and duration)
    -- FIX: Include ALL rows in window so LAG can see the previous (entry) row,
    --       then filter to transition rows AFTER LAG computes
    -- ========================================================================
    WITH all_logs AS (
        -- Include ALL rows so LAG can look back at entry rows
        SELECT
            el.esm_instance_id,
            el.from_stage_id,
            el.to_stage_id,
            el.transition_type,
            el.created_at,
            LAG(el.created_at) OVER (
                PARTITION BY el.esm_instance_id ORDER BY el.created_at
            ) AS prev_created_at
        FROM automation.esm_logs el
        WHERE el.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND el.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND el.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND el.created_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end
    ),
    transition_stats AS (
        -- NOW filter to only transition rows (from_stage_id IS NOT NULL)
        SELECT
            from_stage_id,
            to_stage_id,
            COUNT(*) AS freq,
            AVG(
                EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (created_at - prev_created_at)) * 1000
            ) AS avg_duration_ms
        FROM all_logs
        WHERE from_stage_id IS NOT NULL
          AND to_stage_id IS NOT NULL
          AND prev_created_at IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY from_stage_id, to_stage_id
    )
    SELECT jsonb_agg(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'from_stage', ts.from_stage_id,
            'to_stage', ts.to_stage_id,
            'frequency', ts.freq,
            'avg_duration_ms', ts.avg_duration_ms
        ) ORDER BY ts.freq DESC
    )
    INTO v_transitions
    FROM transition_stats ts;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 3: Discover variants (unique paths through the state machine)
    -- FIX: SUM() OVER() inside jsonb_agg() is window-in-aggregate — not allowed
    -- Compute total_count separately, then use it in the aggregation
    -- ========================================================================
    WITH instance_paths AS (
        SELECT
            el.esm_instance_id,
            ARRAY_AGG(el.to_stage_id ORDER BY el.created_at) AS path,
            MIN(el.created_at) AS started_at,
            MAX(el.created_at) AS ended_at
        FROM automation.esm_logs el
        WHERE el.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND el.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND el.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND el.created_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end
          AND el.to_stage_id IS NOT NULL
        GROUP BY el.esm_instance_id
    ),
    variant_stats AS (
        SELECT
            path,
            COUNT(*) AS cnt,
            AVG(EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (ended_at - started_at)) * 1000) AS avg_dur
        FROM instance_paths
        GROUP BY path
    ),
    variant_total AS (
        SELECT SUM(cnt) AS total_count FROM variant_stats
    )
    SELECT
        jsonb_agg(
            jsonb_build_object(
                'path', ARRAY_TO_STRING(vs.path, '→'),
                'count', vs.cnt,
                'avg_duration_ms', vs.avg_dur,
                'percentage', ROUND(vs.cnt::numeric / NULLIF(vt.total_count, 0) * 100, 1)
            ) ORDER BY vs.cnt DESC
        ),
        vt.total_count::integer
    INTO v_variants, v_instance_count
    FROM variant_stats vs
    CROSS JOIN variant_total vt
    GROUP BY vt.total_count;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 4: Identify bottlenecks (stages with highest avg dwell time)
    -- (unchanged — avg_dwell_ms is a real column alias, works correctly)
    -- ========================================================================
    SELECT jsonb_agg(bottleneck_data ORDER BY avg_dwell_ms DESC)
    INTO v_bottlenecks
    FROM (
        SELECT jsonb_build_object(
            'stage_id', stage_id,
            'avg_dwell_ms', avg_dwell_ms,
            'median_dwell_ms', median_dwell_ms,
            'entry_count', entry_count,
            'impact', CASE
                WHEN avg_dwell_ms > (SELECT AVG(sub.avg_dwell_ms) * 2 FROM (
                    SELECT AVG(dwell_ms) AS avg_dwell_ms
                    FROM automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times d
                    WHERE d.organization_id = p_organization_id
                      AND d.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
                      AND d.entity_type = p_entity_type
                      AND d.dwell_ms IS NOT NULL
                    GROUP BY d.stage_id
                ) sub) THEN 'high'
                WHEN avg_dwell_ms > (SELECT AVG(sub.avg_dwell_ms) * 1.5 FROM (
                    SELECT AVG(dwell_ms) AS avg_dwell_ms
                    FROM automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times d
                    WHERE d.organization_id = p_organization_id
                      AND d.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
                      AND d.entity_type = p_entity_type
                      AND d.dwell_ms IS NOT NULL
                    GROUP BY d.stage_id
                ) sub) THEN 'medium'
                ELSE 'low'
            END
        ) AS bottleneck_data,
        avg_dwell_ms
        FROM (
            SELECT
                d.stage_id,
                AVG(d.dwell_ms) AS avg_dwell_ms,
                PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY d.dwell_ms) AS median_dwell_ms,
                COUNT(*) AS entry_count
            FROM automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times d
            WHERE d.organization_id = p_organization_id
              AND d.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
              AND d.entity_type = p_entity_type
              AND d.dwell_ms IS NOT NULL
              AND d.entered_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end
            GROUP BY d.stage_id
        ) stage_stats
    ) bottleneck_data;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 5: Cycle time stats
    -- (unchanged)
    -- ========================================================================
    WITH cycle_times AS (
        SELECT
            EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (MAX(el.created_at) - MIN(el.created_at))) * 1000 AS cycle_ms
        FROM automation.esm_logs el
        WHERE el.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND el.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND el.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND el.created_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end
        GROUP BY el.esm_instance_id
        HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
    )
    SELECT
        AVG(cycle_ms)::bigint,
        (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY cycle_ms))::bigint,
        (PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY cycle_ms))::bigint
    INTO v_avg_cycle, v_median_cycle, v_p95_cycle
    FROM cycle_times;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 6: SLA breach rate
    -- (unchanged)
    -- ========================================================================
    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT ei.id)
    INTO v_total_instances
    FROM automation.esm_instances ei
    WHERE ei.organization_id = p_organization_id
      AND ei.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
      AND ei.entity_type = p_entity_type
      AND ei.created_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end;

    SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT sb.esm_instance_id)
    INTO v_breached_instances
    FROM automation.sla_breaches sb
    JOIN automation.esm_instances ei ON ei.id = sb.esm_instance_id
    WHERE sb.organization_id = p_organization_id
      AND ei.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
      AND ei.entity_type = p_entity_type
      AND sb.breached_at BETWEEN p_window_start AND p_window_end;

    v_sla_breach_rate := CASE
        WHEN v_total_instances > 0 THEN v_breached_instances::numeric / v_total_instances
        ELSE 0
    END;

    -- ========================================================================
    -- Step 7: Insert mining result
    -- (unchanged)
    -- ========================================================================
    INSERT INTO automation.pm_mining_results (
        organization_id,
        analysis_key,
        analysis_window_start,
        analysis_window_end,
        blueprint_id,
        entity_schema,
        entity_type,
        discovered_steps,
        transitions,
        variants,
        bottlenecks,
        anomalies,
        instance_count,
        avg_cycle_time_ms,
        median_cycle_time_ms,
        p95_cycle_time_ms,
        sla_breach_rate,
        algorithm,
        confidence_score
    ) VALUES (
        p_organization_id,
        COALESCE(p_analysis_key, p_entity_schema || '.' || p_entity_type || '_analysis'),
        p_window_start,
        p_window_end,
        p_blueprint_id,
        p_entity_schema,
        p_entity_type,
        v_discovered_steps,
        v_transitions,
        v_variants,
        v_bottlenecks,
        v_anomalies,
        COALESCE(v_instance_count, 0),
        v_avg_cycle,
        v_median_cycle,
        v_p95_cycle,
        v_sla_breach_rate,
        'esm_log_replay',
        CASE
            WHEN COALESCE(v_instance_count, 0) >= 100 THEN 95.0
            WHEN COALESCE(v_instance_count, 0) >= 30 THEN 80.0
            WHEN COALESCE(v_instance_count, 0) >= 10 THEN 60.0
            ELSE 30.0
        END
    )
    RETURNING id INTO v_mining_id;

    RETURN v_mining_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_discover_process_patterns(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_window_start timestamp with time zone, p_window_end timestamp with time zone, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_analysis_key text); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_discover_process_patterns(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_window_start timestamp with time zone, p_window_end timestamp with time zone, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_analysis_key text) IS 'Core process mining function. Analyzes esm_logs for a given entity type over a time window.
Returns the UUID of the created pm_mining_results row.
Fixed in 004a: ORDER BY on real columns, LAG in CTE, SUM OVER in CTE.
Usage: SELECT automation.pm_discover_process_patterns(org_id, ''esm'', ''tickets'');';


--
-- Name: pm_get_stage_bottlenecks(uuid, text, text, integer, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_get_stage_bottlenecks(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer DEFAULT 30, p_limit integer DEFAULT 5) RETURNS TABLE(stage_id text, stage_category text, avg_dwell_hours numeric, median_dwell_hours numeric, p95_dwell_hours numeric, entry_count bigint, impact text)
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE
    AS $$
    WITH stage_stats AS (
        SELECT
            d.stage_id,
            d.stage_category,
            AVG(d.dwell_hours) AS avg_dwell_hours,
            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.5) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY d.dwell_hours) AS median_dwell_hours,
            PERCENTILE_CONT(0.95) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY d.dwell_hours) AS p95_dwell_hours,
            COUNT(*) AS entry_count
        FROM automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times d
        WHERE d.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND d.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND d.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND d.dwell_ms IS NOT NULL
          AND d.entered_at > now() - (p_days || ' days')::interval
        GROUP BY d.stage_id, d.stage_category
    ),
    overall_avg AS (
        SELECT AVG(avg_dwell_hours) AS grand_avg FROM stage_stats
    )
    SELECT
        ss.stage_id,
        ss.stage_category,
        ROUND(ss.avg_dwell_hours::numeric, 2),
        ROUND(ss.median_dwell_hours::numeric, 2),
        ROUND(ss.p95_dwell_hours::numeric, 2),
        ss.entry_count,
        CASE
            WHEN ss.avg_dwell_hours > oa.grand_avg * 2 THEN 'high'
            WHEN ss.avg_dwell_hours > oa.grand_avg * 1.5 THEN 'medium'
            ELSE 'low'
        END AS impact
    FROM stage_stats ss
    CROSS JOIN overall_avg oa
    ORDER BY ss.avg_dwell_hours DESC
    LIMIT p_limit;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_get_stage_bottlenecks(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer, p_limit integer); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_get_stage_bottlenecks(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer, p_limit integer) IS 'Returns top N bottleneck stages for a given entity type. Uses materialized view data.
Usage: SELECT * FROM automation.pm_get_stage_bottlenecks(org_id, ''esm'', ''tickets'', 30, 5);';


--
-- Name: pm_get_variant_distribution(uuid, text, text, integer, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_get_variant_distribution(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer DEFAULT 30, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10) RETURNS TABLE(variant_key text, instance_count bigint, percentage numeric, avg_cycle_hours numeric, step_count integer)
    LANGUAGE sql STABLE
    AS $$
    WITH variants AS (
        SELECT
            vp.variant_key,
            COUNT(*) AS instance_count,
            AVG(vp.total_duration_ms / 3600000.0) AS avg_cycle_hours,
            (ARRAY_LENGTH(vp.stage_path, 1)) AS step_count
        FROM automation.pm_mv_process_variant_paths vp
        WHERE vp.organization_id = p_organization_id
          AND vp.entity_schema = p_entity_schema
          AND vp.entity_type = p_entity_type
          AND vp.started_at > now() - (p_days || ' days')::interval
        GROUP BY vp.variant_key, ARRAY_LENGTH(vp.stage_path, 1)
    ),
    total AS (
        SELECT SUM(instance_count) AS total_count FROM variants
    )
    SELECT
        v.variant_key,
        v.instance_count,
        ROUND(v.instance_count::numeric / NULLIF(t.total_count, 0) * 100, 1) AS percentage,
        ROUND(v.avg_cycle_hours::numeric, 2),
        v.step_count
    FROM variants v
    CROSS JOIN total t
    ORDER BY v.instance_count DESC
    LIMIT p_limit;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_get_variant_distribution(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer, p_limit integer); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_get_variant_distribution(p_organization_id uuid, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_days integer, p_limit integer) IS 'Returns process variant distribution for a given entity type. Shows which paths are most/least common.
Usage: SELECT * FROM automation.pm_get_variant_distribution(org_id, ''esm'', ''tickets'', 30, 10);';


--
-- Name: pm_refresh_materialized_views(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_refresh_materialized_views() RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_start TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp();
    v_result JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_step_start TIMESTAMPTZ;
BEGIN
    -- Step 1: Dwell times (base layer)
    v_step_start := clock_timestamp();
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY automation.pm_mv_stage_dwell_times;
    v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
        'stage_dwell_times_ms', EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_step_start)) * 1000
    );

    -- Step 2: Variant paths (base layer)
    v_step_start := clock_timestamp();
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY automation.pm_mv_process_variant_paths;
    v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
        'variant_paths_ms', EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_step_start)) * 1000
    );

    -- Step 3: Dashboard (depends on dwell times)
    v_step_start := clock_timestamp();
    REFRESH MATERIALIZED VIEW CONCURRENTLY automation.pm_mv_process_dashboard;
    v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
        'dashboard_ms', EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_step_start)) * 1000
    );

    v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
        'total_ms', EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start)) * 1000,
        'refreshed_at', now()
    );

    RETURN v_result;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_refresh_materialized_views(); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_refresh_materialized_views() IS 'Refreshes all PM materialized views in dependency order. Call via pg_cron or manually.';


--
-- Name: pm_run_daily_mining(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_run_daily_mining() RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_result JSONB := '[]'::jsonb;
    v_mining_id UUID;
    r RECORD;
BEGIN
    -- Find all active blueprints with ESM definitions
    FOR r IN
        SELECT DISTINCT
            bp.organization_id,
            bp.entity_schema,
            bp.entity_type,
            bp.id AS blueprint_id
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
        JOIN automation.esm_definitions ed ON ed.blueprint_id = bp.id AND ed.is_active = true
        WHERE bp.is_active = true
          AND bp.blueprint_type IN ('lifecycle', 'approval')
          -- Only mine blueprints that have recent activity
          AND EXISTS (
              SELECT 1 FROM automation.esm_logs el
              WHERE el.entity_schema = bp.entity_schema
                AND el.entity_type = bp.entity_type
                AND el.organization_id = bp.organization_id
                AND el.created_at > now() - INTERVAL '7 days'
          )
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            v_mining_id := automation.pm_discover_process_patterns(
                p_organization_id := r.organization_id,
                p_entity_schema := r.entity_schema,
                p_entity_type := r.entity_type,
                p_window_start := now() - INTERVAL '30 days',
                p_window_end := now(),
                p_blueprint_id := r.blueprint_id,
                p_analysis_key := r.entity_schema || '.' || r.entity_type || '_daily'
            );

            v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
                'entity', r.entity_schema || '.' || r.entity_type,
                'blueprint_id', r.blueprint_id,
                'mining_id', v_mining_id,
                'status', 'success'
            );
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
                'entity', r.entity_schema || '.' || r.entity_type,
                'blueprint_id', r.blueprint_id,
                'status', 'error',
                'error', SQLERRM
            );
        END;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'ran_at', now(),
        'blueprints_analyzed', jsonb_array_length(v_result),
        'results', v_result
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_run_daily_mining(); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_run_daily_mining() IS 'Runs process mining analysis for all active blueprints with recent ESM activity. Scheduled daily at 02:00 UTC.';


--
-- Name: pm_run_weekly_anomaly_check(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_run_weekly_anomaly_check() RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_result JSONB := '[]'::jsonb;
    v_anomalies JSONB;
    r RECORD;
BEGIN
    FOR r IN
        SELECT DISTINCT
            bp.organization_id,
            bp.entity_schema,
            bp.entity_type
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
        WHERE bp.is_active = true
          AND bp.blueprint_type IN ('lifecycle', 'approval')
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            v_anomalies := automation.pm_detect_anomalies(
                p_organization_id := r.organization_id,
                p_entity_schema := r.entity_schema,
                p_entity_type := r.entity_type,
                p_current_window_days := 7,
                p_baseline_window_days := 30
            );

            IF (v_anomalies ->> 'anomaly_count')::integer > 0 THEN
                v_result := v_result || v_anomalies;

                -- Log anomalies to process_performance_heartbeat
                INSERT INTO automation.process_performance_heartbeat (
                    organization_id,
                    blueprint_id,
                    metric_key,
                    metric_value,
                    unit,
                    is_anomaly,
                    deviation_percent,
                    metadata,
                    captured_at
                )
                SELECT
                    r.organization_id,
                    (SELECT id FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
                     WHERE organization_id = r.organization_id
                       AND entity_schema = r.entity_schema
                       AND entity_type = r.entity_type
                       AND is_active = true
                     LIMIT 1),
                    'pm_anomaly_detected',
                    (v_anomalies ->> 'anomaly_count')::numeric,
                    'count',
                    true,
                    (SELECT MAX((a ->> 'deviation_percent')::numeric)
                     FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_anomalies -> 'anomalies') a),
                    v_anomalies,
                    now();
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            -- Log error but continue
            v_result := v_result || jsonb_build_object(
                'entity', r.entity_schema || '.' || r.entity_type,
                'status', 'error',
                'error', SQLERRM
            );
        END;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'ran_at', now(),
        'entities_checked', jsonb_array_length(v_result),
        'results', v_result
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_run_weekly_anomaly_check(); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_run_weekly_anomaly_check() IS 'Checks all active blueprints for performance anomalies. Logs to process_performance_heartbeat. Scheduled weekly on Mondays 03:00 UTC.';


--
-- Name: pm_trg_sla_breach_resolution(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.pm_trg_sla_breach_resolution() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    -- When resolved_at is set for the first time, compute duration
    IF NEW.resolved_at IS NOT NULL AND OLD.resolved_at IS NULL THEN
        NEW.violation_duration_hours := EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (NEW.resolved_at - NEW.breached_at)) / 3600;
    END IF;
    RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION pm_trg_sla_breach_resolution(); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.pm_trg_sla_breach_resolution() IS 'Auto-calculates violation_duration_hours when resolved_at is set. Fired by BEFORE UPDATE trigger on sla_breaches.';


--
-- Name: process_all_pending_events_v2(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.process_all_pending_events_v2() RETURNS TABLE(processed_count bigint, failed_count bigint)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_event_record RECORD;
    v_processed_count BIGINT := 0;
    v_failed_count BIGINT := 0;
    v_max_retries INT := 3;
    v_batch_size INT := 100;
BEGIN
    FOR v_event_record IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_events
        WHERE (status = 'pending' OR (status = 'failed' AND retry_count < v_max_retries))
        ORDER BY created_at ASC
        LIMIT v_batch_size
        FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET status = 'processing', processing_started_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            -- ▼▼▼ THE FIX IS HERE ▼▼▼
            PERFORM automation.process_event_queue_v5(v_event_record.id); -- Calling v5, not v4
            -- ▲▲▲ END OF FIX ▲▲▲

            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET status = 'processed', processed_at = NOW(), error_message = NULL
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            v_processed_count := v_processed_count + 1;
            
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
            
            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET 
                status = CASE 
                    WHEN COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1 >= v_max_retries 
                    THEN 'permanently_failed' 
                    ELSE 'failed' 
                END,
                retry_count = COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1,
                error_message = SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000)
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to process event % (attempt %): %', 
                         v_event_record.id, 
                         COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1,
                         SQLERRM;
        END;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN QUERY SELECT v_processed_count, v_failed_count;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: process_event_queue_v5(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.process_event_queue_v5(p_event_id uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_event RECORD;
    v_rule RECORD;
    v_action RECORD;
    v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_dispatch_error TEXT;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_duration_ms INT;
    v_event_blueprint_id UUID;
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_esm_instance RECORD;
    v_data_context JSONB;
BEGIN
    RAISE NOTICE '[queue-v5] 0. PROCESSING EVENT %', p_event_id;

    SELECT * INTO v_event
    FROM automation.wf_events
    WHERE id = p_event_id
    FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN 
        RAISE WARNING '[queue-v5] 1. Event % not found or already locked.', p_event_id;
        RETURN; 
    END IF;

    v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
    IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
        v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'old'->>'id')::uuid;
    END IF;

    IF v_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        BEGIN
            SELECT * INTO v_esm_instance
            FROM automation.esm_instances esm
            WHERE esm.entity_id = v_entity_id;
            
            v_event_blueprint_id := v_esm_instance.blueprint_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING '[queue-v5] 3. FAILED to get blueprint/esm instance: %', SQLERRM;
        END;
    END IF;

    v_data_context := jsonb_build_object(
        'new', v_event.trigger_data->'new',
        'old', v_event.trigger_data->'old',
        'esm', to_jsonb(v_esm_instance)
    );

    FOR v_rule IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_rules
        WHERE organization_id = v_event.organization_id
          AND trigger_schema = v_event.trigger_schema
          AND trigger_table = v_event.trigger_table
          AND trigger_type = v_event.trigger_type
          AND ( (v_event_blueprint_id IS NULL AND blueprint_id IS NULL) OR (blueprint_id = v_event_blueprint_id) )
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
    LOOP
        v_conditions_met := FALSE;
        v_start_time := clock_timestamp();

        BEGIN
            IF v_rule.conditions IS NULL OR v_rule.conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR (v_rule.conditions->'rules') IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_rule.conditions->'rules') = 0 THEN
                v_conditions_met := TRUE;
            ELSIF v_rule.condition_type = 'jsonb' OR v_rule.condition_type IS NULL THEN
                v_conditions_met := automation._util_evaluate_jsonb_condition_v2(v_rule.conditions, v_data_context);
            ELSE
                RAISE WARNING '[queue-v5] 4a. Unsupported condition_type "%"', v_rule.condition_type;
                v_conditions_met := FALSE;
            END IF;
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
            v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        IF v_conditions_met THEN
            FOR v_action IN
                SELECT * FROM automation.wf_actions
                WHERE rule_id = v_rule.id AND is_enabled = TRUE
                ORDER BY action_order ASC, created_at ASC
            LOOP
                v_action_conditions_met := TRUE; -- Default to true if no conditions
                
                -- Evaluate action-level conditions if they exist
                IF v_action.conditions IS NOT NULL AND v_action.conditions != '{}'::jsonb THEN
                    BEGIN
                        IF v_action.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
                            v_action_conditions_met := automation._util_evaluate_jsonb_condition_v2(v_action.conditions, v_data_context);
                        ELSE
                            RAISE WARNING '[queue-v5] 5a. Unsupported ACTION condition_type "%" for action %', v_action.condition_type, v_action.id;
                            v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        END IF;
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        RAISE WARNING '[queue-v5] 5b. FAILED to evaluate action condition for action %: %', v_action.id, SQLERRM;
                        v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                    END;
                END IF;

                IF v_action_conditions_met THEN
                    BEGIN
                        PERFORM automation.dispatch_action(
                            p_action_id => v_action.id,
                            p_trigger_data => v_event.trigger_data, 
                            p_context => jsonb_build_object(
                                'event_id', v_event.id,
                                'rule_id', v_rule.id,
                                'organization_id', v_event.organization_id,
                                'trigger_schema', v_event.trigger_schema,
                                'trigger_table', v_event.trigger_table
                            ) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
                        );
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                        v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
                        RAISE; 
                    END;
                ELSE
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, context, log_level)
                    VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_rule.id, v_action.id, v_event.organization_id, 'skipped', 'Action skipped, condition not met.', v_action.conditions, 'info');
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF v_any_action_failed THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed for event %. Last error: %', p_event_id, v_dispatch_error;
    END IF;

END;
$$;


--
-- Name: process_event_trigger(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.process_event_trigger() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
  PERFORM automation.process_event_queue(NEW.id);
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_approval_orchestrator(uuid, uuid, jsonb, text, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_approval_orchestrator(p_event_id uuid, p_esm_instance_id uuid, p_new_data jsonb, p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
 DECLARE
     v_definition jsonb;
     v_current_stage_id text;
     v_stage_config jsonb;
     v_approval_rules jsonb;
     v_phase_idx int;
     v_approvers jsonb;
     v_approver_rec jsonb;
     v_task_name text;
     v_manager_id uuid;
     v_entity_name text;
     v_is_unified boolean;
 BEGIN
     -- 1. Tier Identification (Transactional vs Unified)
     -- Check if parent entity exists in Unified Tier
