     SELECT EXISTS (
         SELECT 1 FROM core.unified_objects 
         WHERE id = (p_new_data->>'id')::uuid
     ) INTO v_is_unified;

     IF NOT v_is_unified THEN
         -- ARCHITECT VERDICT: Skip Task creation for Transactional entities
         -- We log this intent to wf_logs instead of creating a unified.task
         INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
         VALUES (
             gen_random_uuid(), 
             p_event_id, 
             (p_new_data->>'organization_id')::uuid, 
             'info', 
             'info', 
             'Transactional Approval Context (Skipped Task Creation)', 
             jsonb_build_object(
                 'entity_type', p_entity_type,
                 'entity_id', p_new_data->>'id',
                 'reason', 'Entity is not registered in core.unified_objects. ESM state transition only.'
             ), 
             clock_timestamp()
         );
         RETURN;
     END IF;

     -- 2. Get Definition and Stage
     SELECT d.definitions, i.stage_id
     INTO v_definition, v_current_stage_id
     FROM automation.esm_instances i
     JOIN automation.esm_definitions d ON i.esm_definition_id = d.id
     WHERE i.id = p_esm_instance_id;

     -- 3. Extract Stage Config
     SELECT s INTO v_stage_config
     FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_definition->'stages') s
     WHERE s->>'id' = v_current_stage_id;

     v_approval_rules := v_stage_config->'approval_rules';

     -- IF NO APPROVAL RULES, EXIT
     IF v_approval_rules IS NULL THEN
         RETURN;
     END IF;

     -- 4. Idempotency Check
     IF EXISTS (
         SELECT 1 FROM blueprint.tasks 
         WHERE parent_entity_id = (p_new_data->>'id')::uuid 
           AND parent_entity_type = p_entity_type
           AND task_category = 'APPROVAL' 
           AND stage_id NOT IN ('Completed', 'Cancelled', 'Rejected') 
           AND (details->>'workflow_stage_id') = v_current_stage_id
     ) THEN
         RETURN; 
     END IF;

     -- 5. Start Phase 0 (Simplified)
     v_phase_idx := 0;
     v_approvers := v_approval_rules->'phases'->v_phase_idx->'approvers';
     
     -- 6. Loop through approvers and create Tasks
     FOR v_approver_rec IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_approvers)
     LOOP
         v_entity_name := COALESCE(p_new_data->>'display_id', 'Entity');
         v_task_name := 'Approval Required: ' || v_current_stage_id || ' for ' || v_entity_name;

         v_manager_id := (p_new_data->>'created_by')::uuid; 

         INSERT INTO blueprint.tasks (
             organization_id,
             name,
             description,
             parent_entity_schema, 
             parent_entity_type,   
             parent_entity_id,     
             task_category,        
             assignee_id,          
             stage_id,             
             priority_id,          
             details,
             created_by
         )
         VALUES (
             (p_new_data->>'organization_id')::uuid,
             v_task_name,
             'System generated approval task. Please review the parent entity.',
             p_entity_schema, 
             p_entity_type,   
             (p_new_data->>'id')::uuid,
             'APPROVAL',
             v_manager_id,
             'To_Do', 
             NULL,
             jsonb_build_object(
                 'approval_phase', v_phase_idx,
                 'workflow_stage_id', v_current_stage_id,
                 'approver_def', v_approver_rec
             ),
             (p_new_data->>'created_by')::uuid 
         );
         
         RAISE NOTICE 'Approval Task Created: % for Manager %', v_task_name, v_manager_id;
     END LOOP;
 END;
 $$;


--
-- Name: run_core_execute_rules_scheduled(uuid[], jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_core_execute_rules_scheduled(p_rule_ids uuid[], p_data_context jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_additional_context jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_rule RECORD;
    v_action RECORD;
    v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_dispatch_error TEXT;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_duration_ms INT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Shared rule execution engine used by both event and scheduled processing
    -- Preserves: Rule evaluation and action execution logic from both processing paths
    -- Enables: Code reuse between real-time events and scheduled jobs
    
    RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_execute_rules] Executing % rules', array_length(p_rule_ids, 1);

    -- Process each rule
    FOR v_rule IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_rules
        WHERE id = ANY(p_rule_ids)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
    LOOP
        v_conditions_met := FALSE;
        v_start_time := clock_timestamp();

        BEGIN
            -- Evaluate rule conditions using shared utility
            IF v_rule.conditions IS NULL OR v_rule.conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR (v_rule.conditions->'rules') IS NULL THEN
                v_conditions_met := TRUE;
            ELSIF v_rule.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
                v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(
                    v_rule.conditions, 
                    p_trigger_data, 
                    p_data_context->'esm'
                );
            ELSE
                RAISE WARNING '[run_core_execute_rules] Unsupported condition_type "%"', v_rule.condition_type;
                v_conditions_met := FALSE;
            END IF;
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
            v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        -- Execute actions if conditions met
        IF v_conditions_met THEN
            FOR v_action IN
                SELECT * FROM automation.wf_actions
                WHERE rule_id = v_rule.id AND is_enabled = TRUE
                ORDER BY action_order ASC, created_at ASC
            LOOP
                v_action_conditions_met := TRUE;
                
                -- Evaluate action-level conditions
                IF v_action.conditions IS NOT NULL AND v_action.conditions != '{}'::jsonb THEN
                    BEGIN
                        IF v_action.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
                            v_action_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(
                                v_action.conditions, 
                                p_trigger_data, 
                                p_data_context->'esm'
                            );
                        ELSE
                            RAISE WARNING '[run_core_execute_rules] Unsupported ACTION condition_type "%" for action %', v_action.condition_type, v_action.id;
                            v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        END IF;
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        RAISE WARNING '[run_core_execute_rules] FAILED to evaluate action condition for action %: %', v_action.id, SQLERRM;
                        v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                    END;
                END IF;

                IF v_action_conditions_met THEN
                    BEGIN
                        -- Dispatch action with combined context
                        PERFORM automation.run_dispatch_action_optimized(
                            v_action.id,
                            p_trigger_data,
                            p_data_context || jsonb_build_object(
                                'rule_id', v_rule.id,
                                'trigger_schema', v_rule.trigger_schema,
                                'trigger_table', v_rule.trigger_table
                            ) || COALESCE(p_additional_context, '{}'::jsonb) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
                        );
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                        v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
                        RAISE; 
                    END;
                ELSE
                    -- Log skipped action
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (
                        id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, context, log_level
                    ) VALUES (
                        gen_random_uuid(), v_rule.id, v_action.id, 
                        (p_data_context->>'organization_id')::uuid,
                        'skipped', 'Action skipped, condition not met.', 
                        v_action.conditions, 'info'
                    );
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    -- Final error handling
    IF v_any_action_failed THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed in rule execution. Last error: %', v_dispatch_error;
    END IF;

END;$$;


--
-- Name: run_core_process_event(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_core_process_event(p_event_id uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_event RECORD;
    v_rule RECORD;
    v_action RECORD;
    v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_dispatch_error TEXT;
    v_esm_instance_json JSONB;
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_event_blueprint_id UUID;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_esm_instance RECORD;
BEGIN
    SELECT * INTO v_event FROM automation.wf_events WHERE id = p_event_id;
    IF NOT FOUND THEN RETURN; END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_process_event] Processing event %', p_event_id;

    -- 1. Extract entity_id
    v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
    IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
        v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'old'->>'id')::uuid;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Get ESM instance safely (Orphan Handling)
    IF v_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT * INTO v_esm_instance
        FROM automation.esm_instances esm
        WHERE esm.entity_id = v_entity_id
        ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;
        
        IF FOUND THEN
            v_event_blueprint_id := v_esm_instance.blueprint_id;
            v_esm_instance_json := to_jsonb(v_esm_instance);
        ELSE
            -- Log Orphan Event
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, organization_id, status, message, log_level)
            VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_event.organization_id, 'info', 'Orphan event: No ESM instance found for entity ' || v_entity_id, 'warning');
            v_esm_instance_json := '{}'::jsonb;
        END IF;
    ELSE
        v_esm_instance_json := '{}'::jsonb;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Find and execute rules
    FOR v_rule IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_rules
        WHERE (organization_id IS NULL OR organization_id = v_event.organization_id) -- FIXED: Global Rule Match
          AND trigger_schema = v_event.trigger_schema
          AND trigger_table = v_event.trigger_table
          AND trigger_type = v_event.trigger_type
          -- Match specific blueprint if ESM exists, or global rules if not
          AND ( (v_event_blueprint_id IS NULL AND blueprint_id IS NULL) OR (blueprint_id = v_event_blueprint_id) )
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
    LOOP
        v_conditions_met := FALSE;
        
        -- Use shared condition evaluation
        IF v_rule.conditions IS NULL OR v_rule.conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR (v_rule.conditions->'rules') IS NULL THEN
            v_conditions_met := TRUE;
        ELSIF COALESCE(v_rule.condition_type, 'jsonb') = 'jsonb' THEN
            v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_rule.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, v_esm_instance_json);
        ELSE
            RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported condition_type %', v_rule.condition_type;
        END IF;

        IF v_conditions_met THEN
            -- Process actions
            FOR v_action IN 
                SELECT * FROM automation.wf_actions WHERE rule_id = v_rule.id ORDER BY created_at ASC
            LOOP
                -- Log Action Start
                INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, executed_at)
                VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_rule.id, v_action.id, v_event.organization_id, 'info', 'Action found, about to dispatch', clock_timestamp());

                BEGIN
                    PERFORM automation.run_dispatch_action_optimized(
                        v_action.id,
                        v_event.trigger_data,
                        automation.run_helper_build_context(
                            v_event.trigger_data,
                            v_esm_instance_json,
                            v_entity_id,
                            v_event.organization_id
                        ) || jsonb_build_object(
                            'event_id', v_event.id,
                            'rule_id', v_rule.id,
                            'organization_id', v_event.organization_id,
                            'trigger_schema', v_event.trigger_schema,
                            'trigger_table', v_event.trigger_table
                        ) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
                    );
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                    v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
                    RAISE WARNING 'Action % failed for event %: %', v_action.id, v_event.id, v_dispatch_error;
                END;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF v_any_action_failed THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed for event %. Last error: %', p_event_id, v_dispatch_error;
    END IF;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_dispatch_action_optimized(uuid, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_dispatch_action_optimized(p_action_id uuid, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_action RECORD;
    v_action_result_context JSONB;
    v_error_message TEXT;
    v_error_context TEXT;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_duration_ms INT;
    v_event_id UUID := (p_context->>'event_id')::uuid;
    v_rule_id UUID := (p_context->>'rule_id')::uuid;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
    v_smart_action_context JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Optimized action dispatcher with integrated preprocessing
    -- Preserves: All original dispatch_action functionality
    -- Enhances: Integrated context building and error handling
    
    -- 1. Fetch action (original logic)
    SELECT * INTO v_action FROM automation.wf_actions WHERE id = p_action_id;
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE WARNING '[run_dispatch_action_optimized] Action ID % not found. Skipping.', p_action_id;
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, status, log_level, message, context)
        VALUES (v_organization_id, v_event_id, v_rule_id, p_action_id, 'skipped', 'warning', 'Action ID not found.', p_context);
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Check if enabled (original logic)
    IF NOT v_action.is_enabled THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, status, log_level, message, context)
        VALUES (v_organization_id, v_event_id, v_rule_id, p_action_id, 'skipped', 'info', 'Action is disabled.', p_context);
        RETURN;
    END IF;

    v_start_time := clock_timestamp();

    -- 3. Execute action with enhanced context
    BEGIN
        -- Enhanced context with action_id (original fix preserved)
        v_smart_action_context := p_context || jsonb_build_object('action_id', p_action_id);

        -- Route to appropriate smart action
        CASE v_action.action_type
            WHEN 'send_email' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_send_email(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'create_entity' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_create_entity(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'update_entity' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_update_entity(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'assign_owner' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_assign_owner(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'create_activity' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_create_activity(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'create_task' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_create_task(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            WHEN 'manage_tags' THEN
                v_action_result_context := automation.run_smart_manage_tags(v_action.configuration, p_trigger_data, v_smart_action_context);
            ELSE
                RAISE WARNING '[run_dispatch_action_optimized] Unknown action_type "%" for action ID %.', v_action.action_type, v_action.id;
                INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, status, log_level, message, context)
                VALUES (v_organization_id, v_event_id, v_rule_id, p_action_id, 'skipped', 'warning', 'Unknown action_type: ' || v_action.action_type, p_context);
                RETURN;
        END CASE;

        v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));

        -- 4. Log success (original logic)
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (
            organization_id, event_id, rule_id, action_id,
            status, log_level, duration_ms, context, message
        ) VALUES (
            v_organization_id, v_event_id, v_rule_id, v_action.id,
            'success', 'info', v_duration_ms,
            jsonb_build_object('action_name', v_action.name, 'action_type', v_action.action_type) || COALESCE(v_action_result_context, '{}'::jsonb),
            'Action executed successfully.'
        );

    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
        GET STACKED DIAGNOSTICS v_error_message = MESSAGE_TEXT, v_error_context = PG_EXCEPTION_CONTEXT;
        v_error_message := format('Action "%s" (%s) failed. Message: %s | Context: %s', v_action.name, v_action.action_type, v_error_message, v_error_context);

        -- 5. Log failure (original logic)
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (
            organization_id, event_id, rule_id, action_id,
            status, log_level, error_details, duration_ms, context
        ) VALUES (
            v_organization_id, v_event_id, v_rule_id, v_action.id,
            'failed', 'error', v_error_message, v_duration_ms,
            jsonb_build_object('action_name', v_action.name, 'action_type', v_action.action_type)
        );
        RAISE EXCEPTION '%', v_error_message;
    END;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: run_helper_build_context(jsonb, jsonb, uuid, uuid, boolean); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_helper_build_context(p_trigger_data jsonb, p_esm_data jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb, p_entity_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_include_flat boolean DEFAULT false) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_context JSONB;
    v_flat_data JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Unified data context builder used across all processing paths
    -- Replaces: Duplicated context building in process_event_queue_v5, process_scheduled_jobs_v4
    -- Preserves: All original data context structures
    
    -- Build base context (original logic from multiple locations)
    v_context := jsonb_build_object(
        'new', p_trigger_data->'new',
        'old', p_trigger_data->'old',
        'esm', p_esm_data
    );
    
    -- Add entity_id if provided (for scheduled jobs context)
    IF p_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_context := v_context || jsonb_build_object('entity_id', p_entity_id);
    END IF;
    
    -- Add organization_id if provided
    IF p_organization_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_context := v_context || jsonb_build_object('organization_id', p_organization_id);
    END IF;
    
    -- Include flattened data if requested (for efficient placeholder resolution)
    IF p_include_flat THEN
        v_flat_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(v_context);
        v_context := v_context || jsonb_build_object('_flat', v_flat_data);
    END IF;
    
    RETURN v_context;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_helper_build_sql(text, text, text, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_helper_build_sql(p_operation text, p_schema_name text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_table_name text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_data jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb, p_conditions jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS text
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
 DECLARE
     v_columns text;
     v_values text;
     v_set_clause text;
     v_where_clause text;
     v_sql text;
 BEGIN
     IF p_operation = 'INSERT' THEN
         -- Exclude columns that are GENERATED ALWAYS
         SELECT 
             string_agg(format('%I', key), ', '), 
             string_agg(format('%L', value #>> '{}'), ', ')
         INTO v_columns, v_values
         FROM jsonb_each(p_data)
         WHERE key NOT IN (
             SELECT column_name 
             FROM information_schema.columns 
             WHERE table_schema = p_schema_name 
               AND table_name = p_table_name 
               AND is_generated = 'ALWAYS'
         );

         IF v_columns IS NULL OR v_columns = '' THEN
             RETURN NULL;
         END IF;

         v_sql := format('INSERT INTO %I.%I (%s) VALUES (%s)', 
             p_schema_name, p_table_name, v_columns, v_values);

     ELSIF p_operation = 'UPDATE' THEN
         -- Exclude columns that are GENERATED ALWAYS
         SELECT string_agg(format('%I = %L', key, value #>> '{}'), ', ')
         INTO v_set_clause
         FROM jsonb_each(p_data)
         WHERE key NOT IN (
             SELECT column_name 
             FROM information_schema.columns 
             WHERE table_schema = p_schema_name 
               AND table_name = p_table_name 
               AND is_generated = 'ALWAYS'
         );

         IF v_set_clause IS NULL OR v_set_clause = '' THEN
             RETURN NULL;
         END IF;

         -- Generate WHERE clause if conditions provided
         IF p_conditions IS NOT NULL AND jsonb_typeof(p_conditions) = 'object' AND p_conditions != '{}'::jsonb THEN
             v_where_clause := automation.run_helper_build_sql('WHERE', p_conditions => p_conditions);
         END IF;

         IF v_where_clause IS NULL OR v_where_clause = '' THEN
             RAISE EXCEPTION 'UPDATE operation requires WHERE clause conditions';
         END IF;

         v_sql := format('UPDATE %I.%I SET %s WHERE %s', 
             p_schema_name, p_table_name, v_set_clause, v_where_clause);

     ELSIF p_operation = 'WHERE' THEN
         -- Generate WHERE clause (original _util_jsonb_to_where_clause logic)
         IF p_conditions IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_conditions) != 'object' THEN
             RETURN '';
         END IF;

         SELECT string_agg(
             CASE 
                 WHEN jsonb_typeof(value) = 'null' THEN format('%I IS NULL', key)
                 ELSE format('%I = %L', key, value #>> '{}')
             END, ' AND ')
         INTO v_where_clause
         FROM jsonb_each(p_conditions);

         v_sql := COALESCE(v_where_clause, '');

     ELSE
         RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unsupported operation: %', p_operation;
     END IF;

     RETURN v_sql;
 END;
 $$;


--
-- Name: run_helper_get_entity_scheduled(text, text, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_helper_get_entity_scheduled(p_entity_schema text, p_entity_type text, p_entity_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$DECLARE
    v_result JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Simple entity data fetcher - replaces _get_entity_data
    -- Preserves: All original _get_entity_data functionality
    
    EXECUTE format('SELECT to_jsonb(t.*) FROM %I.%I t WHERE t.id = $1', 
                   p_entity_schema, p_entity_type)
    INTO v_result
    USING p_entity_id;
    
    RETURN v_result;
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    RAISE WARNING '[run_helper_get_entity] Failed to fetch entity data for %.% (ID: %): %', 
                  p_entity_schema, p_entity_type, p_entity_id, SQLERRM;
    RETURN NULL;
END;$_$;


--
-- Name: run_main_process_events(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_main_process_events() RETURNS TABLE(processed_count bigint, failed_count bigint)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_event_record RECORD;
    v_processed_count BIGINT := 0;
    v_failed_count BIGINT := 0;
    v_max_retries INT := 3;
    v_batch_size INT := 100;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Main entry point for processing pending events
    -- Preserves: All original process_all_pending_events_v2 functionality
    
    FOR v_event_record IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_events
        WHERE (status = 'pending' OR (status = 'failed' AND retry_count < v_max_retries))
        ORDER BY created_at ASC
        LIMIT v_batch_size
        FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
    LOOP
        BEGIN
            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET status = 'processing', processing_started_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            -- Process single event using core processor
            PERFORM automation.run_core_process_event(v_event_record.id);

            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET status = 'processed', processed_at = NOW(), error_message = NULL
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            v_processed_count := v_processed_count + 1;
            
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
            
            UPDATE automation.wf_events
            SET 
                status = CASE 
                    WHEN COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1 >= v_max_retries 
                    THEN 'permanently_failed' 
                    ELSE 'failed' 
                END,
                retry_count = COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1,
                error_message = SUBSTR(SQLERRM, 1, 1000)
            WHERE id = v_event_record.id;
            
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to process event % (attempt %): %', 
                         v_event_record.id, 
                         COALESCE(v_event_record.retry_count, 0) + 1,
                         SQLERRM;
        END;
    END LOOP;

    RETURN QUERY SELECT v_processed_count, v_failed_count;
END;
$$;

--           AND esm.entity_type = ANY(v_entity_tables)
--           AND entity_data IS NOT NULL;
        
--         GET DIAGNOSTICS v_snapshot_count = ROW_COUNT;

--     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--         RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] CRITICAL: Failed to build entity snapshot: %', SQLERRM;
--         RETURN 'Scheduled jobs processed. Failures: 1 (Snapshot Failed)';
--     END;

--     -- Step 2: Loop through due jobs (original logic)
--     FOR v_job IN

--           AND is_active = true

--     LOOP
--         v_start_time := clock_timestamp();
--         v_breaching_entity_ids := ARRAY[]::UUID[];
--         v_entities_found := 0;
        
--         -- Get the rule for this job (original logic)
--         SELECT * INTO v_rule FROM automation.wf_rules
--         WHERE id = v_job.rule_id AND is_active = TRUE AND condition_type = 'jsonb';

--         IF NOT FOUND THEN
--             v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
--             UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Rule not found, inactive, or not jsonb' WHERE id = v_job.id;
--             RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Job % failed: Rule % is not found or inactive.', v_job.id, v_job.rule_id;
--             CONTINUE;
--         END IF;

--         -- Extract rule logic (original logic)
--         BEGIN
--             v_monitored_stages := ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(v_rule.conditions->'monitored_stages'));
--             v_base_conditions := v_rule.conditions->'base_conditions';
--             v_time_threshold_hours := (v_rule.conditions->>'time_threshold_hours')::NUMERIC;
            
--             IF v_monitored_stages IS NULL OR v_base_conditions IS NULL OR v_time_threshold_hours IS NULL THEN
--                 RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid rule.conditions format.';
--             END IF;
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
--             UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Invalid rule conditions JSON: ' || SQLERRM WHERE id = v_job.id;
--             CONTINUE;
--         END;

--         -- Step 3: Evaluate entities against conditions using shared rule engine
--         RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule % (BP %): Evaluating against snapshot.', v_rule.id, v_rule.blueprint_id;
--         BEGIN
--             FOR v_loop_record IN SELECT * FROM entity_snapshot_temp
--             LOOP
--                 -- Sunset logic with blueprint version locking (original logic)
--                 IF v_loop_record.entity_schema = v_rule.trigger_schema AND
--                    v_loop_record.entity_type = v_rule.trigger_table AND
--                    v_loop_record.stage_id = ANY(v_monitored_stages) AND
--                    (v_loop_record.esm_data->>'blueprint_id')::uuid = v_rule.blueprint_id
--                 THEN
--                     v_entity_jsonb := v_loop_record.entity_data;
--                     v_esm_jsonb := v_loop_record.esm_data;
                
--                     -- Use shared context builder
--                     v_data_context := automation.run_helper_build_context(
--                         jsonb_build_object('new', v_entity_jsonb, 'old', null),
--                         v_esm_jsonb
--                     );
                    
--                     -- Time breach calculation (original logic)
--                     v_is_time_breached := FALSE;
--                     IF v_time_threshold_hours > 0 THEN
--                         v_is_time_breached := v_loop_record.created_at < (NOW() - (v_time_threshold_hours || ' hours')::INTERVAL);
--                     ELSE
--                         v_is_time_breached := TRUE;
--                     END IF;

--                     -- Use shared condition evaluation
--                     v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_base_conditions, v_data_context, v_esm_jsonb);
                    
--                     IF v_is_time_breached AND v_conditions_met THEN
--                         v_breaching_entity_ids := v_breaching_entity_ids || v_loop_record.entity_id;
--                     END IF;
--                 END IF;
--             END LOOP;
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
--             RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule % evaluation failed: %', v_rule.id, SQLERRM;
--             UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Evaluation loop error: ' || SQLERRM WHERE id = v_job.id;
--             CONTINUE;
--         END;

--         v_entities_found := COALESCE(array_length(v_breaching_entity_ids, 1), 0);
--         RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule %: Found % breaching entities.', v_rule.id, v_entities_found;

--         -- Step 4: Process actions using shared rule execution
--         IF v_entities_found > 0 THEN
--             INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, scheduled_job_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
--             VALUES (v_job.organization_id, v_job.id, 'info', 'info', 'Starting scheduled job execution', jsonb_build_object('job_type', v_job.job_type, 'start_time', v_start_time), v_start_time)
--             RETURNING id INTO v_job_log_id;

--             INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, scheduled_job_id, rule_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
--             VALUES (v_job.organization_id, v_job.id, v_rule.id, 'success', 'info', format('Rule evaluation complete - %s entities found', v_entities_found), jsonb_build_object('rule_name', v_rule.name, 'entities_found', v_entities_found, 'entity_ids', to_jsonb(v_breaching_entity_ids)), clock_timestamp())
--             RETURNING id INTO v_rule_log_id;

--             FOREACH v_entity_id IN ARRAY v_breaching_entity_ids
--             LOOP
--                 -- Refetch fresh entity data (original logic)
--                 SELECT automation.run_helper_get_entity_scheduled(v_rule.trigger_schema, v_rule.trigger_table, v_entity_id)
--                 INTO v_entity_jsonb;

--                 IF v_entity_jsonb IS NULL THEN
--                     RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Could not re-fetch entity % during action loop. Skipping.', v_entity_id;
--                     CONTINUE;
--                 END IF;
                
--                 v_trigger_data_for_action := jsonb_build_object('new', v_entity_jsonb, 'old', null);

--                 -- Execute actions using shared rule execution engine
--                 PERFORM automation.run_core_execute_rules_scheduled(
--                     ARRAY[v_rule.id],
--                     automation.run_helper_build_context(v_trigger_data_for_action, NULL, v_entity_id, v_job.organization_id),
--                     v_trigger_data_for_action,
--                     jsonb_build_object('scheduled_job_id', v_job.id, 'sla_rule_id', v_rule.metadata->>'blueprint_sla_rule_id')
--                 );
                
--                 -- Record SLA breach (original logic)
--                 INSERT INTO automation.sla_breaches (organization_id, esm_instance_id, sla_rule_id, sla_rule_name, metadata)
--                 SELECT v_job.organization_id, id, v_rule.metadata->>'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_rule.name, v_rule.metadata
--                 FROM automation.esm_instances WHERE entity_id = v_entity_id
--                 ON CONFLICT (esm_instance_id, sla_rule_id) DO NOTHING;
                
--             END LOOP;
            
--             UPDATE automation.wf_logs SET
--                 status = 'success',
--                 message = format('Job completed. Processed actions for %s entities.', v_entities_found),
--                 duration_ms = EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time)),
--                 context = context || jsonb_build_object('end_time', clock_timestamp(), 'entities_processed', v_entities_found)
--             WHERE id = v_job_log_id;

--         END IF;

--         -- Step 5: Reschedule the job (original logic)
--         BEGIN
--             SELECT automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('NEXT_RUN', v_job.cron_expression, NOW()) INTO v_next_run;
--             UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
--             SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = v_next_run, status = 'pending', error_message = NULL, updated_at = NOW()
--             WHERE id = v_job.id;
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
--             SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = NOW() + interval '1 hour', status = 'pending', error_message = 'Cron parsing failed, using 1h fallback: ' || SQLERRM, updated_at = NOW()
--             WHERE id = v_job.id;
--         END;

--     END LOOP;
    
--     RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Finished processing. Actions run. Failures: %.', v_failed_count;
--     RETURN format('Scheduled jobs processed. Entities actioned. Failures: %s.', v_failed_count);
-- END;
DECLARE
    v_job RECORD;
    v_rule RECORD;
    v_loop_record RECORD;
    v_entity_jsonb JSONB;
    v_esm_jsonb JSONB;
    v_data_context JSONB;
    v_base_conditions JSONB;
    v_monitored_stages TEXT[];
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_time_threshold_hours NUMERIC;
    v_is_time_breached BOOLEAN;
    v_action RECORD;
    v_trigger_data_for_action JSONB;
    v_context_for_action JSONB;
    v_breaching_entity_ids UUID[] := ARRAY[]::UUID[];
    v_entities_found INT := 0;
    v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_processed_count INT := 0;
    v_failed_count INT := 0;
    v_next_run TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_job_log_id UUID;
    v_rule_log_id UUID;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_snapshot_count INT := 0;
    v_entity_tables TEXT[];
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Main entry point for processing scheduled jobs
    -- Preserves: All original process_scheduled_jobs_v4 functionality
    
    RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Starting scheduled job processing';
    
    -- Step 1: Create temp table for entity snapshot (original logic)
    CREATE TEMP TABLE IF NOT EXISTS entity_snapshot_temp (
        entity_id UUID,
        entity_schema TEXT,
        entity_type TEXT, 
        stage_id TEXT,
        created_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
        esm_updated_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
        entity_data JSONB,
        esm_data JSONB
    ) ON COMMIT DROP;

    -- Step 1a: Get distinct entity types (original logic)
    SELECT array_agg(DISTINCT esm.entity_type)
    INTO v_entity_tables
    FROM automation.esm_instances esm
    WHERE esm.status = 'Active'
      AND esm.entity_type IN (
        SELECT r.trigger_table 
        FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs j
        JOIN automation.wf_rules r ON j.rule_id = r.id
        WHERE j.status = 'pending' AND j.next_run <= NOW()
          AND j.is_active = true
          AND r.is_active = true
      );

    -- If no entities match, reschedule and exit (original logic)
    IF v_entity_tables IS NULL OR array_length(v_entity_tables, 1) = 0 THEN
        RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] No candidate entities found. Rescheduling jobs.';
        FOR v_job IN
            SELECT * FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
            WHERE status = 'pending' AND next_run <= NOW()
              AND is_active = true
            FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
        LOOP
            BEGIN
                SELECT automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('NEXT_RUN', v_job.cron_expression, NOW()) INTO v_next_run;
                UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
                SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = v_next_run, status = 'pending', error_message = NULL, updated_at = NOW()
                WHERE id = v_job.id;
            EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
                SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = NOW() + interval '1 hour', status = 'pending', error_message = 'Cron parsing failed, using 1h fallback: ' || SQLERRM, updated_at = NOW()
                WHERE id = v_job.id;
            END;
        END LOOP;
        RETURN 'Scheduled jobs processed. Entities actioned: 0. Failures: 0.';
    END IF;

    -- Step 1b: Build dynamic snapshot (original logic)
    BEGIN
        TRUNCATE entity_snapshot_temp;
        
        INSERT INTO entity_snapshot_temp (
            entity_id, entity_schema, entity_type, stage_id, 
            created_at, esm_updated_at, entity_data, esm_data
        )
        SELECT 
            esm.entity_id,
            esm.entity_schema,
            esm.entity_type,
            esm.stage_id,
            -- FIX 1: Safely handle missing/invalid created_at from entity_data JSON
            COALESCE(
                NULLIF( (entity_data->>'created_at'), '' )::TIMESTAMPTZ, 
                esm.updated_at
            ),
            esm.updated_at,
            entity_data,
            to_jsonb(esm.*)
        FROM automation.esm_instances esm
        CROSS JOIN LATERAL (
            -- FIX 2: Use the corrected function name
            SELECT automation.run_helper_get_entity_scheduled(esm.entity_schema, esm.entity_type, esm.entity_id) AS entity_data
        ) data
        WHERE esm.status = 'Active'
          AND esm.entity_type = ANY(v_entity_tables)
          AND entity_data IS NOT NULL;
        
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_snapshot_count = ROW_COUNT;
        RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Snapshot created with % entities from % table types', 
                     v_snapshot_count, array_length(v_entity_tables, 1);

    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] CRITICAL: Failed to build entity snapshot: %', SQLERRM;
        RETURN 'Scheduled jobs processed. Failures: 1 (Snapshot Failed)';
    END;

    -- Step 2: Loop through due jobs (original logic)
    FOR v_job IN
        SELECT * FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
        WHERE status = 'pending' AND next_run <= NOW()
          AND is_active = true
        FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED
    LOOP
        v_start_time := clock_timestamp();
        v_breaching_entity_ids := ARRAY[]::UUID[];
        v_entities_found := 0;
        
        -- Get the rule for this job (original logic)
        SELECT * INTO v_rule FROM automation.wf_rules
        WHERE id = v_job.rule_id AND is_active = TRUE AND condition_type = 'jsonb';

        IF NOT FOUND THEN
            v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
            UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Rule not found, inactive, or not jsonb' WHERE id = v_job.id;
            RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Job % failed: Rule % is not found or inactive.', v_job.id, v_job.rule_id;
            CONTINUE;
        END IF;

        -- Extract rule logic (original logic)
        BEGIN
            v_monitored_stages := ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(v_rule.conditions->'monitored_stages'));
            v_base_conditions := v_rule.conditions->'base_conditions';
            v_time_threshold_hours := (v_rule.conditions->>'time_threshold_hours')::NUMERIC;
            
            IF v_monitored_stages IS NULL OR v_base_conditions IS NULL OR v_time_threshold_hours IS NULL THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid rule.conditions format.';
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
            UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Invalid rule conditions JSON: ' || SQLERRM WHERE id = v_job.id;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        -- Step 3: Evaluate entities against conditions using shared rule engine
        RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule % (BP %): Evaluating against snapshot.', v_rule.id, v_rule.blueprint_id;
        BEGIN
            FOR v_loop_record IN SELECT * FROM entity_snapshot_temp
            LOOP
                -- Sunset logic with blueprint version locking (original logic)
                IF v_loop_record.entity_schema = v_rule.trigger_schema AND
                   v_loop_record.entity_type = v_rule.trigger_table AND
                   v_loop_record.stage_id = ANY(v_monitored_stages) AND
                   (v_loop_record.esm_data->>'blueprint_id')::uuid = v_rule.blueprint_id
                THEN
                    v_entity_jsonb := v_loop_record.entity_data;
                    v_esm_jsonb := v_loop_record.esm_data;
                
                    -- Use shared context builder
                    v_data_context := automation.run_helper_build_context(
                        jsonb_build_object('new', v_entity_jsonb, 'old', null),
                        v_esm_jsonb
                    );
                    
                    -- Time breach calculation (original logic)
                    v_is_time_breached := FALSE;
                    IF v_time_threshold_hours > 0 THEN
                        v_is_time_breached := v_loop_record.created_at < (NOW() - (v_time_threshold_hours || ' hours')::INTERVAL);
                    ELSE
                        v_is_time_breached := TRUE;
                    END IF;

                    -- Use shared condition evaluation
                    v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_base_conditions, v_data_context, v_esm_jsonb);
                    
                    IF v_is_time_breached AND v_conditions_met THEN
                        v_breaching_entity_ids := v_breaching_entity_ids || v_loop_record.entity_id;
                    END IF;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_failed_count := v_failed_count + 1;
            RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule % evaluation failed: %', v_rule.id, SQLERRM;
            UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs SET status = 'failed', error_message = 'Evaluation loop error: ' || SQLERRM WHERE id = v_job.id;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        v_entities_found := COALESCE(array_length(v_breaching_entity_ids, 1), 0);
        RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Rule %: Found % breaching entities.', v_rule.id, v_entities_found;

        -- Step 4: Process actions using shared rule execution
        IF v_entities_found > 0 THEN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, scheduled_job_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
            VALUES (v_job.organization_id, v_job.id, 'info', 'info', 'Starting scheduled job execution', jsonb_build_object('job_type', v_job.job_type, 'start_time', v_start_time), v_start_time)
            RETURNING id INTO v_job_log_id;

            INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (organization_id, scheduled_job_id, rule_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
            VALUES (v_job.organization_id, v_job.id, v_rule.id, 'success', 'info', format('Rule evaluation complete - %s entities found', v_entities_found), jsonb_build_object('rule_name', v_rule.name, 'entities_found', v_entities_found, 'entity_ids', to_jsonb(v_breaching_entity_ids)), clock_timestamp())
            RETURNING id INTO v_rule_log_id;

            FOREACH v_entity_id IN ARRAY v_breaching_entity_ids
            LOOP
                -- Refetch fresh entity data (original logic)
                SELECT automation.run_helper_get_entity_scheduled(v_rule.trigger_schema, v_rule.trigger_table, v_entity_id)
                INTO v_entity_jsonb;

                IF v_entity_jsonb IS NULL THEN
                    RAISE WARNING '[run_main_process_scheduled] Could not re-fetch entity % during action loop. Skipping.', v_entity_id;
                    CONTINUE;
                END IF;
                
                -- FIX 3: Ensure the entity ID is merged into the 'new' object's data for update actions to find the WHERE condition.
                v_entity_jsonb := v_entity_jsonb || jsonb_build_object('id', v_entity_id);

                v_trigger_data_for_action := jsonb_build_object('new', v_entity_jsonb, 'old', null);

                -- Execute actions using shared rule execution engine
                PERFORM automation.run_core_execute_rules_scheduled(
                    ARRAY[v_rule.id],
                    automation.run_helper_build_context(v_trigger_data_for_action, NULL, v_entity_id, v_job.organization_id),
                    v_trigger_data_for_action,
                    jsonb_build_object('scheduled_job_id', v_job.id, 'sla_rule_id', v_rule.metadata->>'blueprint_sla_rule_id')
                );
                
                -- Record SLA breach (original logic)
                INSERT INTO automation.sla_breaches (organization_id, esm_instance_id, sla_rule_id, sla_rule_name, metadata)
                SELECT v_job.organization_id, id, v_rule.metadata->>'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_rule.name, v_rule.metadata
                FROM automation.esm_instances WHERE entity_id = v_entity_id
                ON CONFLICT (esm_instance_id, sla_rule_id) DO NOTHING;
                
            END LOOP;
            
            UPDATE automation.wf_logs SET
                status = 'success',
                message = format('Job completed. Processed actions for %s entities.', v_entities_found),
                duration_ms = EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time)),
                context = context || jsonb_build_object('end_time', clock_timestamp(), 'entities_processed', v_entities_found)
            WHERE id = v_job_log_id;

        END IF;

        -- Step 5: Reschedule the job (original logic)
        BEGIN
            SELECT automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('NEXT_RUN', v_job.cron_expression, NOW()) INTO v_next_run;
            UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
            SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = v_next_run, status = 'pending', error_message = NULL, updated_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = v_job.id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            UPDATE automation.wf_scheduled_jobs
            SET last_run = NOW(), next_run = NOW() + interval '1 hour', status = 'pending', error_message = 'Cron parsing failed, using 1h fallback: ' || SQLERRM, updated_at = NOW()
            WHERE id = v_job.id;
        END;

    END LOOP;
    
    RAISE NOTICE '[run_main_process_scheduled] Finished processing. Actions run. Failures: %.', v_failed_count;
    RETURN format('Scheduled jobs processed. Entities actioned. Failures: %s.', v_failed_count);
END;$$;


--
-- Name: run_service_cron_scheduled(text, text, timestamp with time zone, text, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_service_cron_scheduled(p_operation text, p_expression text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_base_time timestamp with time zone DEFAULT NULL::timestamp with time zone, p_cron_part text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_value integer DEFAULT NULL::integer) RETURNS timestamp with time zone
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_next_run TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_result BOOLEAN;
    v_parts TEXT[];
    v_minute_part TEXT;
    v_hour_part TEXT;
    v_day_part TEXT;
    v_month_part TEXT;
    v_dow_part TEXT;
    v_current_time_val TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_found BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_max_iterations INT := 10000;
    v_iterations INT := 0;
    v_current_minute INT;
    v_current_hour INT;
    v_current_day INT;
    v_current_month INT;
    v_current_dow INT;
    v_cron_dow INT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Unified cron service combining cron_next_run and cron_part_matches
    -- Preserves: All original cron logic functionality
    
    IF p_operation = 'NEXT_RUN' THEN
        -- Original cron_next_run logic
        IF p_expression IS NULL OR p_base_time IS NULL THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Cron expression and base time required for NEXT_RUN operation';
        END IF;
        
        v_parts := string_to_array(p_expression, ' ');
        IF array_length(v_parts, 1) != 5 THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid cron expression: %', p_expression;
        END IF;
        
        v_minute_part := v_parts[1];
        v_hour_part := v_parts[2];
        v_day_part := v_parts[3];
        v_month_part := v_parts[4];
        v_dow_part := v_parts[5];
        
        -- Start from next minute to avoid re-running in same minute (original fix)
        v_current_time_val := date_trunc('minute', p_base_time) + interval '1 minute';
        
        WHILE NOT v_found AND v_iterations < v_max_iterations LOOP
            v_iterations := v_iterations + 1;
            
            v_current_minute := extract(minute from v_current_time_val)::INT;
            v_current_hour := extract(hour from v_current_time_val)::INT;
            v_current_day := extract(day from v_current_time_val)::INT;
            v_current_month := extract(month from v_current_time_val)::INT;
            v_current_dow := extract(dow from v_current_time_val)::INT;
            v_cron_dow := v_current_dow;
            
            IF automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_minute_part, p_value => v_current_minute) AND
               automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_hour_part, p_value => v_current_hour) AND
               automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_month_part, p_value => v_current_month) THEN
                
                IF (v_dow_part = '*' AND v_day_part = '*') THEN
                    v_found := TRUE;
                ELSIF (v_dow_part != '*' AND v_day_part = '*') THEN
                    v_found := automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_dow_part, p_value => v_cron_dow);
                ELSIF (v_dow_part = '*' AND v_day_part != '*') THEN
                    v_found := automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_day_part, p_value => v_current_day);
                ELSE
                    v_found := automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_day_part, p_value => v_current_day) OR 
                              automation.run_service_cron_scheduled('MATCH_PART', p_cron_part => v_dow_part, p_value => v_cron_dow);
                END IF;
            END IF;
            
            IF NOT v_found THEN
                v_current_time_val := v_current_time_val + interval '1 minute';
            END IF;
        END LOOP;
        
        IF NOT v_found THEN
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Could not find next run time for cron expression: %', p_expression;
        END IF;
        
        RETURN v_current_time_val;
        
    ELSIF p_operation = 'MATCH_PART' THEN
        -- Original cron_part_matches logic
        DECLARE
            elements TEXT[];
            element TEXT;
            range_parts TEXT[];
            start_val INT;
            end_val INT;
            step_val INT;
            step_parts TEXT[];
        BEGIN
            -- Handle wildcard
            IF p_cron_part = '*' THEN
                RETURN TRUE;
            END IF;
            
            -- Handle step values (e.g., */5)
            IF position('/' in p_cron_part) > 0 THEN
                step_parts := string_to_array(p_cron_part, '/');
                IF step_parts[1] = '*' THEN
                    RETURN p_value % step_parts[2]::INT = 0;
                END IF;
                -- Handle ranges with steps (e.g., 1-30/5)
                range_parts := string_to_array(step_parts[1], '-');
                start_val := range_parts[1]::INT;
                end_val := range_parts[2]::INT;
                step_val := step_parts[2]::INT;
                RETURN p_value >= start_val AND p_value <= end_val AND (p_value - start_val) % step_val = 0;
            END IF;
            
            -- Handle lists (e.g., 1,3,5)
            IF position(',' in p_cron_part) > 0 THEN
                elements := string_to_array(p_cron_part, ',');
                FOREACH element IN ARRAY elements LOOP
                    IF element::INT = p_value THEN
                        RETURN TRUE;
                    END IF;
                END LOOP;
                RETURN FALSE;
            END IF;
            
            -- Handle ranges (e.g., 1-5)
            IF position('-' in p_cron_part) > 0 THEN
                range_parts := string_to_array(p_cron_part, '-');
                start_val := range_parts[1]::INT;
                end_val := range_parts[2]::INT;
                RETURN p_value >= start_val AND p_value <= end_val;
            END IF;
            
            -- Handle single value
            RETURN p_cron_part::INT = p_value;
        EXCEPTION
            WHEN OTHERS THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid cron part: %', p_cron_part;
        END;
    ELSE
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unsupported cron operation: %', p_operation;
    END IF;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: run_service_resolve_recipients(jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(p_recipient_config jsonb, p_flattened_data jsonb) RETURNS text[]
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_all_emails TEXT[] := ARRAY[]::TEXT[];
    v_user_emails_jsonb JSONB[];
    v_user_emails_text TEXT[];
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Wrapper for recipient resolution to maintain compatibility
    -- Simply calls the new user resolution service and converts result
    
    IF p_recipient_config IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_recipient_config) != 'object' THEN
        RETURN v_all_emails;
    END IF;

    -- Use the new user resolution service
    v_user_emails_jsonb := automation.run_service_resolve_users(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'select_columns', 'details->>''email''',
            'sources', p_recipient_config->'sources',
            'pool_logic', COALESCE(p_recipient_config->>'pool_logic', 'OR')
        ),
        p_flattened_data
    );

    -- Convert JSONB array to TEXT array
    SELECT array_agg(elem #>> '{}')
    INTO v_user_emails_text
    FROM unnest(COALESCE(v_user_emails_jsonb, ARRAY[]::JSONB[])) elem;

    RETURN COALESCE(v_user_emails_text, ARRAY[]::TEXT[]);
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_service_resolve_users(jsonb, jsonb, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_service_resolve_users(p_config jsonb, p_flat_data jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb, p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS jsonb[]
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_user_id_1 UUID := '601c0706-deb2-4bbe-9c3d-1f14f2f80248';
    v_user_id_2 UUID := '6ba504d2-65b7-4018-b8a1-323dd686996c';
    v_user_id_3 UUID := 'abd7663c-aa29-4c15-9a91-8356bb0ffb90';
    v_email_1 TEXT;
    v_email_2 TEXT;
    v_email_3 TEXT;
    v_select_columns TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Expanded user/team/role resolution service
    -- Preserves: All original _util_resolve_users mock functionality
    -- Supports: Assignment resolution, email resolution, user lookup
    
    v_select_columns := p_config->>'select_columns';
    
    -- Mock response for assignment resolver (original logic)
    IF v_select_columns LIKE '%last_assigned_at_for_source%' THEN
        RETURN ARRAY[
            jsonb_build_object(
                'id', v_user_id_1, 
                'last_assigned_at_for_source', '2025-10-27T10:00:00Z'::timestamptz, 
                'rr_source_id', 'b7cf4f27-d2b8-4380-a135-02cf549551cd', 
                'rr_source_type', 'team'
            ),
            jsonb_build_object(
                'id', v_user_id_2, 
                'last_assigned_at_for_source', '-infinity'::timestamptz, 
                'rr_source_id', 'b7cf4f27-d2b8-4380-a135-02cf549551cd', 
                'rr_source_type', 'team'
            ),
            jsonb_build_object(
                'id', v_user_id_3, 
                'last_assigned_at_for_source', '2025-10-28T09:00:00Z'::timestamptz, 
                'rr_source_id', '80d2b431-7b95-453a-9a24-b697eefeca42', 
                'rr_source_type', 'role'
            )
        ];
    END IF;

    -- Mock response for email resolution (original logic)
    IF v_select_columns = 'details->>''email''' THEN
        v_email_1 := (SELECT email FROM identity.users WHERE id = v_user_id_1);
        v_email_2 := (SELECT email FROM identity.users WHERE id = v_user_id_2);
        v_email_3 := (SELECT email FROM identity.users WHERE id = v_user_id_3);
        RETURN ARRAY(
            SELECT to_jsonb(email) 
            FROM unnest(ARRAY[v_email_1, v_email_2, v_email_3]) email 
            WHERE email IS NOT NULL
        );
    END IF;

    -- Mock response for user ID + email lookup (original logic)
    IF v_select_columns = 'id, email' THEN
        v_email_1 := (SELECT email FROM identity.users WHERE id = v_user_id_1);
        v_email_2 := (SELECT email FROM identity.users WHERE id = v_user_id_2);
        RETURN ARRAY[
            jsonb_build_object('id', v_user_id_1, 'email', v_email_1),
            jsonb_build_object('id', v_user_id_2, 'email', v_email_2)
        ];
    END IF;

    -- Fallback empty array
    RETURN ARRAY[]::jsonb[];
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_assign_lead(uuid, text, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_assign_lead(p_entity_id uuid, p_entity_type text, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_sales_team_id UUID := '5a1e5000-0000-0000-0000-000000000001';
    v_assigned_user_id UUID;
    v_assignment_config JSONB;
    v_trigger_data JSONB;
    v_result JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- Configure round robin from the Sales team
    v_assignment_config := jsonb_build_object(
        'rule', 'round_robin',
        'sources', jsonb_build_array(
            jsonb_build_object('type', 'team', 'id', v_sales_team_id)
        )
    );

    -- Minimal trigger data for the resolver
    v_trigger_data := jsonb_build_object('new', jsonb_build_object('id', p_entity_id));

    -- Call the core assignment resolver
    v_result := automation.smart_assign_owner(
        jsonb_build_object(
            'table', p_entity_type,
            'schema', 'public',
            'field', 'assigned_user_id',
            'assignment', v_assignment_config
        ),
        v_trigger_data,
        jsonb_build_object('organization_id', p_organization_id)
    );

    RETURN v_result;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_create_entity(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_create_entity(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_schema_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'entity_schema', 'public');
    v_table_name TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_name';
    v_payload JSONB := p_action_config->'payload';
    v_prefilled_payload JSONB;
    v_insert_clauses RECORD;
    v_sql TEXT;
    v_new_entity_id UUID;
    v_new_record_jsonb JSONB;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
    v_flat_data JSONB;
    
    -- Variables for the helper function
    v_parent_schema TEXT;
    v_parent_table TEXT;
    v_parent_id UUID;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Minimal changes - only replaced function calls with new names
    -- Preserves: All original tested logic exactly as-is
    
    IF v_table_name IS NULL OR v_payload IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_payload) != 'object' OR v_payload = '{}'::jsonb OR v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_create_entity] Invalid config: "entity_name", non-empty "payload", and organization_id in context are required.';
    END IF;

    -- Pre-flatten the data and get parent info (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_flat_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);
    v_parent_schema := p_context->>'trigger_schema';
    v_parent_table := p_context->>'trigger_table';
    v_parent_id := (p_trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;

    -- Step 1: Resolve placeholders in the payload (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_payload := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        v_payload, 
        v_flat_data, 
        v_organization_id
    );

    -- Step 2: Intelligent Prefill (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_prefilled_payload := automation.run_util_prefill_payload(
        v_schema_name,       -- p_target_schema (text)
        v_table_name,        -- p_target_table (text)
        v_flat_data,         -- p_parent_flat_data (jsonb)
        v_parent_schema,     -- p_parent_schema (text)
        v_parent_table,      -- p_parent_table (text)
        v_parent_id          -- p_parent_id (uuid)
    );

    -- Step 3: Merge Payloads (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    v_payload := v_prefilled_payload || v_payload;

    -- Step 4: Add organization_id (as a final failsafe) (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF NOT (v_payload ? 'organization_id') OR (v_payload->>'organization_id') IS NULL THEN
        v_payload := v_payload || jsonb_build_object('organization_id', v_organization_id);
    END IF;

    -- Step 5: Get columns and values strings (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_sql := automation.run_helper_build_sql('INSERT', v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_payload);
    
    -- Extract columns and values from the generated SQL (preserving original logic flow)
    -- Note: run_helper_build_sql returns complete SQL, so we need to adapt slightly
    IF v_sql IS NULL OR v_sql = '' THEN
         RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'failed', 
            'reason', 'Payload became empty after prefill and resolution.',
            'prefilled_payload', v_prefilled_payload,
            'original_payload', p_action_config->'payload'
         );
    END IF;

    -- Step 6: Build INSERT statement (ADAPTED for new SQL builder)
    -- The new SQL builder returns complete SQL, so we add RETURNING clause
    v_sql := v_sql || format(' RETURNING id, to_jsonb(%I.*)', v_table_name);

    -- Step 7: Execute (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'true', false);
    BEGIN
        EXECUTE v_sql INTO v_new_entity_id, v_new_record_jsonb;
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);
        RAISE;
    END;
    PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);

    -- Step 8: Return context (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF v_new_entity_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_create_entity] INSERT failed for table %s. SQL: %', v_table_name, v_sql;
    END IF;

    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', 'success',
        'created_entity_id', v_new_entity_id,
        'created_entity', v_new_record_jsonb
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_send_email(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_send_email(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_template_id TEXT := p_action_config->>'template_id';
    v_recipients_config JSONB := p_action_config->'recipients';
    v_template_details JSONB;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;

    v_to_recipients TEXT[];
    v_cc_recipients TEXT[];
    v_bcc_recipients TEXT[];

    v_flattened_data JSONB;
    v_final_subject TEXT;
    v_final_body TEXT;

    v_supabase_service_role_key TEXT;
    v_project_ref TEXT;
    v_send_email_url TEXT;
    v_from_email TEXT;

    v_job_id bigint;

    v_http_content text;
    v_http_error text;
    v_http_status integer;
    v_http_timeout boolean;
BEGIN
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Load secrets + config (MINIMAL FIX: use decrypted_secrets)
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT decrypted_secret
    INTO v_supabase_service_role_key
    FROM vault.decrypted_secrets
    WHERE name = 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY'
    LIMIT 1;

    SELECT value
    INTO v_from_email
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'RESEND_FROM_EMAIL';

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- [019b] URL Resolution: app_config → JWT → correct fallback
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Priority 1: app_config (explicit configuration)
    SELECT value INTO v_send_email_url
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';

    -- Priority 2: Extract from JWT issuer
    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL THEN
        BEGIN
            v_project_ref := (
                SELECT
                    split_part(
                        replace(
                            (current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::jsonb ->> 'iss'),
                            'https://', ''
                        ),
                        '.', 1
                    )
                WHERE current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true) IS NOT NULL
            );

            IF v_project_ref IS NOT NULL AND v_project_ref <> '' THEN
                v_send_email_url := format(
                    'https://%s.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email',
                    v_project_ref
                );
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            NULL; -- fall through to fallback
        END;
    END IF;

    -- Priority 3: Hardcoded correct fallback [019b: was begqjgsemuiisjznayzx]
    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL THEN
        v_send_email_url := 'https://yfqpniqzyesnvriewysj.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Validate configuration
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF v_supabase_service_role_key IS NULL OR v_from_email IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Missing secrets/config.';
    END IF;

    IF v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] organization_id missing in p_context';
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Flatten trigger data
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    v_flattened_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Resolve recipients
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    v_to_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'to',  v_flattened_data);
    v_cc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'cc',  v_flattened_data);
    v_bcc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'bcc', v_flattened_data);

    IF COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0) = 0
       AND COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'skipped',
            'reason', 'No "to" or "cc" recipients'
        );
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Resolve template
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF v_template_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT details INTO v_template_details
        FROM public.email_templates
        WHERE name = v_template_id
          AND organization_id = v_organization_id;
    END IF;

    v_final_subject := COALESCE(
        p_action_config->>'subject',
        v_template_details->>'subject',
        'No Subject'
    );

    v_final_body := COALESCE(
        v_template_details->>'body',
        p_action_config->>'body',
        'This is an automated notification.'
    );

    v_final_subject := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_subject), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';

    v_final_body := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_body), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- EXECUTE HTTP POST (ASYNC)
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT net.http_post(
        url := v_send_email_url,
        headers := jsonb_build_object(
            'Content-Type', 'application/json',
            'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_supabase_service_role_key
        ),
        body := jsonb_build_array(
            jsonb_build_object(
                'from', COALESCE(p_action_config->>'from', v_from_email),
                'to', v_to_recipients,
                'cc', v_cc_recipients,
                'bcc', v_bcc_recipients,
                'subject', v_final_subject,
                'html', v_final_body
            )
        )
    )
    INTO v_job_id;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Wait for async response
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    PERFORM pg_sleep(1);

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Fetch async pg_net response
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT
        content,
        error_msg,
        status_code,
        timed_out
    INTO
        v_http_content,
        v_http_error,
        v_http_status,
        v_http_timeout
    FROM net._http_response
    WHERE id = v_job_id;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Error reporting
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF v_http_error IS NOT NULL THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'failed',
            'job_id', v_job_id,
            'error', v_http_error,
            'status_code', v_http_status,
            'timed_out', v_http_timeout
        );
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- SUCCESS
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', 'success',
        'job_id', v_job_id,
        'status_code', v_http_status,
        'response', v_http_content,
        'recipient_counts', jsonb_build_object(
            'to', COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0),
            'cc', COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0),
            'bcc', COALESCE(array_length(v_bcc_recipients, 1), 0)
        )
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_send_email_test(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_send_email_test() RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'vault', 'net'
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_key TEXT;
    v_url TEXT;
    v_request_id BIGINT;
BEGIN
    RAISE NOTICE 'Entering function';

    SELECT secret INTO v_key
    FROM vault.secrets
    WHERE name = 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY';

    -- [019b] Load URL from app_config, fallback to correct project
    SELECT value INTO v_url
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';

    IF v_url IS NULL THEN
        v_url := 'https://yfqpniqzyesnvriewysj.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Key prefix: %...', LEFT(v_key, 8);

    SELECT net.http_post(
        url := v_url,
        headers := jsonb_build_object(
            'Content-Type','application/json',
            'Authorization','Bearer ' || v_key
        ),
        body := jsonb_build_object(
            'from','support@vkbs.zoworks.ai',
            'to',jsonb_build_array('ravi@claritiz.com'),
            'subject','Test',
            'html','<p>Hello!</p>'
        )
    ) INTO v_request_id;

    RAISE NOTICE 'ID = %', v_request_id;

    PERFORM pg_sleep(1);

    RAISE NOTICE 'Response: %',
    (
        SELECT
            'status='||status_code||' | error='||coalesce(error_msg,'none')
        FROM net._http_response
        WHERE id = v_request_id
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_send_email_v2(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_send_email_v2(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_template_id TEXT := p_action_config->>'template_id';
    v_recipients_config JSONB := p_action_config->'recipients';
    v_template_details JSONB;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;

    v_to_recipients TEXT[];
    v_cc_recipients TEXT[];
    v_bcc_recipients TEXT[];

    v_flattened_data JSONB;
    v_final_subject TEXT;
    v_final_body TEXT;

    v_supabase_service_role_key TEXT;
    v_project_ref TEXT;
    v_send_email_url TEXT;
    v_from_email TEXT;

    -- pg_net returns BIGINT id
    v_job_id bigint;

    -- Correct pg_net response fields
    v_http_content text;
    v_http_error text;
    v_http_status integer;
    v_http_timeout boolean;

BEGIN
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Load secrets + config
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT secret
    INTO v_supabase_service_role_key
    FROM vault.secrets
    WHERE name = 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY';

    SELECT value
    INTO v_from_email
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'RESEND_FROM_EMAIL';

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- [019b] URL Resolution: app_config → JWT → correct fallback
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT value INTO v_send_email_url
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';

    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL THEN
        BEGIN
            v_project_ref := (
                SELECT
                    split_part(
                        replace(
                            (current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::jsonb ->> 'iss'),
                            'https://', ''
                        ),
                        '.', 1
                    )
                WHERE current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true) IS NOT NULL
            );

            IF v_project_ref IS NOT NULL AND v_project_ref <> '' THEN
                v_send_email_url := format(
                    'https://%s.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email',
                    v_project_ref
                );
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            NULL;
        END;
    END IF;

    -- [019b] Correct fallback
    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL THEN
        v_send_email_url := 'https://yfqpniqzyesnvriewysj.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Validate configuration
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF v_supabase_service_role_key IS NULL OR v_from_email IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Missing secrets/config.';
    END IF;

    IF v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] organization_id missing in p_context';
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Flatten data
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    v_flattened_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Resolve recipients
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    v_to_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'to',  v_flattened_data);
    v_cc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'cc',  v_flattened_data);
    v_bcc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'bcc', v_flattened_data);

    IF COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0) = 0
       AND COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'skipped',
            'reason', 'No "to" or "cc" recipients'
        );
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Resolve template
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF v_template_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT details INTO v_template_details
        FROM public.email_templates
        WHERE name = v_template_id
          AND organization_id = v_organization_id;
    END IF;

    v_final_subject := COALESCE(
        p_action_config->>'subject',
        v_template_details->>'subject',
        'No Subject'
    );

    v_final_body := COALESCE(
        v_template_details->>'body',
        p_action_config->>'body',
        'This is an automated notification.'
    );
