    v_final_subject := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_subject), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';

    v_final_body := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_body), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Execute async HTTP POST
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT net.http_post(
        url := v_send_email_url,
        headers := jsonb_build_object(
            'Content-Type', 'application/json',
            'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_supabase_service_role_key
        ),
        body := jsonb_build_array(
            jsonb_build_object(
                'from', COALESCE(p_action_config->>'from', v_from_email),
                'to', v_to_recipients,
                'cc', v_cc_recipients,
                'bcc', v_bcc_recipients,
                'subject', v_final_subject,
                'html', v_final_body
            )
        )
    )
    INTO v_job_id;

    PERFORM pg_sleep(1);

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Fetch async result
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT
        content,
        error_msg,
        status_code,
        timed_out
    INTO
        v_http_content,
        v_http_error,
        v_http_status,
        v_http_timeout
    FROM net._http_response
    WHERE id = v_job_id;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Handle error
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    IF COALESCE(TRIM(v_http_error), '') <> '' THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'failed',
            'job_id', v_job_id,
            'error', v_http_error,
            'status_code', v_http_status,
            'timed_out', v_http_timeout
        );
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- Success
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', 'success',
        'job_id', v_job_id,
        'status_code', v_http_status,
        'timed_out', v_http_timeout,
        'response_content', v_http_content,
        'recipient_counts', jsonb_build_object(
            'to', COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0),
            'cc', COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0),
            'bcc', COALESCE(array_length(v_bcc_recipients, 1), 0)
        )
    );

END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_smart_update_entity(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_smart_update_entity(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- DECLARE
--     v_schema_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'entity_schema', 'public');
--     v_table_name TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_name';
--     v_payload JSONB := p_action_config->'payload';
--     v_filter JSONB := p_action_config->'filter';
    
--     v_entity_id_config TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_id';
--     v_entity_id_query TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_id_from_query';

--     v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
--     v_flat_data JSONB;
--     v_trigger_id UUID;

--     v_set_clause TEXT;  -- CHANGED: Use separate SET clause
--     v_where_clause TEXT := NULL;
--     v_sql TEXT;
--     v_rows_affected INT := 0;
-- BEGIN
--     -- 1. Validate config (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_table_name IS NULL OR v_payload IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_payload) != 'object' OR v_payload = '{}'::jsonb THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Invalid config: "entity_name" and non-empty "payload" object are required.';
--     END IF;

--     -- 2. Flatten data for resolution (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
--     v_flat_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);
--     v_trigger_id := (p_trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;

--     -- 3. Resolve Placeholders Recursively (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
--     v_payload := automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_payload, v_flat_data, v_organization_id);
--     v_filter := automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_filter, v_flat_data, v_organization_id);
    
--     -- 4. Build SET clause (FIXED: Use SQL builder for SET clause only)
--     v_set_clause := automation.run_helper_build_sql('UPDATE', p_data => v_payload);
    
--     -- Remove "UPDATE ... SET" prefix to get just the SET clause
--     IF v_set_clause IS NOT NULL AND v_set_clause != '' THEN
--         v_set_clause := regexp_replace(v_set_clause, '^UPDATE\s+\S+\s+SET\s+', '', 'i');
--     END IF;
    
--     IF v_set_clause IS NULL OR v_set_clause = '' THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Payload resulted in empty SET clause.';
--     END IF;

--     -- 5. Build WHERE clause (with priority) - ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED
--     -- Priority 1: Filter
--     IF v_filter IS NOT NULL AND jsonb_typeof(v_filter) = 'object' AND v_filter != '{}'::jsonb THEN
--         v_where_clause := automation.run_helper_build_sql('WHERE', p_conditions => v_filter);
--     END IF;
    
--     -- Priority 2: Entity ID (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_where_clause IS NULL AND v_entity_id_config IS NOT NULL THEN
--         v_entity_id_config := automation.run_util_resolve_template(to_jsonb(v_entity_id_config), v_flat_data, v_organization_id) #>> '{}';
--         IF v_entity_id_config IS NOT NULL THEN
--             v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_entity_id_config::uuid);
--         END IF;
--     END IF;

--     -- Priority 3: Entity ID from Query (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_where_clause IS NULL AND v_entity_id_query IS NOT NULL THEN
--         DECLARE 
--             v_placeholder_query TEXT := v_entity_id_query;
--             v_placeholder RECORD;
--             v_placeholder_path TEXT;
--             v_resolved_text TEXT;
--             v_target_id UUID;
--         BEGIN
--             FOR v_placeholder IN SELECT DISTINCT regexp_matches(v_entity_id_query, '{{([^{}]+)}}', 'g') AS ph LOOP
--                 v_placeholder_path := v_placeholder.ph[1];
--                 v_resolved_text := v_flat_data->>v_placeholder_path;
--                 IF v_resolved_text IS NOT NULL THEN
--                     v_placeholder_query := replace(v_placeholder_query, '{{' || v_placeholder_path || '}}', quote_literal(v_resolved_text));
--                 ELSE
--                     RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Could not resolve placeholder "%"', v_placeholder_path;
--                 END IF;
--             END LOOP;
--             EXECUTE v_placeholder_query INTO v_target_id;
            
--             IF v_target_id IS NULL THEN
--                 RETURN jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'skipped', 'reason', 'entity_id_from_query returned NULL.', 'query', v_placeholder_query);
--             END IF;
--             v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_target_id);
--         END;
--     END IF;

--     -- Priority 4: Fallback to triggering ID (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_where_clause IS NULL THEN
--         IF v_trigger_id IS NOT NULL THEN
--             v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_trigger_id);
--         ELSE
--              RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Could not determine WHERE clause. All methods (filter, entity_id, query, default) failed.';
--         END IF;
--     END IF;

--     -- 6. Build and Execute SQL (FIXED: Build complete SQL manually)
--     v_sql := format('UPDATE %I.%I SET %s WHERE %s',
--         v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_set_clause, v_where_clause);

--     -- ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED for automation flag
--     IF v_schema_name = (p_context->>'trigger_schema') AND v_table_name = (p_context->>'trigger_table') THEN
--          PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'true', false);
--     END IF;

--     BEGIN
--         EXECUTE v_sql;
--         GET DIAGNOSTICS v_rows_affected = ROW_COUNT;
--     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--          PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);
--          RAISE WARNING '[run_smart_update_entity] SQL Error: %, SQL: %', SQLERRM, v_sql;
--          RAISE;
--     END;
--     PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);

--     -- 7. Return context (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     RETURN jsonb_build_object(
--         'outcome', CASE WHEN v_rows_affected > 0 THEN 'success' ELSE 'no_change_or_not_found' END,
--         'sql', v_sql, 
--         'rows_affected', v_rows_affected
--     );
-- END;
DECLARE
    v_schema_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'entity_schema', 'public');
    v_table_name TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_name';
    v_payload JSONB := p_action_config->'payload';
    v_filter JSONB := p_action_config->'filter';
    
    v_entity_id_config TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_id';
    v_entity_id_query TEXT := p_action_config->>'entity_id_from_query';

    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
    v_flat_data JSONB;
    v_trigger_id UUID;

    v_set_clause TEXT;  -- CHANGED: Use separate SET clause
    v_where_clause TEXT := NULL;
    v_sql TEXT;
    v_rows_affected INT := 0;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate config (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF v_table_name IS NULL OR v_payload IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_payload) != 'object' OR v_payload = '{}'::jsonb THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Invalid config: "entity_name" and non-empty "payload" object are required.';
    END IF;

    -- 2. Flatten data for resolution (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_flat_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);
    v_trigger_id := (p_trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;

    -- 3. Resolve Placeholders Recursively (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
    v_payload := automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_payload, v_flat_data, v_organization_id);
    v_filter := automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_filter, v_flat_data, v_organization_id);
    
    -- 4. Build SET clause (FIXED: Use SQL builder for SET clause only)
    v_set_clause := automation.run_helper_build_sql('UPDATE', p_data => v_payload);
    
    -- Remove "UPDATE ... SET" prefix to get just the SET clause
    IF v_set_clause IS NOT NULL AND v_set_clause != '' THEN
        v_set_clause := regexp_replace(v_set_clause, '^UPDATE\s+\S+\s+SET\s+', '', 'i');
    END IF;
    
    IF v_set_clause IS NULL OR v_set_clause = '' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Payload resulted in empty SET clause.';
    END IF;

    -- 5. Build WHERE clause (with priority) - ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED
    -- Priority 1: Filter
    IF v_filter IS NOT NULL AND jsonb_typeof(v_filter) = 'object' AND v_filter != '{}'::jsonb THEN
        v_where_clause := automation.run_helper_build_sql('WHERE', p_conditions => v_filter);
    END IF;
    
    -- Priority 2: Entity ID (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF v_where_clause IS NULL AND v_entity_id_config IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_entity_id_config := automation.run_util_resolve_template(to_jsonb(v_entity_id_config), v_flat_data, v_organization_id) #>> '{}';
        IF v_entity_id_config IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_entity_id_config::uuid);
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- Priority 3: Entity ID from Query (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF v_where_clause IS NULL AND v_entity_id_query IS NOT NULL THEN
        DECLARE 
            v_placeholder_query TEXT := v_entity_id_query;
            v_placeholder RECORD;
            v_placeholder_path TEXT;
            v_resolved_text TEXT;
            v_target_id UUID;
        BEGIN
            FOR v_placeholder IN SELECT DISTINCT regexp_matches(v_entity_id_query, '{{([^{}]+)}}', 'g') AS ph LOOP
                v_placeholder_path := v_placeholder.ph[1];
                v_resolved_text := v_flat_data->>v_placeholder_path;
                IF v_resolved_text IS NOT NULL THEN
                    v_placeholder_query := replace(v_placeholder_query, '{{' || v_placeholder_path || '}}', quote_literal(v_resolved_text));
                ELSE
                    RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_update_entity] Could not resolve placeholder "%"', v_placeholder_path;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
            EXECUTE v_placeholder_query INTO v_target_id;
            
            IF v_target_id IS NULL THEN
                RETURN jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'skipped', 'reason', 'entity_id_from_query returned NULL.', 'query', v_placeholder_query);
            END IF;
            v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_target_id);
        END;
    END IF;

    -- Priority 4: Fallback to triggering ID (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    IF v_where_clause IS NULL THEN
        IF v_trigger_id IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_where_clause := format('id = %L', v_trigger_id);
        ELSE
             -- MINIMAL FIX: Don't raise an exception here, return skipped status
             -- The error will be handled by the outer logic if the WHERE clause is still missing
             RETURN jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'skipped', 'reason', 'Could not determine WHERE clause, missing trigger ID.');
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- 6. Build and Execute SQL (FIXED: Build complete SQL manually)
    v_sql := format('UPDATE %I.%I SET %s WHERE %s',
        v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_set_clause, v_where_clause);

    -- ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED for automation flag
    IF v_schema_name = (p_context->>'trigger_schema') AND v_table_name = (p_context->>'trigger_table') THEN
         PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'true', false);
    END IF;

    BEGIN
        EXECUTE v_sql;
        GET DIAGNOSTICS v_rows_affected = ROW_COUNT;
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
         PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);
         RAISE WARNING '[run_smart_update_entity] SQL Error: %, SQL: %', SQLERRM, v_sql;
         RAISE;
    END;
    PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false);

    -- 7. Return context (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', CASE WHEN v_rows_affected > 0 THEN 'success' ELSE 'no_change_or_not_found' END,
        'sql', v_sql, 
        'rows_affected', v_rows_affected
    );
END;$$;


--
-- Name: run_util_eval_conditions(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_util_eval_conditions(p_conditions jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_esm_data jsonb DEFAULT NULL::jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_data_context JSONB;
    v_flat_data JSONB;
    v_result BOOLEAN;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Enhanced condition evaluation with internal data handling
    -- Preserves: All original _util_evaluate_jsonb_condition_v2 logic
    -- Adds: Automatic data context building and flattening
    
    -- Build data context (replaces duplicated code from multiple locations)
    v_data_context := jsonb_build_object(
        'new', p_trigger_data->'new',
        'old', p_trigger_data->'old',
        'esm', p_esm_data
    );
    
    -- Flatten data for efficient lookup (internalized _util_flatten_jsonb)
    v_flat_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(v_data_context);
    
    -- Evaluate conditions using original logic
    v_result := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_core(p_conditions, v_data_context, v_flat_data);
    
    RETURN v_result;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_util_eval_conditions_core(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_util_eval_conditions_core(p_conditions jsonb, p_data_context jsonb, p_flat_data jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $_$
DECLARE
    v_rule jsonb;
    v_result BOOLEAN;
    v_field_key TEXT;
    v_operator TEXT;
    v_compare_value JSONB;
    v_prefix TEXT;
    v_path TEXT;
    v_data_source JSONB;
    v_field_value JSONB;
    v_field_value_text TEXT;
    v_compare_value_text TEXT;
    v_comparison_result BOOLEAN;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Preserves exact logic from _util_evaluate_jsonb_condition_v2
    IF p_conditions IS NULL OR p_conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR NOT(p_conditions ? 'rules') OR jsonb_array_length(p_conditions->'rules') = 0 THEN
        RETURN TRUE;
    END IF;

    CASE COALESCE(p_conditions->>'type', 'AND')
        WHEN 'AND' THEN
            v_result := TRUE;
            FOR v_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_conditions->'rules') 
            LOOP
                IF v_rule ? 'type' THEN
                    v_result := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_core(v_rule, p_data_context, p_flat_data);
                ELSE
                    -- Base case evaluation (original logic preserved)
                    v_field_key := v_rule->>'field';
                    v_operator := v_rule->>'operator';
                    v_compare_value := v_rule->'value';
                    v_prefix := split_part(v_field_key, '.', 1);
                    v_path := substring(v_field_key from (length(v_prefix) + 2));

                    IF v_prefix = 'new' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'new';
                    ELSIF v_prefix = 'old' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'old';
                    ELSIF v_prefix = 'esm' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'esm';
                    ELSE
                        RAISE WARNING '[run_util_eval_conditions] Invalid field prefix: %', v_prefix;
                        v_result := FALSE;
                        EXIT;
                    END IF;

                    BEGIN
                        IF v_path LIKE '%->>%' THEN
                            v_field_value := to_jsonb(jsonb_path_query_first(v_data_source, ('$.' || replace(v_path, '->>', '.'))::jsonpath));
                        ELSIF v_path LIKE '%->%' THEN
                            v_field_value := jsonb_path_query_first(v_data_source, ('$.' || replace(v_path, '->', '.'))::jsonpath);
                        ELSE
                            v_field_value := v_data_source->v_path;
                        END IF;
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN 
                        v_field_value := NULL; 
                    END;

                    v_compare_value_text := v_compare_value#>>'{}';
                    v_field_value_text := v_field_value#>>'{}';
                    v_comparison_result := FALSE;

                    -- Complex operations with type casting
                    IF v_operator IN ('less_than', 'greater_than', 'hours_ago') THEN
                        BEGIN
                            IF v_operator = 'less_than' THEN
                                IF v_compare_value_text = 'NOW()' THEN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < NOW();
                                ELSE
                                    BEGIN
                                        v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::NUMERIC < v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC;
                                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                                        v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < v_compare_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ;
                                    END;
                                END IF;
                            ELSIF v_operator = 'greater_than' THEN
                                BEGIN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::NUMERIC > v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC;
                                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ > v_compare_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ;
                                END;
                            ELSIF v_operator = 'hours_ago' THEN
                                v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < (NOW() - (v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC || ' hours')::INTERVAL);
                            END IF;
                        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                            v_comparison_result := FALSE;
                        END;
                    END IF;

                    v_result := CASE v_operator
                        WHEN 'equals' THEN v_field_value = v_compare_value
                        WHEN 'not_equals' THEN v_field_value IS DISTINCT FROM v_compare_value
                        WHEN 'is_null' THEN v_field_value IS NULL OR v_field_value = 'null'::jsonb
                        WHEN 'is_not_null' THEN v_field_value IS NOT NULL AND v_field_value != 'null'::jsonb
                        WHEN 'less_than' THEN v_comparison_result
                        WHEN 'greater_than' THEN v_comparison_result
                        WHEN 'hours_ago' THEN v_comparison_result
                        ELSE FALSE
                    END;
                END IF;

                IF NOT v_result THEN EXIT; END IF;
            END LOOP;

        WHEN 'OR' THEN
            v_result := FALSE;
            FOR v_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_conditions->'rules') 
            LOOP
                IF v_rule ? 'type' THEN
                    v_result := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_core(v_rule, p_data_context, p_flat_data);
                ELSE
                    -- Base case evaluation (same as AND block)
                    v_field_key := v_rule->>'field';
                    v_operator := v_rule->>'operator';
                    v_compare_value := v_rule->'value';
                    v_prefix := split_part(v_field_key, '.', 1);
                    v_path := substring(v_field_key from (length(v_prefix) + 2));

                    IF v_prefix = 'new' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'new';
                    ELSIF v_prefix = 'old' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'old';
                    ELSIF v_prefix = 'esm' THEN v_data_source := p_data_context->'esm';
                    ELSE v_result := FALSE; CONTINUE;
                    END IF;

                    BEGIN
                        IF v_path LIKE '%->>%' THEN
                            v_field_value := to_jsonb(jsonb_path_query_first(v_data_source, ('$.' || replace(v_path, '->>', '.'))::jsonpath));
                        ELSIF v_path LIKE '%->%' THEN
                            v_field_value := jsonb_path_query_first(v_data_source, ('$.' || replace(v_path, '->', '.'))::jsonpath);
                        ELSE
                            v_field_value := v_data_source->v_path;
                        END IF;
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN v_field_value := NULL; END;

                    v_compare_value_text := v_compare_value#>>'{}';
                    v_field_value_text := v_field_value#>>'{}';
                    v_comparison_result := FALSE;

                    IF v_operator IN ('less_than', 'greater_than', 'hours_ago') THEN
                        BEGIN
                            IF v_operator = 'less_than' THEN
                                IF v_compare_value_text = 'NOW()' THEN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < NOW();
                                ELSE
                                    BEGIN
                                        v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::NUMERIC < v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC;
                                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                                        v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < v_compare_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ;
                                    END;
                                END IF;
                            ELSIF v_operator = 'greater_than' THEN
                                BEGIN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::NUMERIC > v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC;
                                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                                    v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ > v_compare_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ;
                                END;
                            ELSIF v_operator = 'hours_ago' THEN
                                v_comparison_result := v_field_value_text::TIMESTAMPTZ < (NOW() - (v_compare_value_text::NUMERIC || ' hours')::INTERVAL);
                            END IF;
                        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                            v_comparison_result := FALSE;
                        END;
                    END IF;

                    v_result := CASE v_operator
                        WHEN 'equals' THEN v_field_value = v_compare_value
                        WHEN 'not_equals' THEN v_field_value IS DISTINCT FROM v_compare_value
                        WHEN 'is_null' THEN v_field_value IS NULL OR v_field_value = 'null'::jsonb
                        WHEN 'is_not_null' THEN v_field_value IS NOT NULL AND v_field_value != 'null'::jsonb
                        WHEN 'less_than' THEN v_comparison_result
                        WHEN 'greater_than' THEN v_comparison_result
                        WHEN 'hours_ago' THEN v_comparison_result
                        ELSE FALSE
                    END;
                END IF;

                IF v_result THEN EXIT; END IF;
            END LOOP;
        ELSE
            RAISE WARNING '[run_util_eval_conditions] Unsupported condition type: %', p_conditions->>'type';
            RETURN FALSE;
    END CASE;

    RETURN v_result;
END;
$_$;


--
-- Name: run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(jsonb, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_data jsonb, p_prefix text DEFAULT ''::text) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_result JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_rec RECORD;
    v_new_key TEXT;
BEGIN
    IF p_data IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(p_data) != 'object' THEN
        RETURN v_result;
    END IF;
    
    FOR v_rec IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_data) 
    LOOP
        v_new_key := p_prefix || v_rec.key;
        IF jsonb_typeof(v_rec.value) = 'object' THEN
            v_result := v_result || automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(v_rec.value, v_new_key || '.');
        ELSE
            v_result := jsonb_set(v_result, ARRAY[v_new_key], v_rec.value);
        END IF;
    END LOOP;
    
    RETURN v_result;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_util_prefill_payload(text, text, jsonb, text, text, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_util_prefill_payload(p_target_schema text, p_target_table text, p_parent_flat_data jsonb, p_parent_schema text, p_parent_table text, p_parent_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_prefilled_payload JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_target_meta JSONB;
    v_column_meta JSONB;
    v_col_name TEXT;
    v_parent_key_top_level TEXT;
    v_parent_key_in_details TEXT;
    v_parent_value JSONB;
    v_target_columns TEXT[];
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Direct copy of _smart_prefill_payload_v3 with renamed function
    -- Preserves all original tested logic exactly
    
    -- Get metadata for the new entity we are creating
    SELECT e.metadata INTO v_target_meta
    FROM core.entities e
    WHERE e.entity_schema = p_target_schema AND e.entity_type = p_target_table;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE DEBUG '[run_util_prefill_payload] No metadata found for %.%', p_target_schema, p_target_table;
        RETURN v_prefilled_payload;
    END IF;

    -- Build array of actual column names from metadata
    SELECT ARRAY_AGG(meta->>'key') INTO v_target_columns
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_target_meta) AS meta;

    -- Loop through every column on the target table
    FOR v_column_meta IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_target_meta)
    LOOP
        v_col_name := v_column_meta->>'key';
        
        -- Skip auto-generated columns
        IF v_col_name IN ('id', 'display_id') THEN
            CONTINUE;
        END IF;

        -- THE "MAGIC": Automatically copy 1:1 matching fields
        v_parent_key_top_level := 'new.' || v_col_name;
        v_parent_key_in_details := 'new.details.' || v_col_name;

        IF p_parent_flat_data ? v_parent_key_top_level THEN
            v_parent_value := p_parent_flat_data->v_parent_key_top_level;
        ELSIF p_parent_flat_data ? v_parent_key_in_details THEN
            v_parent_value := p_parent_flat_data->v_parent_key_in_details;
        ELSE
            v_parent_value := NULL;
        END IF;

        IF v_parent_value IS NOT NULL AND v_parent_value::text != 'null' THEN
             v_prefilled_payload := jsonb_set(v_prefilled_payload, ARRAY[v_col_name], v_parent_value);
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    -- FALLBACK: Explicitly set parent links, but ONLY IF the target table has those columns
    IF 'parent_entity_id' = ANY(v_target_columns) THEN
        IF NOT (v_prefilled_payload ? 'parent_entity_id') THEN
            v_prefilled_payload := jsonb_set(v_prefilled_payload, ARRAY['parent_entity_id'], to_jsonb(p_parent_id));
        END IF;
    END IF;
    
    IF 'parent_entity_type' = ANY(v_target_columns) THEN
        IF NOT (v_prefilled_payload ? 'parent_entity_type') THEN
            v_prefilled_payload := jsonb_set(v_prefilled_payload, ARRAY['parent_entity_type'], to_jsonb(p_parent_table));
        END IF;
    END IF;
    
    IF 'parent_entity_schema' = ANY(v_target_columns) THEN
        IF NOT (v_prefilled_payload ? 'parent_entity_schema') THEN
             v_prefilled_payload := jsonb_set(v_prefilled_payload, ARRAY['parent_entity_schema'], to_jsonb(p_parent_schema));
        END IF;
    END IF;
    
    RAISE DEBUG '[run_util_prefill_payload] Final prefilled payload for %.%: %', p_target_schema, p_target_table, v_prefilled_payload;
    
    RETURN v_prefilled_payload;

EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    RAISE WARNING '[run_util_prefill_payload] Error during prefill: %', SQLERRM;
    RETURN '{}'::jsonb;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: run_util_resolve_template(jsonb, jsonb, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.run_util_resolve_template(p_template jsonb, p_flat_data jsonb, p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_key TEXT;
    v_value JSONB;
    v_output_obj JSONB := '{}'::jsonb;
    v_output_arr JSONB := '[]'::jsonb;
    v_val_text TEXT;
    v_placeholder_path TEXT;
    v_resolved_text TEXT;
    v_resolved_datetime TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_interval_expr TEXT;
    v_enum_parts TEXT[];
    v_enum_id UUID;
    v_placeholder RECORD;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Unified placeholder resolution supporting recursive structures
    -- Handles: {{new.field}}, {{NOW()}}, {{NOW() + interval}}, {{ENUM:type:value}}
    -- Preserves: All legacy v1 + v2 functionality in single system
    
    IF p_template IS NULL THEN 
        RETURN 'null'::jsonb; 
    END IF;

    -- Recursive object processing
    IF jsonb_typeof(p_template) = 'object' THEN
        FOR v_key, v_value IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_template) 
        LOOP
            v_output_obj := v_output_obj || jsonb_build_object(
                v_key, 
                automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_value, p_flat_data, p_organization_id)
            );
        END LOOP;
        RETURN v_output_obj;
    
    -- Recursive array processing  
    ELSIF jsonb_typeof(p_template) = 'array' THEN
        FOR v_value IN SELECT value FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_template) 
        LOOP
            v_output_arr := v_output_arr || jsonb_build_array(
                automation.run_util_resolve_template(v_value, p_flat_data, p_organization_id)
            );
        END LOOP;
        RETURN v_output_arr;

    -- Base case: string resolution
    ELSIF jsonb_typeof(p_template) = 'string' THEN
        v_val_text := p_template #>> '{}';
        v_placeholder_path := trim(both '{{}}' FROM v_val_text);

        -- Single placeholder: {{...}}
        IF v_val_text LIKE '{{%}}' AND v_placeholder_path NOT LIKE '%{{%' AND v_placeholder_path NOT LIKE '%}}%' THEN
            -- NOW() and time expressions
            IF v_placeholder_path = 'NOW()' THEN
                EXECUTE 'SELECT NOW()' INTO v_resolved_datetime;
                RETURN to_jsonb(v_resolved_datetime);
            ELSIF v_placeholder_path LIKE 'NOW() + %' THEN
                BEGIN
                    v_interval_expr := substring(v_placeholder_path from 8);
                    EXECUTE 'SELECT NOW() ' || v_interval_expr INTO v_resolved_datetime;
                    RETURN to_jsonb(v_resolved_datetime);
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN 
                    RETURN 'null'::jsonb; 
                END;
            -- ENUM resolution
            ELSIF v_placeholder_path LIKE 'ENUM:%' THEN
                v_enum_parts := string_to_array(substring(v_placeholder_path from 6), ':');
                BEGIN
                    SELECT id INTO v_enum_id FROM core.enums
                    WHERE organization_id = p_organization_id 
                      AND value_type = v_enum_parts[1] 
                      AND value = v_enum_parts[2];
                    IF FOUND THEN 
                        RETURN to_jsonb(v_enum_id);
                    ELSE 
                        RETURN 'null'::jsonb; 
                    END IF;
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN 
                    RETURN 'null'::jsonb; 
                END;
            -- Data placeholder: {{new.field}}
            ELSE
                v_resolved_text := p_flat_data->>v_placeholder_path;
                IF v_resolved_text IS NULL THEN 
                    RETURN 'null'::jsonb;
                ELSE 
                    RETURN to_jsonb(v_resolved_text); 
                END IF;
            END IF;
        
        -- Complex string with multiple placeholders: "Hello {{new.name}}"
        ELSIF v_val_text LIKE '%{{%}}%' THEN
            FOR v_placeholder IN 
                SELECT DISTINCT (regexp_matches(v_val_text, '{{([^{}]+)}}', 'g'))[1] AS path 
            LOOP
                v_placeholder_path := v_placeholder.path;
                v_resolved_text := p_flat_data->>v_placeholder_path;
                v_val_text := replace(v_val_text, '{{' || v_placeholder_path || '}}', COALESCE(v_resolved_text, ''));
            END LOOP;
            RETURN to_jsonb(v_val_text);
        ELSE
            -- Not a placeholder, return as is
            RETURN p_template;
        END IF;
    ELSE
        -- Not a container or string (number, boolean, null), return as is
        RETURN p_template;
    END IF;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: smart_assign_owner(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.smart_assign_owner(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    -- Configuration values
    v_table_name TEXT := p_action_config->>'table';
    v_schema_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'schema', 'public'); -- Default to public schema
    v_field_to_update TEXT := p_action_config->>'field';
    v_assignment_config JSONB := p_action_config->'assignment';
    v_condition JSONB := p_action_config->'condition'; -- e.g., {"field": "assignee_id", "must_be": "NULL"}

    -- Dynamic values
    v_entity_id UUID; -- ID of the record to update
    v_flattened_data JSONB;
    v_assigned_user_id UUID;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid; -- Get org_id from context

    -- SQL construction
    v_sql TEXT;
    v_where_clause TEXT;
    v_update_successful BOOLEAN := false;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate config
    IF v_table_name IS NULL OR v_field_to_update IS NULL OR v_assignment_config IS NULL OR v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_assign_owner] Invalid config: "table", "field", "assignment" required, and organization_id must be in context.';
    END IF;

     -- Determine the entity ID to update (usually from the 'new' record in trigger_data)
     -- Handle potential variations if the action updates a *related* entity
     -- For now, assume it's the primary entity that triggered the event
    v_entity_id := (p_trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
    IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
         RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_assign_owner] Could not determine entity ID from trigger_data->new->>id.';
    END IF;

    v_flattened_data := automation._util_flatten_jsonb(p_trigger_data);

    -- 2. Resolve the user ID using the refactored resolver
    v_assigned_user_id := automation._resolve_assignment(
        v_assignment_config,
        v_flattened_data,
        v_organization_id -- Pass org_id
    );

    -- 3. Execute or skip
    IF v_assigned_user_id IS NULL THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'skipped',
            'reason', 'No eligible user found for assignment.'
        );
    END IF;

    -- Build UPDATE statement
    v_sql := format('UPDATE %I.%I SET %I = %L',
        v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_field_to_update, v_assigned_user_id);
    v_where_clause := format(' WHERE id = %L', v_entity_id);

    -- Add optional condition (e.g., only update if field IS NULL)
    IF v_condition IS NOT NULL AND (v_condition->>'must_be') = 'NULL' AND v_condition->>'field' IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_where_clause := v_where_clause || format(' AND %I IS NULL', v_condition->>'field');
    -- TODO: Add more conditions if needed (e.g., must_not_be)
    END IF;

    -- Prevent trigger loops if updating the same table that caused the event
    IF v_schema_name = (p_context->>'trigger_schema') AND v_table_name = (p_context->>'trigger_table') THEN
         PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'true', false);
    END IF;

    -- Execute the update
    BEGIN
        EXECUTE v_sql || v_where_clause;
        -- GET DIAGNOSTICS v_update_successful = FOUND; -- Check if any row was actually updated
        v_update_successful := FOUND; -- Check if any row was actually updated
    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
         PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false); -- Reset flag on error
         RAISE; -- Re-raise the original error
    END;
    PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false); -- Reset flag after execution

    -- 4. Return context
    IF v_update_successful THEN
      RETURN jsonb_build_object(
          'outcome', 'success',
          'assigned_user_id', v_assigned_user_id,
          'updated_entity_id', v_entity_id
      );
    ELSE
      RETURN jsonb_build_object(
          'outcome', 'skipped',
          'reason', 'Target entity condition not met or entity not found.',
          'assigned_user_id', v_assigned_user_id,
          'target_entity_id', v_entity_id
      );
    END IF;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION smart_assign_owner(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.smart_assign_owner(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) IS 'Assigns a user ID to a specified field on an entity based on assignment rules (e.g., round-robin), using _resolve_assignment.';


--
-- Name: smart_manage_tags(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.smart_manage_tags(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_schema_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'schema', 'public');
    v_table_name TEXT := p_action_config->>'table';
    v_column_name TEXT := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'column', 'tags'); -- Default column 'tags'
    v_tags_to_add JSONB := p_action_config->'add'; -- Expects ["tag1", "tag2"]
    v_tags_to_remove JSONB := p_action_config->'remove'; -- Expects ["tag3"]
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_sql TEXT;
    v_current_tags JSONB;
    v_final_tags JSONB;
    v_rows_affected INT := 0;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Validate config
    IF v_table_name IS NULL OR (v_tags_to_add IS NULL AND v_tags_to_remove IS NULL) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_manage_tags] Invalid config: "table" and at least one of "add" or "remove" arrays are required.';
    END IF;
    IF v_tags_to_add IS NOT NULL AND jsonb_typeof(v_tags_to_add) != 'array' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_manage_tags] "add" must be a JSON array of strings.';
    END IF;
    IF v_tags_to_remove IS NOT NULL AND jsonb_typeof(v_tags_to_remove) != 'array' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_manage_tags] "remove" must be a JSON array of strings.';
    END IF;

    -- 2. Get Entity ID
    v_entity_id := (p_trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
    IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_manage_tags] Could not determine entity ID from trigger_data->new->>id.';
    END IF;

    -- 3. Fetch current tags (handle NULL)
    BEGIN
        EXECUTE format('SELECT COALESCE(%I, ''[]''::jsonb) FROM %I.%I WHERE id = %L',
            v_column_name, v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_entity_id)
        INTO v_current_tags;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN
         RAISE EXCEPTION '[smart_manage_tags] Column "%" does not exist on table "%"."%".', v_column_name, v_schema_name, v_table_name;
         -- Handle other potential errors during fetch
    END;

    -- Ensure current_tags is an array
    IF jsonb_typeof(v_current_tags) != 'array' THEN
         RAISE WARNING '[smart_manage_tags] Existing value in column "%" is not a JSONB array. Initializing as empty array. Entity ID: %', v_column_name, v_entity_id;
         v_current_tags := '[]'::jsonb;
    END IF;

    -- 4. Calculate final tags
    v_final_tags := v_current_tags;
    -- Add tags (ensuring uniqueness)
    IF v_tags_to_add IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT jsonb_agg(DISTINCT tag)
        INTO v_final_tags
        FROM (
            SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(v_final_tags) AS tag
            UNION
            SELECT jsonb_array_elements_text(v_tags_to_add) AS tag
        ) AS combined_tags;
        v_final_tags := COALESCE(v_final_tags, '[]'::jsonb); -- Ensure it's an array if result is NULL
    END IF;

    -- Remove tags
    IF v_tags_to_remove IS NOT NULL THEN
         v_final_tags := (
            SELECT COALESCE(jsonb_agg(elem), '[]'::jsonb)
            FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_final_tags) elem
            WHERE NOT (elem IN (SELECT jsonb_array_elements(v_tags_to_remove)))
        );
    END IF;

    -- 5. Update only if tags changed
    IF v_final_tags IS DISTINCT FROM v_current_tags THEN
        v_sql := format('UPDATE %I.%I SET %I = %L WHERE id = %L',
            v_schema_name, v_table_name, v_column_name, v_final_tags, v_entity_id);

        -- Prevent trigger loops
        IF v_schema_name = (p_context->>'trigger_schema') AND v_table_name = (p_context->>'trigger_table') THEN
             PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'true', false);
        END IF;

        BEGIN
            EXECUTE v_sql;
            GET DIAGNOSTICS v_rows_affected = ROW_COUNT;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
             PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false); -- Reset flag
             RAISE; -- Re-raise
        END;
        PERFORM set_config('my.workflow.is_automation', 'false', false); -- Reset flag

    END IF; -- End if tags changed

    -- 6. Return context
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', CASE WHEN v_rows_affected > 0 THEN 'success' ELSE 'no_change' END,
        'updated_entity_id', v_entity_id,
        'final_tags', v_final_tags
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION smart_manage_tags(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.smart_manage_tags(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) IS 'Adds or removes tags (JSONB array elements) from a specified column on an entity.';

--
-- Name: soi_log_performance_heartbeat(uuid, uuid, text, numeric, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.soi_log_performance_heartbeat(p_org_id uuid, p_blueprint_id uuid, p_metric_key text, p_metric_value numeric, p_unit text DEFAULT NULL::text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_baseline NUMERIC;
    v_deviation NUMERIC;
    v_is_anomaly BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_id UUID;
BEGIN
    SELECT metric_value INTO v_baseline
    FROM automation.process_performance_heartbeat
    WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND metric_key = p_metric_key
    ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT 1;
    
    IF v_baseline IS NOT NULL AND v_baseline != 0 THEN
        v_deviation := ((p_metric_value - v_baseline) / v_baseline) * 100;
        IF ABS(v_deviation) > 20 THEN 
            v_is_anomaly := TRUE;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    INSERT INTO automation.process_performance_heartbeat (
        organization_id, blueprint_id, metric_key, metric_value, unit,
        is_anomaly, baseline_value, deviation_percent
    ) VALUES (
        p_org_id, p_blueprint_id, p_metric_key, p_metric_value, p_unit,
        v_is_anomaly, v_baseline, v_deviation
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_id;
    RETURN v_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: soi_log_run_trace(uuid, uuid, text, text, jsonb, jsonb, jsonb, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
