CREATE FUNCTION automation.soi_log_run_trace(p_org_id uuid, p_trace_id uuid, p_step_name text, p_model_name text, p_reasoning jsonb, p_observation jsonb, p_action jsonb, p_latency integer DEFAULT NULL::integer) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_id UUID;
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO automation.run_traces (
        organization_id, trace_id, step_name, model_name, 
        reasoning_process, observation, action_taken, latency_ms
    ) VALUES (
        p_org_id, p_trace_id, p_step_name, p_model_name, 
        p_reasoning, p_observation, p_action, p_latency
    ) RETURNING id INTO v_id;
    RETURN v_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: soi_toxicity_check(uuid, uuid, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.soi_toxicity_check(p_org_id uuid, p_trace_id uuid, p_text text) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_is_toxic BOOLEAN;
    v_id UUID;
BEGIN
    v_is_toxic := p_text ~* 'badword'; -- SIMPLE MOCK REGEX
    
    IF v_is_toxic THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.toxicity_guardrails (
            organization_id, trace_id, guardrail_type, severity, 
            input_text, violation_details, action_enforced
        ) VALUES (
            p_org_id, p_trace_id, 'toxicity', 'high', 
            p_text, '{"reason": "Mock toxicity detected"}'::jsonb, 'block'
        ) RETURNING id INTO v_id;
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'FAIL', 'id', v_id);
    END IF;
    
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('status', 'PASS');
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: soi_validate_capacity_gaps(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.soi_validate_capacity_gaps(p_project_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS TABLE(skill text, hours_needed numeric, hours_available numeric, shortfall_hours numeric)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
BEGIN
    RETURN QUERY
    WITH TaskHours AS (
        SELECT 
            unnest(required_skills) as req_skill,
            SUM(COALESCE(duration_planned_minutes, 0)) / 60.0 as needed
        FROM unified.tasks
        WHERE (p_project_id IS NULL OR project_id = p_project_id)
          AND status IN ('open', 'planned')
        GROUP BY 1
    ),
    ContactHours AS (
        SELECT 
            unnest(skills) as avail_skill,
            COUNT(*) * 40.0 as available 
        FROM unified.contacts
        WHERE status = 'active'
        GROUP BY 1
    )
    SELECT 
        th.req_skill as skill,
        th.needed::NUMERIC as hours_needed,
        COALESCE(ch.available, 0)::NUMERIC as hours_available,
        (th.needed - COALESCE(ch.available, 0))::NUMERIC as shortfall_hours
    FROM TaskHours th
    LEFT JOIN ContactHours ch ON th.req_skill = ch.avail_skill
    WHERE (th.needed - COALESCE(ch.available, 0)) > 0;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: sys_trg_snapshot_process_blueprint_history(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.sys_trg_snapshot_process_blueprint_history() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
BEGIN
    IF TG_OP = 'DELETE' THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.bp_process_blueprints_history (
            blueprint_id, entity_type, entity_schema, data, version, created_by, intent
        ) VALUES (
            OLD.id, OLD.entity_type, OLD.entity_schema, to_jsonb(OLD.*), OLD.version, OLD.updated_by, OLD.intent
        );
        RETURN OLD;
    ELSIF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
        -- Only snapshot if something meaningful changed (definition, metadata, intent)
        IF (OLD.definition != NEW.definition OR OLD.metadata != NEW.metadata OR OLD.intent IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.intent) THEN
            INSERT INTO automation.bp_process_blueprints_history (
                blueprint_id, entity_type, entity_schema, data, version, created_by, intent
            ) VALUES (
                OLD.id, OLD.entity_type, OLD.entity_schema, to_jsonb(OLD.*), OLD.version, OLD.updated_by, OLD.intent
            );
            NEW.version := OLD.version + 1;
            NEW.updated_at := now();
        END IF;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;
    RETURN NULL;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: tel_event(jsonb, text, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.tel_event(p_events jsonb, p_name text, p_attrs jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE sql
    AS $$
    SELECT p_events || jsonb_build_array(jsonb_build_object(
        'name', p_name,
        'ts',   to_char(clock_timestamp(), 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.USOF'),
        'attrs', p_attrs
    ));
$$;


--
-- Name: tel_flush(uuid, text, timestamp with time zone, text, uuid, text, text, jsonb, jsonb, text, uuid, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.tel_flush(p_trace_id uuid, p_name text, p_start_time timestamp with time zone, p_status text, p_org_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_entity_schema text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_entity_type text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_attributes jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, p_events jsonb DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, p_error text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_parent_span uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_span_kind text DEFAULT 'SERVER'::text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
-- Flush the accumulated span to telemetry_log (1 INSERT)
-- p_trace_id = NEW.id (entity's shared UUID, same as bp_instance.id, esm_instance.id)
    v_span_id UUID := gen_random_uuid();
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO automation.telemetry_log (
        trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, name, span_kind,
        start_time, end_time, duration_ms, status_code,
        organization_id, entity_schema, entity_type,
        attributes, events, error_message
    ) VALUES (
        p_trace_id, v_span_id, p_parent_span, p_name, p_span_kind,
        p_start_time, clock_timestamp(),
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - p_start_time)) * 1000,
        p_status,
        p_org_id, p_entity_schema, p_entity_type,
        p_attributes, p_events, p_error
    );
    RETURN v_span_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: tel_flush(uuid, text, timestamp with time zone, text, uuid, uuid, text, jsonb, jsonb, text, uuid, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.tel_flush(p_trace_id uuid, p_name text, p_start_time timestamp with time zone, p_status text, p_org_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_entity_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_entity_type text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_attributes jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb, p_events jsonb DEFAULT '[]'::jsonb, p_error text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_parent_span uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_span_kind text DEFAULT 'SERVER'::text) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_span_id UUID := gen_random_uuid();
BEGIN
    INSERT INTO automation.telemetry_log (
        trace_id, span_id, parent_span_id, name, span_kind,
        start_time, end_time, duration_ms, status_code,
        organization_id, entity_id, entity_type,
        attributes, events, error_message
    ) VALUES (
        p_trace_id, v_span_id, p_parent_span, p_name, p_span_kind,
        p_start_time, clock_timestamp(),
        EXTRACT(EPOCH FROM (clock_timestamp() - p_start_time)) * 1000,
        p_status,
        p_org_id, p_entity_id, p_entity_type,
        p_attributes, p_events, p_error
    );
    RETURN v_span_id;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: tel_inspect(text, text, integer); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.tel_inspect(p_schema text, p_table text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 1) RETURNS TABLE(trace_id uuid, function_name text, duration_ms real, status_code text, event_timeline text[], attributes jsonb, error_message text, started_at timestamp with time zone)
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
-- Inspect telemetry for the latest record in any table
-- Usage: SELECT * FROM automation.tel_inspect('workforce', 'leave_applications');
--        SELECT * FROM automation.tel_inspect('crm', 'contacts');
--        SELECT * FROM automation.tel_inspect('workforce', 'leave_applications', 5);  -- last 5 records
    v_ids UUID[];
BEGIN
    -- Get the latest N record IDs from the source table
    EXECUTE format(
        'SELECT ARRAY(SELECT id FROM %I.%I ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT %s)',
        p_schema, p_table, p_limit
    ) INTO v_ids;
 
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT
        t.trace_id,
        t.name,
        t.duration_ms,
        t.status_code,
        ARRAY(SELECT jsonb_array_elements(t.events)->>'name'),
        t.attributes,
        t.error_message,
        t.start_time
    FROM automation.telemetry_log t
    WHERE t.trace_id = ANY(v_ids)
    ORDER BY t.trace_id, t.start_time;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: test_send_email_v3(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.test_send_email_v3() RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_url text;
    v_service_key text;
    v_from_email text;
    v_job_id bigint;

    v_status integer;
    v_content text;
    v_error text;
    v_timeout boolean;
BEGIN
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- 1) Load secrets + config
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT secret INTO v_service_key
    FROM vault.secrets
    WHERE name = 'SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY';

    SELECT value INTO v_from_email
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'RESEND_FROM_EMAIL';

    -- [019b] Load URL from app_config
    SELECT value INTO v_url
    FROM public.app_config
    WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';

    IF v_url IS NULL THEN
        v_url := 'https://yfqpniqzyesnvriewysj.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
    END IF;

    IF v_service_key IS NULL THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'error', 'Missing vault secret: SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY',
            'fix', 'Run: SELECT vault.create_secret(''your-service-role-key'', ''SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY'');'
        );
    END IF;

    IF v_from_email IS NULL THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object(
            'error', 'Missing app_config: RESEND_FROM_EMAIL',
            'fix', 'INSERT INTO public.app_config (name, value) VALUES (''RESEND_FROM_EMAIL'', ''noreply@yourdomain.com'');'
        );
    END IF;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- 2) MAKE CLEAN TEST HTTP CALL
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT net.http_post(
        url := v_url,
        headers := jsonb_build_object(
            'Content-Type', 'application/json',
            'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_service_key
        ),
        body := jsonb_build_array(
            jsonb_build_object(
                'from', v_from_email,
                'to', jsonb_build_array('ravi@claritiz.com'),
                'subject', 'Test Email From SQL (pg_net clean test)',
                'html', '<h1>Hello from pg_net</h1><p>This is a test.</p>'
            )
        )
    )
    INTO v_job_id;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- 3) Wait for async pg_net response
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    PERFORM pg_sleep(1);

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- 4) Read response
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    SELECT
        status_code,
        content,
        error_msg,
        timed_out
    INTO
        v_status,
        v_content,
        v_error,
        v_timeout
    FROM net._http_response
    WHERE id = v_job_id;

    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    -- 5) Return test results
    --------------------------------------------------------------------
    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'job_id', v_job_id,
        'url_used', v_url,
        'status_code', v_status,
        'error', v_error,
        'timed_out', v_timeout,
        'content', v_content
    );
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: trg_invoke_soi_orchestrator(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.trg_invoke_soi_orchestrator() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
  v_url text;
  v_key text;
  v_depth int;
BEGIN
  -- Safe access to soi_depth via jsonb to handle different tables
  v_depth := COALESCE((to_jsonb(NEW)->>'soi_depth')::int, 0);

  -- Harden against missing settings
  BEGIN
    v_url := current_setting('app.settings.supabase_url', true) || '/functions/v1/soi-orchestrator';
    v_key := current_setting('app.settings.service_role_key', true);
    
    -- current_setting with 'true' as second arg returns NULL instead of throwing error
    IF v_url IS NULL OR v_key IS NULL THEN
       RAISE WARNING '[trg_invoke_soi_orchestrator] Missing Supabase settings. Skipping edge function call.';
       RETURN NEW;
    END IF;
  EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    RAISE WARNING '[trg_invoke_soi_orchestrator] Failed to fetch settings: %', SQLERRM;
    RETURN NEW;
  END;

  IF v_depth > 5 THEN
    RAISE WARNING 'SOI Orchestrator recursion depth limit reached (5) for instance %', NEW.id;
    RETURN NEW;
  END IF;

  PERFORM
    net.http_post(
      url := v_url,
      headers := jsonb_build_object(
        'Content-Type', 'application/json',
        'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_key
      ),
      body := jsonb_build_object(
        'instance_id', NEW.id,
        'trigger_event', TG_OP,
        'previous_stage', OLD.stage_id,
        'soi_depth', v_depth + 1
      )
    );
    
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: trg_sys_log_esm_activity(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.trg_sys_log_esm_activity() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
 DECLARE
     v_is_unified BOOLEAN;
 BEGIN
     -- 1. Check if the entity is registered in Tier 0 (Unified Tier)
     -- This prevents 'Object Not Found' errors for Transactional/Definition entities
     SELECT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM core.unified_objects WHERE id = NEW.entity_id) INTO v_is_unified;

     IF v_is_unified AND NEW.transition_type = 'transition' THEN
         PERFORM core.sys_log_activity(
             NEW.entity_id,
             'esm_transition', -- Action label
             jsonb_build_object(
                 'from_stage', NEW.from_stage_id,
                 'to_stage', NEW.to_stage_id,
                 'transition_type', NEW.transition_type,
                 'esm_instance_id', NEW.esm_instance_id,
                 'entity_schema', NEW.entity_schema,
                 'entity_type', NEW.entity_type
             )
         );
     END IF;
     RETURN NEW;
 END;$$;


--
-- Name: FUNCTION trg_sys_log_esm_activity(); Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON FUNCTION automation.trg_sys_log_esm_activity() IS 'v4: Bridges ESM lifecycle logs to the global core activity feed.';


--
-- Name: trigger_capture_event(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.trigger_capture_event() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    AS $$
DECLARE
    -- Standardize Event Types to match System Format: "table_name.created"
    -- This matches what the Rule Engine expects.
    v_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_table TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_entity_path TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA || '.' || TG_TABLE_NAME;
    
    v_event_type TEXT;
    v_payload JSONB;
    v_organization_id UUID;
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_record_to_use RECORD;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Recursion Guard
    -- Prevent infinite loops if an Automation Rule updates the entity itself.
    IF current_setting('my.workflow.is_automation', true) = 'true' THEN
        IF TG_OP = 'DELETE' THEN RETURN OLD; ELSE RETURN NEW; END IF;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Operation Handling (Safe Record Access)
    IF TG_OP = 'INSERT' THEN
        v_record_to_use := NEW;
        v_event_type := v_table || '.created';
        v_payload := jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW));
        
    ELSIF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' THEN
        v_record_to_use := NEW;
        v_event_type := v_table || '.updated';
        -- We calculate diffs dynamically if needed, or just store old/new
        v_payload := jsonb_build_object(
            'new', to_jsonb(NEW), 
            'old', to_jsonb(OLD)
            -- Optional: Add 'changes' calculation here if 'wff_utils_jsonb_diff' is migrated to automation
            -- 'changes', automation.util_jsonb_diff(to_jsonb(OLD), to_jsonb(NEW)) 
        );
        
    ELSIF TG_OP = 'DELETE' THEN
        v_record_to_use := OLD;
        v_event_type := v_table || '.deleted';
        v_payload := jsonb_build_object('old', to_jsonb(OLD));
        
    ELSE
        RETURN NULL; -- Should not happen
    END IF;

    -- 3. Extract Common Data
    -- We assume the table has 'id' and 'organization_id'
    v_entity_id := v_record_to_use.id;
    v_organization_id := v_record_to_use.organization_id;

    -- 4. Insert into the Unified Event Bus
    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
        id,
        organization_id,
        trigger_schema,
        trigger_table,
        trigger_type,
        trigger_data,
        context_entity_id,
        context_entity_type,
        status
    ) VALUES (
        gen_random_uuid(),
        v_organization_id,
        v_schema,
        v_table,
        v_event_type,
        v_payload,
        v_entity_id,
        v_entity_path,
        'pending'
    );

    -- 5. Return Control
    IF TG_OP = 'DELETE' THEN RETURN OLD; ELSE RETURN NEW; END IF;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: util_get_stage_category(uuid, text); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.util_get_stage_category(p_esm_definition_id uuid, p_stage_id text) RETURNS text
    LANGUAGE plpgsql STABLE
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_category text;
BEGIN
    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_category
    FROM automation.esm_definitions,
         jsonb_array_elements(definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE id = p_esm_definition_id
      AND s->>'id' = p_stage_id;
    
    RETURN v_category;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: utils_backfill_esm_logs(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.utils_backfill_esm_logs() RETURNS integer
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    deleted_count INTEGER;
    inserted_count INTEGER;
BEGIN
    -- First, delete any previous backfill entries to avoid duplicates
    WITH deleted AS (
        DELETE FROM automation.esm_logs 
        WHERE metadata->>'automation_trigger' = 'initial_backfill'
        RETURNING 1
    )
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO deleted_count FROM deleted;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Deleted % previous backfill records', deleted_count;
    
    -- Then insert fresh backfill entries
    WITH initial_logs AS (
        INSERT INTO automation.esm_logs (
            organization_id, esm_instance_id, entity_id, entity_schema, entity_type,
            from_stage_id, to_stage_id, transition_type, metadata
        )
        SELECT 
            organization_id, id, entity_id, entity_schema, entity_type,
            NULL, stage_id, 'entry',
            jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_id', blueprint_id,
                'blueprint_version', blueprint_version,
                'automation_trigger', 'initial_backfill',
                'backfilled_at', NOW()
            )
        FROM automation.esm_instances
        WHERE stage_id IS NOT NULL
        RETURNING 1
    )
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO inserted_count FROM initial_logs;
    
    RETURN inserted_count;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: utils_get_blueprint_artifacts(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.utils_get_blueprint_artifacts(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_artifacts JSONB;
    v_active_version INT;
BEGIN
    -- Get the currently active version from the main blueprint table
    SELECT "version" INTO v_active_version
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint with ID % not found.', p_blueprint_id;
    END IF;

    -- Aggregate all active artifacts for this blueprint
    SELECT jsonb_build_object(
        'blueprint_id', p_blueprint_id,
        'active_version', v_active_version,
        
        'esm_definitions', COALESCE(
            (SELECT jsonb_agg(ed)
             FROM automation.esm_definitions ed
             WHERE ed.blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND ed.is_active = true),
            '[]'::jsonb
        ),
        
        'rules', COALESCE(
            (SELECT jsonb_agg(wr)
             FROM automation.wf_rules wr
             WHERE wr.blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND wr.is_active = true),
            '[]'::jsonb
        ),
        
        'actions', COALESCE(
            (SELECT jsonb_agg(wa)
             FROM automation.wf_actions wa
             WHERE wa.rule_id IN (
                SELECT id FROM automation.wf_rules 
                WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND is_active = true
             )),
            '[]'::jsonb
        ),
        
        'scheduled_jobs', COALESCE(
            (SELECT jsonb_agg(sj)
             FROM automation.wf_scheduled_jobs sj
             WHERE sj.rule_id IN (
                SELECT id FROM automation.wf_rules 
                WHERE blueprint_id = p_blueprint_id AND is_active = true
             )),
            '[]'::jsonb
        )
    )
    INTO v_artifacts;

    RETURN v_artifacts;
END;
$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_artifacts_esm_v1(uuid, jsonb, integer, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_artifacts_esm_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_definition jsonb, p_version integer, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_esm_def_id UUID; 
    v_esm_definitions_json JSONB; 
    v_stage JSONB; 
    v_stages JSONB[] := ARRAY[]::JSONB[];
    v_seq INT := 1; 
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema'; 
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type';
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_metadata JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Compiles ESM definitions from blueprint lifecycle configuration
    -- 2. Generates stage entry/exit event names and sequencing
    -- 3. Uses utility functions for consistent metadata creation
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile
    
    -- Process each stage to add event names and sequencing
    FOR v_stage IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') LOOP
        v_stage := v_stage || jsonb_build_object(
            'on_entry_event_name', format('%s.%s.stage.%s.entered', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage->>'id'),
            'on_exit_event_name', format('%s.%s.stage.%s.exited', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage->>'id'),
            'sequence', v_seq
        );
        v_stages := array_append(v_stages, v_stage);
        v_seq := v_seq + 1;
    END LOOP;
    
    -- Build complete ESM definitions JSON
    v_esm_definitions_json := jsonb_build_object(
        'name', p_definition->>'name', 
        'entity_type', v_entity_type, 
        'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
        'startStateId', p_definition->'lifecycle'->>'start_stage', 
        'stages', to_jsonb(v_stages),
        'transitions', p_definition->'lifecycle'->'transitions'
    );
    
    -- Build standardized metadata
    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
        p_blueprint_id, 
        p_version, 
        NULL, -- esm_def_id not available yet
        NULL, -- no specific context
        jsonb_build_object('blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name)
    );
    
    -- Create ESM definition record
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_definitions (
        organization_id, name, entity_schema, entity_type, definitions, version, 
        is_active, blueprint_id, metadata
    )
    VALUES (
        p_organization_id, 
        p_definition->>'name' || ' - ESM v' || p_version::TEXT, 
        v_entity_schema, 
        v_entity_type,
        v_esm_definitions_json, 
        p_version, 
        true, -- Create as active
        p_blueprint_id, 
        v_metadata
    )
    RETURNING id INTO v_esm_def_id;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Created ESM definition: % for blueprint %', v_esm_def_id, p_blueprint_id;
    
    RETURN v_esm_def_id;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_artifacts_rules_actions_v1(uuid, jsonb, integer, uuid, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_artifacts_rules_actions_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_definition jsonb, p_version integer, p_esm_def_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_automations JSONB; 
    v_stage_automations JSONB; 
    v_event_automations JSONB; 
    v_stage_name TEXT; 
    v_stage_config JSONB; 
    v_actions JSONB; 
    v_action JSONB; 
    v_rule_id UUID;
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema'; 
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type'; 
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_trigger_schema TEXT; 
    v_trigger_table TEXT; 
    v_trigger_type TEXT; 
    v_rule_name TEXT;
    v_conditions JSONB; 
    v_condition_type TEXT; 
    v_rule_count INT := 0; 
    v_action_count INT := 0; 
    v_event_name TEXT; 
    v_event_config JSONB; 
    v_action_priority INT;
    v_metadata JSONB;
    v_action_conditions JSONB;
    v_final_action_config JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Compiles rules and actions from blueprint automations with heavy optimization
    -- 2. Uses utility functions for condition extraction, template merging, and metadata creation
    -- 3. Eliminates 100+ lines of duplicated code from original implementation
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile
    
    v_automations := p_definition->'automations';
    IF v_automations IS NULL THEN 
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', 0, 'actions_created', 0); 
    END IF;
    
    -- Process Stage Entry Automations
    v_stage_automations := v_automations->'on_stage_entry';
    IF v_stage_automations IS NOT NULL THEN
        FOR v_stage_name, v_stage_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(v_stage_automations) LOOP
            v_actions := v_stage_config->'actions'; 
            v_conditions := v_stage_config->'conditions';
            v_rule_name := 'Blueprint: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - Stage ' || v_stage_name || ' Entry';
            v_trigger_type := format('%s.%s.stage.%s.entered', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_stage_name);
            
            -- Enhanced condition handling using standardized approach
            IF v_conditions IS NULL OR v_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR v_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN 
                v_conditions := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb; 
            END IF;
            
            -- Build standardized metadata
            v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                p_blueprint_id, 
                p_version, 
                p_esm_def_id,
                'on_stage_entry.' || v_stage_name,
                jsonb_build_object('blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name)
            );
            
            -- Create Rule using utility function
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                'Auto-generated from blueprint for stage entry', 
                v_entity_schema, 
                v_entity_type, 
                v_trigger_type, 
                v_conditions, 
                p_version, 
                10, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            
            -- Create Actions for Stage Entry with optimized utility functions
            FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_action->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_action);
                    
                    -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                    v_final_action_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                        COALESCE(v_action->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                        v_blueprint_name,
                        p_version
                    );
                    
                    -- Build action metadata with condition source tracking
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        'on_stage_entry.' || v_stage_name,
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_action_name', v_action->>'name', 
                            'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                WHEN v_action->'applies_to_categories' IS NOT NULL THEN 'category_conversion'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create Action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'Unnamed Action'), 
                        v_action->>'action_type', 
                        v_final_action_config,
                        v_action_priority, 
                        COALESCE((v_action->>'run_asynchronous')::BOOLEAN, false),
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        COALESCE(v_action->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                        v_action_conditions
                    );
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create action "%" for stage %: %', 
                        v_action->>'name', v_stage_name, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END LOOP;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    -- Process Related Event Automations with same optimized approach
    v_event_automations := v_automations->'on_related_event';
    IF v_event_automations IS NOT NULL THEN
        FOR v_event_name, v_event_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(v_event_automations) LOOP
            v_actions := v_event_config->'actions'; 
            v_rule_name := 'Blueprint: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - Event: ' || v_event_name;
            v_trigger_schema := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_schema'; 
            v_trigger_table  := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_type'; 
            v_trigger_type   := v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'trigger_type';
            
            -- Validate listen_to structure
            IF v_trigger_schema IS NULL OR v_trigger_table IS NULL OR v_trigger_type IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid "listen_to" structure for on_related_event "%".', v_event_name; 
            END IF;
            
            v_conditions := v_event_config->'conditions';
            IF v_conditions IS NULL OR v_conditions = 'null'::jsonb OR v_conditions = '{}'::jsonb THEN 
                v_conditions := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb;
            ELSIF jsonb_typeof(v_conditions) != 'object' OR v_conditions->>'type' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'Condition for on_related_event "%" must be a valid JSONB object.', v_event_name; 
            END IF;
            
            -- Build standardized metadata
            v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                p_blueprint_id, 
                p_version, 
                p_esm_def_id,
                'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
                jsonb_build_object('blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name)
            );
            
            -- Create Rule using utility function
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                'Auto-generated from blueprint for related event', 
                v_trigger_schema, 
                v_trigger_table, 
                v_trigger_type, 
                v_conditions, 
                p_version, 
                10, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            
            -- Create Actions for Related Event with optimized utility functions
            FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_action->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_action);
                    
                    -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                    v_final_action_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                        COALESCE(v_action->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                        v_blueprint_name,
                        p_version
                    );
                    
                    -- Build action metadata with condition source tracking
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        'on_related_event.' || v_event_name,
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_action_name', v_action->>'name', 
                            'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                WHEN v_action->'applies_to_categories' IS NOT NULL THEN 'category_conversion'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create Action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'Unnamed Action'), 
                        v_action->>'action_type', 
                        v_final_action_config,
                        v_action_priority, 
                        COALESCE((v_action->>'run_asynchronous')::BOOLEAN, false),
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        COALESCE(v_action->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                        v_action_conditions
                    );
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create action "%" for event %: %', 
                        v_action->>'name', v_event_name, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END LOOP;
        END LOOP;
    END IF;
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Created % rules and % actions for blueprint %', v_rule_count, v_action_count, p_blueprint_id;
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count);
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs_v1(uuid, jsonb, integer, uuid, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_definition jsonb, p_version integer, p_esm_def_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_sla_rules JSONB;
    v_sla_rule JSONB;
    v_rule_id UUID;
    v_rule_name TEXT;
    v_entity_schema TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_schema';
    v_entity_type TEXT := p_definition->>'entity_type';
    v_blueprint_name TEXT := p_definition->>'name';
    v_monitored_stages JSONB;
    v_time_threshold_hours TEXT;
    v_conditions_jsonb JSONB;
    v_rule_conditions_payload JSONB;
    v_rule_count INT := 0;
    v_action_count INT := 0;
    v_job_count INT := 0;
    v_action_priority INT;
    v_sla_rule_id TEXT;
    v_escalation_level INT;
    v_on_breach_config JSONB;
    v_email_config JSONB;
    v_metadata JSONB;
    v_sla_action_conditions JSONB;
    v_on_breach_conditions JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Compiles SLA rules, actions, and scheduled jobs with heavy optimization
    -- 2. Uses utility functions for condition extraction, template merging, and metadata creation
    -- 3. Eliminates 80+ lines of duplicated code from original implementation
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile
    
    RAISE NOTICE '=== Starting comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs ===';
    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint: %, Version: %, Organization: %', p_blueprint_id, p_version, p_organization_id;
    
    v_sla_rules := p_definition->'sla_rules';
    
    IF v_sla_rules IS NULL OR jsonb_typeof(v_sla_rules) != 'array' OR jsonb_array_length(v_sla_rules) = 0 THEN
        RAISE NOTICE 'No SLA rules to process, returning early';
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', 0, 'actions_created', 0, 'jobs_created', 0);
    END IF;

    FOR v_sla_rule IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_sla_rules) 
    LOOP
        v_sla_rule_id := v_sla_rule->>'id';
        v_rule_name := 'Blueprint SLA: ' || v_blueprint_name || ' - ' || v_sla_rule_id;
        v_monitored_stages := v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages';
        v_time_threshold_hours := COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'time_threshold_hours', '0');
        v_conditions_jsonb := v_sla_rule->'conditions';
        
        RAISE NOTICE 'Processing SLA rule: %', v_sla_rule_id;

        -- Validate monitored_stages
        IF v_monitored_stages IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_monitored_stages) = 0 THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Skipping SLA rule %: `monitored_stages` is missing or empty.', v_sla_rule_id;
            CONTINUE;
        END IF;
        
        -- Handle empty conditions
        IF v_conditions_jsonb IS NULL OR v_conditions_jsonb = 'null'::jsonb THEN
            v_conditions_jsonb := '{"type": "AND", "rules": []}'::jsonb;
        END IF;

        -- Build rule conditions payload
        v_rule_conditions_payload := jsonb_build_object(
            'monitored_stages', v_monitored_stages,
            'time_threshold_hours', v_time_threshold_hours,
            'base_conditions', v_conditions_jsonb,
            'sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id
        );

        -- Calculate escalation level from on_breach configuration
        v_escalation_level := 1;
        v_on_breach_config := v_sla_rule->'on_breach';
        IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
            v_escalation_level := COALESCE(
                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->>'value')::INT,
                1
            );
        END IF;

        -- Build standardized metadata
        v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            p_version, 
            p_esm_def_id,
            NULL, -- no specific context
            jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                'target_escalation_level', v_escalation_level
            )
        );

        -- Create the rule using utility function
        BEGIN
            v_rule_id := automation.comp_utils_create_rule(
                p_organization_id, 
                v_rule_name, 
                COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'description', 'SLA rule for ' || v_sla_rule_id),
                v_entity_schema, 
                v_entity_type,
                'scheduled', 
                v_rule_conditions_payload,
                p_version, 
                20, -- priority
                p_blueprint_id, 
                v_metadata
            );
            
            v_rule_count := v_rule_count + 1;
            RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created rule: % (ID: %)', v_rule_name, v_rule_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create rule for SLA %: %', v_sla_rule_id, SQLERRM;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        -- Create EMAIL action with optimized utility functions
        IF v_sla_rule->'action' IS NOT NULL AND v_sla_rule->'action' != 'null'::jsonb THEN
            BEGIN
                v_action_priority := COALESCE((v_sla_rule->'action'->>'priority')::INT, 0);
                
                -- Use utility function for template variable merging
                v_email_config := automation.comp_utils_merge_template_vars(
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'config', '{}'::jsonb),
                    v_blueprint_name,
                    p_version
                );
                
                -- Add SLA-specific template variables
                v_email_config := jsonb_set(
                    v_email_config,
                    '{template_variables}',
                    COALESCE(v_email_config->'template_variables', '{}'::jsonb) || 
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'entity_id', '{{new.display_id}}',
                        'entity_type', v_entity_type,
                        'escalation_level', v_escalation_level,
                        'sla_rule_name', v_sla_rule_id,
                        'time_threshold', v_time_threshold_hours || ' hours'
                    )
                );
                
                -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                v_sla_action_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_sla_rule->'action');
                
                -- Build action metadata
                v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                    p_blueprint_id, 
                    p_version, 
                    p_esm_def_id,
                    NULL, -- no specific context
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                        'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                        'blueprint_action_name', v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name',
                        'blueprint_action_order', v_action_priority,
                        'condition_source', CASE 
                            WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                            WHEN v_sla_rule->'action'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                            ELSE 'none'
                        END
                    )
                );
                
                -- Create action using utility function
                PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                    v_rule_id, 
                    p_organization_id, 
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->>'name', 'SLA Action for ' || v_sla_rule_id),
                    v_sla_rule->'action'->>'type', 
                    v_email_config, 
                    v_action_priority, 
                    false, -- run_asynchronous
                    p_blueprint_id,
                    v_metadata,
                    COALESCE(v_sla_rule->'action'->'retry_policy', '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb),
                    v_sla_action_conditions
                );
                
                v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created SLA email action with conditions: %', 
                    CASE WHEN v_sla_action_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
            EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create email action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
            END;
        END IF;

        -- Create ON_BREACH update_entity action with optimized utility functions
        IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config != 'null'::jsonb THEN
            IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
                BEGIN
                    -- Use utility function for condition extraction
                    v_on_breach_conditions := automation.comp_utils_extract_conditions(v_on_breach_config->'update_entity');
                    
                    -- Build action metadata
                    v_metadata := automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                        p_blueprint_id, 
                        p_version, 
                        p_esm_def_id,
                        NULL, -- no specific context
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                            'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id,
                            'is_on_breach_action', true,
                            'condition_source', CASE 
                                WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'top_level'
                                WHEN v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN 'nested_config'
                                ELSE 'none'
                            END
                        )
                    );
                    
                    -- Create action using utility function
                    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_create_action(
                        v_rule_id, 
                        p_organization_id, 
                        'Update Entity for SLA: ' || v_sla_rule_id,
                        'update_entity', 
                        jsonb_build_object(
                            'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
                            'entity_name', v_entity_type,
                            'payload', jsonb_build_object(
                                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->>'field'), 
                                (v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'value')
                            )
                        ),
                        COALESCE(v_action_priority, 0) + 1,
                        false, -- run_asynchronous
                        p_blueprint_id,
                        v_metadata,
                        '{"max_retries": 3, "retry_delay": "5 minutes"}'::jsonb,
                        v_on_breach_conditions
                    );
                    
                    v_action_count := v_action_count + 1;
                    RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created on_breach action with conditions: %', 
                        CASE WHEN v_on_breach_conditions IS NOT NULL THEN 'YES' ELSE 'NO' END;
                EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                    RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create on_breach action for SLA rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        -- Create scheduled job
        BEGIN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_scheduled_jobs (
                organization_id, rule_id, job_type, cron_expression, 
                next_run, status, blueprint_id, metadata,
                is_active
            )
            VALUES (
                p_organization_id, v_rule_id, 'SLA_MONITOR', 
                COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'check_frequency', '0 */6 * * *'),
                NOW(), 'pending', p_blueprint_id, 
                automation.comp_utils_build_metadata(
                    p_blueprint_id, 
                    p_version, 
                    p_esm_def_id,
                    NULL, -- no specific context
                    jsonb_build_object(
                        'blueprint_name', v_blueprint_name,
                        'blueprint_sla_rule_id', v_sla_rule_id
                    )
                ),
                true
            );
            
            v_job_count := v_job_count + 1;
            RAISE NOTICE '  -> Created scheduled job for rule %', v_rule_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING 'Failed to create scheduled job for rule %: %', v_rule_id, SQLERRM;
        END;

    END LOOP;

    RAISE NOTICE '=== Completed comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs ===';
    RAISE NOTICE 'Rules: %, Actions: %, Jobs: %', v_rule_count, v_action_count, v_job_count;
    
    RETURN jsonb_build_object('rules_created', v_rule_count, 'actions_created', v_action_count, 'jobs_created', v_job_count);
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_blueprint_v1(uuid, uuid, uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_blueprint_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid, p_organization_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_compiled_by uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$
DECLARE
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_org_id UUID;
BEGIN
    SELECT * INTO v_blueprint FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;
    IF NOT FOUND THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint % not found', p_blueprint_id; END IF;
    
    v_org_id := COALESCE(p_organization_id, v_blueprint.organization_id, '55555555-5555-5555-5555-555555555555'::uuid);

    -- V4.6 Flexible Validation
    IF NOT (
        (v_blueprint.definition->'lifecycle' ? 'start_stage' OR v_blueprint.definition->'lifecycle' ? 'startStateId') 
        AND v_blueprint.definition->'lifecycle' ? 'stages'
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint validation failed: missing start_stage/startStateId or stages';
    END IF;

    -- ESM Artifact Creation
    PERFORM automation.comp_artifacts_esm(v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_org_id, v_blueprint.definition);
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_core_compile_and_activate_v1(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_core_compile_and_activate_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_compilation_id UUID;
    v_version INT;
    v_blueprint_name TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Main entry point for blueprint compilation and activation
    -- 2. Orchestrates the complete compilation-to-activation pipeline
    -- 3. Enhanced error handling and validation throughout the process
    -- 4. Called by: External applications and APIs
    
    -- Step 0: Validate blueprint exists and is valid
    IF NOT EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints WHERE id = p_blueprint_id) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint with ID % not found.', p_blueprint_id;
    END IF;

    -- Retrieve blueprint details for logging
    SELECT version, definition->>'name'
    INTO v_version, v_blueprint_name
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Starting compilation and activation for blueprint: % (ID: %, Version: %)', 
        v_blueprint_name, p_blueprint_id, v_version;

    -- Step 1: Enhanced cleanup using utility function
    PERFORM automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

    -- Step 2: Compile with enhanced validation and optimization
    v_compilation_id := automation.comp_core_compile(p_blueprint_id);

    -- Step 3: Activate with improved conflict handling
    PERFORM automation.comp_core_activate(p_blueprint_id, v_version);

    RAISE NOTICE 'Successfully compiled and activated blueprint: % (Compilation ID: %)', 
        v_blueprint_name, v_compilation_id;

    -- Return compilation ID for tracking
    RETURN v_compilation_id;
    
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint compilation and activation failed for ID %: %', p_blueprint_id, SQLERRM;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_core_compile_v1(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_core_compile_v1(p_blueprint_id uuid) RETURNS uuid
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_blueprint RECORD; 
    v_is_valid BOOLEAN; 
    v_esm_def_id UUID; 
    v_compilation_id UUID := gen_random_uuid();
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp(); 
    v_rules_actions_result JSONB; 
    v_scheduled_jobs_result JSONB;
    v_final_details JSONB; 
    v_compiled_by UUID;
    v_cleanup_result JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Main compilation orchestrator with enhanced optimization
    -- 2. Uses utility functions for cleanup, validation, and artifact compilation
    -- 3. Maintains comprehensive error handling and logging
    -- 4. Called by: comp_core_compile_and_activate
    
    -- 1. Fetch blueprint with enhanced validation
    SELECT * INTO STRICT v_blueprint 
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
    WHERE id = p_blueprint_id;
    
    -- 2. Enhanced blueprint structure validation using utility function
    PERFORM automation.comp_validate_structure(v_blueprint.definition);

    -- 3. Targeted cleanup for this version only using utility function
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleaning up artifacts for Blueprint ID %, Version %...', p_blueprint_id, v_blueprint.version;
    
    v_cleanup_result := automation.comp_utils_clean_artifacts(
        p_blueprint_id, 
        v_blueprint.version, 
        ARRAY['jobs', 'actions', 'rules', 'esm']  -- Clean all artifact types
    );
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Cleanup completed: %', v_cleanup_result;

    -- 4. Proceed with enhanced compilation pipeline
    BEGIN
        -- Validate blueprint definition
        v_is_valid := automation.comp_validate_definition(v_blueprint.definition);
        
        -- Compile ESM definitions
        v_esm_def_id := automation.comp_artifacts_esm(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_blueprint.definition, 
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id
        );
        
        -- Compile rules and actions
        v_rules_actions_result := automation.comp_artifacts_rules_actions(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_blueprint.definition, 
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_esm_def_id, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id
        );
        
        -- Compile scheduled jobs
        v_scheduled_jobs_result := automation.comp_artifacts_scheduled_jobs(
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_blueprint.definition, 
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_esm_def_id, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id
        );
        
        -- Build compilation results
        v_final_details := jsonb_build_object(
            'message', 'Blueprint compiled successfully with enhanced optimization.',
            'artifacts', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_definition_id', v_esm_def_id, 
                'rules_created', v_rules_actions_result->'rules_created',
                'actions_created', v_rules_actions_result->'actions_created', 
                'jobs_created', v_scheduled_jobs_result->'jobs_created',
                'cleanup_result', v_cleanup_result
            )
        );
        
        -- Log successful compilation
        PERFORM automation.comp_utils_log(
            v_compilation_id, 
            p_blueprint_id, 
            v_blueprint.version, 
            v_blueprint.organization_id, 
            'success', 
            v_final_details, 
            v_start_time, 
            clock_timestamp(), 
            v_compiled_by
        );

    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
        -- Build error details
        v_final_details := jsonb_build_object(
            'message', 'Enhanced blueprint compilation failed.', 
            'error', SQLERRM, 
            'detail', SQLSTATE,
            'context', 'comp_core_compile',
            'blueprint_id', p_blueprint_id,
            'blueprint_version', COALESCE(v_blueprint.version, 0)
        );
        
        -- Log compilation failure
        PERFORM automation.comp_utils_log(
            v_compilation_id, 
            p_blueprint_id, 
            COALESCE(v_blueprint.version, 0), 
            COALESCE(v_blueprint.organization_id, '00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000'),
            'failed', 
            v_final_details, 
            v_start_time, 
            clock_timestamp(), 
            v_compiled_by
        );
        RAISE;
    END;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Compilation completed successfully for blueprint %: % rules, % actions, % jobs', 
        p_blueprint_id,
        v_rules_actions_result->'rules_created',
        v_rules_actions_result->'actions_created',
        v_scheduled_jobs_result->'jobs_created';

    RETURN v_compilation_id;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_validate_definition_v1(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_validate_definition_v1(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_stage_key TEXT;
    v_stage_config JSONB;
    v_event_key TEXT;
    v_event_config JSONB;
    v_sla_rule JSONB;
    v_sla_conditions JSONB; 
    v_sla_conditions_type TEXT; 
    v_rule_index INT := 0; 
    v_sla_id TEXT;
    v_action JSONB;
    v_actions JSONB;
    v_on_breach_config JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Comprehensive blueprint definition validation with enhanced condition checking
    -- 2. Validates automations, SLA rules, and action condition structures
    -- 3. Detects conflicting condition definitions and invalid configurations
    -- 4. Used by: comp_core_compile
    
    -- Basic required fields validation
    IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name' AND p_definition ? 'lifecycle' AND p_definition ? 'entity_schema' AND p_definition ? 'entity_type') THEN 
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing required keys: name, lifecycle, entity_schema, entity_type'; 
    END IF;

    -- Lifecycle structure validation
    IF NOT (p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'start_stage' AND p_definition->'lifecycle' ? 'stages' 
            AND jsonb_typeof(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') = 'array' 
            AND jsonb_array_length(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages') > 0) THEN 
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint lifecycle invalid: missing start_stage or stages array or stages array is empty'; 
    END IF;

    -- Validate automations section if present
    IF p_definition->'automations' IS NOT NULL THEN
        -- Validate on_stage_entry automations
        IF p_definition->'automations'->'on_stage_entry' IS NOT NULL THEN
            FOR v_stage_key, v_stage_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_definition->'automations'->'on_stage_entry') LOOP
                -- Validate stage conditions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_stage_config->'conditions', 
                    'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->conditions'
                );
                
                -- Validate stage actions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_actions(
                    v_stage_config->'actions', 
                    'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions'
                );

                -- Enhanced: Validate action-level condition structure conflicts
                v_actions := v_stage_config->'actions';
                IF v_actions IS NOT NULL THEN
                    FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                        -- Detect conflicting condition definitions
                        IF (v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL AND v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL) THEN
                            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Action "%" in on_stage_entry.% has conditions defined in both top-level and config. Choose one location.', 
                                COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed'), v_stage_key;
                        END IF;
                        
                        -- Validate condition structure if present in top-level
                        IF v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                        
                        -- Validate condition structure if present in nested config
                        IF v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'config'->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_stage_entry->' || v_stage_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->config->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
        
        -- Validate on_related_event automations
        IF p_definition->'automations'->'on_related_event' IS NOT NULL THEN
            FOR v_event_key, v_event_config IN SELECT key, value FROM jsonb_each(p_definition->'automations'->'on_related_event') LOOP
                -- Validate listen_to structure
                IF v_event_config->'listen_to' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_schema' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'entity_type' IS NULL 
                   OR v_event_config->'listen_to'->>'trigger_type' IS NULL THEN 
                    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Invalid listen_to structure for event "%": missing entity_schema, entity_type, or trigger_type', v_event_key; 
                END IF;

                -- Validate event conditions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_event_config->'conditions', 
                    'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->conditions'
                );
                
                -- Validate event actions
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_actions(
                    v_event_config->'actions', 
                    'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions'
                );

                -- Enhanced: Validate action-level condition structure conflicts
                v_actions := v_event_config->'actions';
                IF v_actions IS NOT NULL THEN
                    FOR v_action IN SELECT * FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_actions) LOOP
                        IF (v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL AND v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL) THEN
                            RAISE EXCEPTION 'Action "%" in on_related_event.% has conditions defined in both top-level and config. Choose one location.', 
                                COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed'), v_event_key;
                        END IF;
                        
                        IF v_action->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                        
                        IF v_action->'config'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                                v_action->'config'->'conditions', 
                                'automations->on_related_event->' || v_event_key || '->actions[' || COALESCE(v_action->>'name', 'unnamed') || ']->config->conditions'
                            );
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- Enhanced: Validate sla_rules with action condition validation
    IF p_definition->'sla_rules' IS NOT NULL THEN
        IF jsonb_typeof(p_definition->'sla_rules') != 'array' THEN 
            RAISE EXCEPTION 'sla_rules must be an array.'; 
        END IF;
        
        v_rule_index := 0; 
        FOR v_sla_rule IN SELECT value FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'sla_rules') LOOP
            v_sla_id := COALESCE(v_sla_rule->>'id', 'UNKNOWN'); 

            -- Basic SLA rule structure validation
            IF jsonb_typeof(v_sla_rule) != 'object' THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule element at index % is not a JSON object!', v_rule_index; 
            END IF;
            
            IF v_sla_rule->>'id' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->>'check_frequency' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->>'time_threshold_hours' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule at index % (ID: %) is missing required key(s): id, check_frequency, or time_threshold_hours', v_rule_index, v_sla_id; 
            END IF;

            -- Validate monitored_stages
            IF v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages' IS NULL OR jsonb_array_length(v_sla_rule->'monitored_stages') = 0 THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % has missing or empty monitored_stages array', v_sla_id, v_rule_index;
            END IF;

            -- Validate SLA conditions
            v_sla_conditions := v_sla_rule->'conditions'; 
            v_sla_conditions_type := jsonb_typeof(v_sla_conditions);
            
            IF v_sla_conditions_type != 'object' AND v_sla_conditions_type != 'null' THEN
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % has invalid "conditions" type. Expected object/null, got %.', v_sla_id, v_rule_index, v_sla_conditions_type;
            END IF;

            PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                v_sla_conditions, 
                'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->conditions'
            ); 

            -- Enhanced: Validate SLA action conditions
            IF v_sla_rule->'action' IS NULL OR v_sla_rule->'action'->>'type' IS NULL THEN 
                RAISE EXCEPTION 'SLA rule ID % at index % is missing "action" or "action.type"', v_sla_id, v_rule_index; 
            END IF;

            -- Validate SLA action conditions if present
            IF v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                    v_sla_rule->'action'->'conditions',
                    'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->action->conditions'
                );
            END IF;

            -- Enhanced: Validate on_breach action conditions if present
            v_on_breach_config := v_sla_rule->'on_breach';
            IF v_on_breach_config IS NOT NULL AND v_on_breach_config != 'null'::jsonb THEN
                IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity' IS NOT NULL THEN
                    IF v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions' IS NOT NULL THEN
                        PERFORM automation.comp_validate_conditions(
                            v_on_breach_config->'update_entity'->'conditions',
                            'sla_rules[id=' || v_sla_id || ']->on_breach->update_entity->conditions'
                        );
                    END IF;
                END IF;
            END IF;

            v_rule_index := v_rule_index + 1; 
        END LOOP;
    END IF;

    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint validation passed for: %', p_definition->>'name';
    RETURN TRUE;

EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint validation failed: %', SQLERRM;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_comp_validate_structure_v1(jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_comp_validate_structure_v1(p_definition jsonb) RETURNS boolean
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- -- ***** WORKED B4 BP TYPES - tested dec 9 - REVERTED BACK ******** --
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTES:
    -- 1. Structural validation beyond JSON schema - checks business logic constraints
    -- 2. Validates stage ID uniqueness and required field presence
    -- 3. Early detection of blueprint structural issues before compilation
    -- 4. Used by: comp_core_compile
    
    -- Check for required top-level fields
    IF NOT (p_definition ? 'name' AND p_definition ? 'entity_schema' AND p_definition ? 'entity_type') THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing required fields: name, entity_schema, entity_type';
    END IF;
    
    -- Validate lifecycle structure exists
    IF p_definition->'lifecycle' IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint missing lifecycle definition';
    END IF;
    
    -- Check for stage ID conflicts (no duplicates)
    IF EXISTS (
        SELECT 1 FROM (
            SELECT value->>'id' as stage_id, COUNT(*)
            FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_definition->'lifecycle'->'stages')
            GROUP BY value->>'id'
            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
        ) duplicates
    ) THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Duplicate stage IDs found in lifecycle stages';
    END IF;
    
    -- Validate template variable usage in actions (placeholder for future enhancement)
    -- This can be expanded later for template variable validation
    
    RAISE NOTICE 'Blueprint structure validation passed for: %', p_definition->>'name';
    RETURN TRUE;
    
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'Blueprint structure validation failed: %', SQLERRM;
END;

-- -- UPDATE DID NOT WORK PROPERLY

-- BEGIN

--             RAISE EXCEPTION 'Lifecycle blueprint missing required fields: name, entity_schema, entity_type'; 
--         END IF;
--     ELSIF v_blueprint_type = 'approval' THEN

--         END IF;

--
-- Name: xz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v1(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v1() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- DECLARE

--         WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;
