--             FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
--             WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;

--             IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
--                 INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
--                     (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
--                 VALUES
--                     (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
--             END IF;

--             -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
--             SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
--             FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
--             WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
            
--             IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
--                 INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
--                     (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
--                 VALUES
--                     (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
--             END IF;
--         END IF;
--     END IF;

--     -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
--     RETURN NULL; 
-- END;

DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_on_exit_event TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT; -- New variable
BEGIN
    -- Only run if the stage_id actually changed and an instance ID exists
    IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id AND NEW.automation_esm_instance_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        
        -- 1. Update the stage in the esm_instance table
        UPDATE automation.esm_instances
        SET stage_id = NEW.stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
        WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

        -- 2. Find the ESM definition for this instance
        SELECT esm.*, (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_type') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_esm_definition
        FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
        JOIN automation.esm_instances inst ON inst.esm_definition_id = esm.id
        WHERE inst.id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

        IF FOUND THEN
            v_blueprint_type := COALESCE(v_esm_definition.blueprint_type_safe, 'lifecycle');
            RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_update] Stage changed for entity % from % to % (Type: %)', NEW.id, OLD.stage_id, NEW.stage_id, v_blueprint_type;
            
            -- Only Fire Events for 'lifecycle' types (Approval ESM is light/has NULL events)
            IF v_blueprint_type = 'lifecycle' THEN
                -- 3. Fire the EXIT event for the OLD stage
                SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;

                IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;

                -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
                SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
                
                IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;
            ELSE
                 RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_update] Skipping event firing for non-lifecycle blueprint type %', v_blueprint_type;
            END IF;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
    RETURN NULL;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v2(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v2() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- DECLARE
--     v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
--     v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
--     v_esm_definition RECORD;
--     v_on_entry_event TEXT;
--     v_on_exit_event TEXT;
-- BEGIN
--     -- Only run if the stage_id actually changed and an instance ID exists
--     IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id AND NEW.automation_esm_instance_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        
--         -- 1. Update the stage in the esm_instance table
--         UPDATE automation.esm_instances
--         SET stage_id = NEW.stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
--         WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

--         -- 2. Find the ESM definition for this instance
--         SELECT esm.* INTO v_esm_definition
--         FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
--         JOIN automation.esm_instances inst ON inst.esm_definition_id = esm.id
--         WHERE inst.id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

--         IF FOUND THEN
--             -- 3. Fire the EXIT event for the OLD stage
--             SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
--             FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
--             WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;

--             IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
--                 INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
--                     (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
--                 VALUES
--                     (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
--             END IF;

--             -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
--             SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
--             FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
--             WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
            
--             IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
--                 INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
--                     (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
--                 VALUES
--                     (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
--             END IF;
--         END IF;
--     END IF;

--     -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
--     RETURN NULL; 
-- END;

DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_on_exit_event TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT; -- New variable
BEGIN
    -- Only run if the stage_id actually changed and an instance ID exists
    IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id AND NEW.automation_esm_instance_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        
        -- 1. Update the stage in the esm_instance table
        UPDATE automation.esm_instances
        SET stage_id = NEW.stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
        WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

        -- 2. Find the ESM definition for this instance
        SELECT esm.*, (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_type') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_esm_definition
        FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
        JOIN automation.esm_instances inst ON inst.esm_definition_id = esm.id
        WHERE inst.id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;

        IF FOUND THEN
            v_blueprint_type := COALESCE(v_esm_definition.blueprint_type_safe, 'lifecycle');
            RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_update] Stage changed for entity % from % to % (Type: %)', NEW.id, OLD.stage_id, NEW.stage_id, v_blueprint_type;
            
            -- Only Fire Events for 'lifecycle' types (Approval ESM is light/has NULL events)
            IF v_blueprint_type = 'lifecycle' THEN
                -- 3. Fire the EXIT event for the OLD stage
                SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;

                IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;

                -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
                SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
                
                IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;
            ELSE
                 RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_update] Skipping event firing for non-lifecycle blueprint type %', v_blueprint_type;
            END IF;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
    RETURN NULL;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v1(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v1() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_esm_instance_id UUID;
    v_bp_instance_id UUID;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Find the single ACTIVE blueprint for this entity type
    SELECT * INTO v_blueprint
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
    WHERE bp.entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND bp.entity_type = v_entity_type
      AND bp.is_active = TRUE
    LIMIT 1;

    -- If no blueprint is active for this entity, do nothing.
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] No active blueprint found for %.%. Skipping orchestration.', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Find the compiled ESM definition for this specific blueprint version
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id;
    
    -- This is the critical fix: If no compiled definition, throw an error
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[esm_trigger_insert] Blueprint % (v%) is active, but its compiled ESM definition was not found. Re-compile blueprint.', v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Determine the final starting stage
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    -- 4. Create the ESM Instance (tracks current stage)
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances
        (organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id)
    VALUES
        (NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id)
    RETURNING id INTO v_esm_instance_id;

    -- 5. Create the Blueprint Instance (locks the version)
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances
        (organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
    VALUES
        (NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
    RETURNING id INTO v_bp_instance_id;

    -- 6. Write the instance IDs back to the entity table
    NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := v_bp_instance_id;
    NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := v_esm_instance_id;
    NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id; -- Ensure entity stage_id is correct

    -- 7. Fire the initial standardized stage entry event
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;
    
    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
            (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
        VALUES
            (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
    END IF;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v2(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v2() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- ***** WORKED B4 BP TYPES - tested dec 9 ******** --
-- DECLARE
--     v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
--     v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
--     v_blueprint RECORD;
--     v_esm_definition RECORD;
--     v_esm_instance_id UUID;
--     v_bp_instance_id UUID;
--     v_start_stage_id TEXT;
--     v_final_stage_id TEXT;
--     v_on_entry_event TEXT;
-- BEGIN
--     -- 1. Find the single ACTIVE blueprint for this entity type
--     SELECT * INTO v_blueprint
--     FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
--     WHERE bp.entity_schema = v_entity_schema
--       AND bp.entity_type = v_entity_type
--       AND bp.is_active = TRUE
--     LIMIT 1;

--     -- If no blueprint is active for this entity, do nothing.
--     IF NOT FOUND THEN
--         RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] No active blueprint found for %.%. Skipping orchestration.', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;
--         RETURN NEW;
--     END IF;

--     -- 2. Find the compiled ESM definition for this specific blueprint version
--     SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
--     FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
--     WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id;
    
--     -- This is the critical fix: If no compiled definition, throw an error
--     IF NOT FOUND THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION '[esm_trigger_insert] Blueprint % (v%) is active, but its compiled ESM definition was not found. Re-compile blueprint.', v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version;
--     END IF;

--     -- 3. Determine the final starting stage
--     v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
--     v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

--     -- 4. Create the ESM Instance (tracks current stage)
--     INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances
--         (organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id)
--     VALUES
--         (NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id)
--     RETURNING id INTO v_esm_instance_id;

--     -- 5. Create the Blueprint Instance (locks the version)
--     INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances
--         (organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
--     VALUES
--         (NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
--     RETURNING id INTO v_bp_instance_id;

--     -- 6. Write the instance IDs back to the entity table
--     NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := v_bp_instance_id;
--     NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := v_esm_instance_id;
--     NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id; -- Ensure entity stage_id is correct

--     -- 7. Fire the initial standardized stage entry event
--     SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
--     FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
--     WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;
    
--     IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
--         INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
--             (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
--         VALUES
--             (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
--     END IF;

--     RETURN NEW;
-- END;

DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_esm_instance_id UUID;
    v_bp_instance_id UUID;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Find the single ACTIVE blueprint for this entity type
    SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_blueprint
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
    WHERE bp.entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND bp.entity_type = v_entity_type
      AND bp.is_active = TRUE
    LIMIT 1;

    -- If no blueprint is active for this entity, do nothing.
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] No active blueprint found for %.%. Skipping orchestration.', v_entity_schema, v_entity_type;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    v_blueprint_type := v_blueprint.blueprint_type_safe;

    -- MINIMAL FIX: Skip all state machine setup for non-lifecycle/approval types
    IF v_blueprint_type NOT IN ('lifecycle', 'approval') THEN
        RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] Blueprint % is type %, skipping entity state management.', v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint_type;
        -- Still create a BP instance (crucial for general process tracking/audit)
        INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances
            (organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
        VALUES
            (NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
        RETURNING id INTO v_bp_instance_id;
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := v_bp_instance_id;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Find the compiled ESM definition for this specific blueprint version
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
    AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version -- Added version check for safety
    LIMIT 1;
    
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[esm_trigger_insert] Blueprint % (v%) is active, but its compiled ESM definition was not found. Re-compile blueprint.', v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Determine the final starting stage
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    -- 4. Create the ESM Instance (tracks current stage)
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances
        (organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id)
    VALUES
        (NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id)
    RETURNING id INTO v_esm_instance_id;

    -- 5. Create the Blueprint Instance (locks the version)
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances
        (organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
    VALUES
        (NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
    RETURNING id INTO v_bp_instance_id;

    -- 6. Write the instance IDs back to the entity table
    NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := v_bp_instance_id;
    NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := v_esm_instance_id;
    NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id; -- Ensure entity stage_id is correct

    -- 7. Fire the initial standardized stage entry event
    -- The ESM definition contains event names ONLY for 'lifecycle' types.
    -- For 'approval' types, v_on_entry_event will be NULL, and the event won't fire, 
    -- which is correct, as the approval engine handles initial state transitions separately.
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;
    
    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
            (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
        VALUES
            (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
        RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] Fired entry event: % for entity % (type: %)', v_on_entry_event, NEW.id, v_blueprint_type;
    ELSE
        RAISE NOTICE '[esm_trigger_insert] Blueprint type % does not trigger events on insert.', v_blueprint_type;
    END IF;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: xzz_run_core_process_event_duplicate_v1(uuid); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xzz_run_core_process_event_duplicate_v1(p_event_id uuid) RETURNS void
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- -- DECLARE
-- --     v_event RECORD;
-- --     v_rule RECORD;
-- --     v_action RECORD;
-- --     v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
-- --     v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
-- --     v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
-- --     v_dispatch_error TEXT;
-- --     v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
-- --     v_duration_ms INT;
-- --     v_event_blueprint_id UUID;
-- --     v_entity_id UUID;
-- --     v_esm_instance RECORD;
-- --     v_data_context JSONB;
-- --     v_flat_data JSONB;
-- -- BEGIN
-- --     -- DEV NOTE: Core processor for single events - replaces process_event_queue_v5
-- --     -- Preserves: All original process_event_queue_v5 functionality
-- --     -- Optimizes: Uses shared utilities for context building and evaluation
    
-- --     RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_process_event] Processing event %', p_event_id;

-- --     -- Fetch and lock event (original logic)
-- --     SELECT * INTO v_event
-- --     FROM automation.wf_events
-- --     WHERE id = p_event_id
-- --     FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

-- --     IF NOT FOUND THEN 
-- --         RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Event % not found or already locked.', p_event_id;
-- --         RETURN; 
-- --     END IF;

-- --     -- Extract entity_id (original logic)
-- --     v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
-- --     IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
-- --         v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'old'->>'id')::uuid;
-- --     END IF;

-- --     -- Get ESM instance and blueprint (original logic)
-- --     IF v_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
-- --         BEGIN
-- --             SELECT * INTO v_esm_instance
-- --             FROM automation.esm_instances esm
-- --             WHERE esm.entity_id = v_entity_id;
            
-- --             v_event_blueprint_id := v_esm_instance.blueprint_id;
-- --         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
-- --             RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Failed to get blueprint/esm instance: %', SQLERRM;
-- --         END;
-- --     END IF;

-- --     -- Use shared context builder instead of manual building
-- --     v_data_context := automation.run_helper_build_context(
-- --         v_event.trigger_data,
-- --         to_jsonb(v_esm_instance),
-- --         v_entity_id,
-- --         v_event.organization_id,
-- --         true  -- Include flattened data for efficient evaluation
-- --     );

-- --     -- Get rules matching the event (original logic)
-- --     FOR v_rule IN
-- --         SELECT *
-- --         FROM automation.wf_rules
-- --         WHERE organization_id = v_event.organization_id
-- --           AND trigger_schema = v_event.trigger_schema
-- --           AND trigger_table = v_event.trigger_table
-- --           AND trigger_type = v_event.trigger_type

-- --                 v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_rule.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
-- --             ELSE

-- --             v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
-- --         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
-- --             v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));

-- --                         IF v_action.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
-- --                             v_action_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_action.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
-- --                         ELSE
-- --                             RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported ACTION condition_type "%" for action %', v_action.condition_type, v_action.id;
-- --                             v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
-- --                         END IF;
-- --                     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
-- --                         RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] FAILED to evaluate action condition for action %: %', v_action.id, SQLERRM;
-- --                         v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
-- --                         v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
-- --                     END;
-- --                 END IF;

-- --                         PERFORM automation.dispatch_action(
-- --                             v_action.id,
-- --                             v_event.trigger_data,
-- --                             automation.run_helper_build_context(
-- --                                 v_event.trigger_data,
