-- --                             ) || jsonb_build_object(
-- --                                 'event_id', v_event.id,
-- --                                 'rule_id', v_rule.id,
-- --                                 'organization_id', v_event.organization_id,
-- --                                 'trigger_schema', v_event.trigger_schema,
-- --                                 'trigger_table', v_event.trigger_table
-- --                             ) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
-- --                         );
-- --                     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
-- --                         v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
-- --                         v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
-- --                         RAISE; 
-- --                     END;
-- --                 ELSE
-- --                     -- Log skipped action (original logic)
-- --                     INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, context, log_level)
-- --                     VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_rule.id, v_action.id, v_event.organization_id, 'skipped', 'Action skipped, condition not met.', v_action.conditions, 'info');
-- --                 END IF;
-- --             END LOOP;
-- --         END IF;
-- --     END LOOP;

-- --     -- Final error handling (original logic)
-- --     IF v_any_action_failed THEN
-- --         RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed for event %. Last error: %', p_event_id, v_dispatch_error;
-- --     END IF;

-- -- END;


-- DECLARE
--     v_event RECORD;
--     v_rule RECORD;
--     v_action RECORD;
--     v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
--     v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
--     v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
--     v_dispatch_error TEXT;
--     v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
--     v_duration_ms INT;
--     v_event_blueprint_id UUID;
--     v_entity_id UUID;
--     v_esm_instance RECORD;
--     v_data_context JSONB;
--     v_flat_data JSONB;
-- BEGIN
--     -- DEV NOTE: Core processor for single events - replaces process_event_queue_v5
--     -- Preserves: All original process_event_queue_v5 functionality
--     -- Optimizes: Uses shared utilities for context building and evaluation
    
--     RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_process_event] Processing event %', p_event_id;

--     -- Fetch and lock event (original logic)
--     SELECT * INTO v_event
--     FROM automation.wf_events
--     WHERE id = p_event_id
--     FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

--     IF NOT FOUND THEN 
--         RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Event % not found or already locked.', p_event_id;
--         RETURN; 
--     END IF;

--     -- Add this at the beginning of run_core_process_event - FIXED STATUS
--     INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
--     VALUES (
--         gen_random_uuid(), 
--         p_event_id, 
--         v_event.organization_id, 
--         'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
--         'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
--         'Starting event processing', 
--         jsonb_build_object('event_id', p_event_id, 'trigger_type', v_event.trigger_type),
--         clock_timestamp()
--     );

--     -- Extract entity_id (original logic)
--     v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
--     IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
--         v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'old'->>'id')::uuid;
--     END IF;

--     -- Get ESM instance and blueprint (original logic)
--     IF v_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
--         BEGIN
--             SELECT * INTO v_esm_instance
--             FROM automation.esm_instances esm
--             WHERE esm.entity_id = v_entity_id;
            
--             v_event_blueprint_id := v_esm_instance.blueprint_id;
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Failed to get blueprint/esm instance: %', SQLERRM;
--         END;
--     END IF;

--     -- Use shared context builder instead of manual building
--     v_data_context := automation.run_helper_build_context(
--         v_event.trigger_data,
--         to_jsonb(v_esm_instance),
--         v_entity_id,
--         v_event.organization_id,
--         true  -- Include flattened data for efficient evaluation
--     );

--     -- Get rules matching the event (original logic)
--     FOR v_rule IN
--         SELECT *
--         FROM automation.wf_rules
--         WHERE organization_id = v_event.organization_id
--           AND trigger_schema = v_event.trigger_schema
--           AND trigger_table = v_event.trigger_table
--           AND trigger_type = v_event.trigger_type
--           AND ( (v_event_blueprint_id IS NULL AND blueprint_id IS NULL) OR (blueprint_id = v_event_blueprint_id) )
--           AND is_active = TRUE
--         ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
--     LOOP
--         v_conditions_met := FALSE;
--         v_start_time := clock_timestamp();

--         BEGIN
--             -- Use shared condition evaluation
--             IF v_rule.conditions IS NULL OR v_rule.conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR (v_rule.conditions->'rules') IS NULL THEN
--                 v_conditions_met := TRUE;
--             ELSIF v_rule.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
--                 v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_rule.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
--             ELSE
--                 RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported condition_type "%"', v_rule.condition_type;
--                 v_conditions_met := FALSE;
--             END IF;
--             v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
--         EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--             v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
--             v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
--             CONTINUE;
--         END;

--         -- Add this after rule matching - FIXED STATUS
--         INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
--         VALUES (
--             gen_random_uuid(), 
--             p_event_id, 
--             v_rule.id, 
--             v_event.organization_id, 
--             'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
--             'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
--             'Rule matched, evaluating conditions', 
--             jsonb_build_object('rule_name', v_rule.name, 'conditions_met', v_conditions_met),
--             clock_timestamp()
--         );

--         -- If conditions met, execute actions
--         IF v_conditions_met THEN
--             FOR v_action IN
--                 SELECT * FROM automation.wf_actions
--                 WHERE rule_id = v_rule.id AND is_enabled = TRUE
--                 ORDER BY action_order ASC, created_at ASC
--             LOOP
--                 v_action_conditions_met := TRUE;
                
--                 -- Evaluate action-level conditions if they exist (original logic)
--                 IF v_action.conditions IS NOT NULL AND v_action.conditions != '{}'::jsonb THEN
--                     BEGIN
--                         IF v_action.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
--                             v_action_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_action.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
--                         ELSE
--                             RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported ACTION condition_type "%" for action %', v_action.condition_type, v_action.id;
--                             v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
--                         END IF;
--                     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--                         RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] FAILED to evaluate action condition for action %: %', v_action.id, SQLERRM;
--                         v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
--                         v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
--                     END;
--                 END IF;

--                 IF v_action_conditions_met THEN
--                     -- Add this when actions are found - FIXED STATUS
--                     INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
--                     VALUES (
--                         gen_random_uuid(), 
--                         p_event_id, 
--                         v_rule.id, 
--                         v_action.id, 
--                         v_event.organization_id, 
--                         'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
--                         'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
--                         'Action found, about to dispatch', 
--                         jsonb_build_object('action_name', v_action.name, 'action_type', v_action.action_type),
--                         clock_timestamp()
--                     );

--                     BEGIN
--                         -- Build context for action (enhanced with shared builder)
--                         PERFORM automation.dispatch_action(
--                             v_action.id,
--                             v_event.trigger_data,
--                             automation.run_helper_build_context(
--                                 v_event.trigger_data,
--                                 to_jsonb(v_esm_instance),
--                                 v_entity_id,
--                                 v_event.organization_id
--                             ) || jsonb_build_object(
--                                 'event_id', v_event.id,
--                                 'rule_id', v_rule.id,
--                                 'organization_id', v_event.organization_id,
--                                 'trigger_schema', v_event.trigger_schema,
--                                 'trigger_table', v_event.trigger_table
--                             ) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
--                         );
--                     EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
--                         v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
--                         v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
--                         RAISE; 
--                     END;
--                 ELSE
--                     -- Log skipped action (original logic)
--                     INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, context, log_level)
--                     VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_rule.id, v_action.id, v_event.organization_id, 'skipped', 'Action skipped, condition not met.', v_action.conditions, 'info');
--                 END IF;
--             END LOOP;
--         END IF;
--     END LOOP;

--     -- Final error handling (original logic)
--     IF v_any_action_failed THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed for event %. Last error: %', p_event_id, v_dispatch_error;
--     END IF;

-- END;
DECLARE
    v_event RECORD;
    v_rule RECORD;
    v_action RECORD;
    v_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_action_conditions_met BOOLEAN;
    v_any_action_failed BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_dispatch_error TEXT;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ;
    v_duration_ms INT;
    v_event_blueprint_id UUID;
    v_entity_id UUID;
    v_esm_instance RECORD;
    v_data_context JSONB;
    v_flat_data JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- DEV NOTE: Core processor for single events - replaces process_event_queue_v5
    -- Preserves: All original process_event_queue_v5 functionality
    -- Optimizes: Uses shared utilities for context building and evaluation
    
    RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_process_event] Processing event %', p_event_id;

    -- Fetch and lock event (original logic)
    SELECT * INTO v_event
    FROM automation.wf_events
    WHERE id = p_event_id
    FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN 
        RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Event % not found or already locked.', p_event_id;
        RETURN; 
    END IF;

    -- Add this at the beginning of run_core_process_event - FIXED STATUS
    INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
    VALUES (
        gen_random_uuid(), 
        p_event_id, 
        v_event.organization_id, 
        'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
        'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
        'Starting event processing', 
        jsonb_build_object('event_id', p_event_id, 'trigger_type', v_event.trigger_type),
        clock_timestamp()
    );

    -- Extract entity_id (original logic)
    v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'new'->>'id')::uuid;
    IF v_entity_id IS NULL THEN
        v_entity_id := (v_event.trigger_data->'old'->>'id')::uuid;
    END IF;

    -- Get ESM instance and blueprint (original logic)
    IF v_entity_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        BEGIN
            SELECT * INTO v_esm_instance
            FROM automation.esm_instances esm
            WHERE esm.entity_id = v_entity_id;
            
            v_event_blueprint_id := v_esm_instance.blueprint_id;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Failed to get blueprint/esm instance: %', SQLERRM;
        END;
    END IF;

    -- Use shared context builder instead of manual building
    v_data_context := automation.run_helper_build_context(
        v_event.trigger_data,
        to_jsonb(v_esm_instance),
        v_entity_id,
        v_event.organization_id,
        true  -- Include flattened data for efficient evaluation
    );

    -- Get rules matching the event (original logic)
    FOR v_rule IN
        SELECT *
        FROM automation.wf_rules
        WHERE organization_id = v_event.organization_id
          AND trigger_schema = v_event.trigger_schema
          AND trigger_table = v_event.trigger_table
          AND trigger_type = v_event.trigger_type
          AND ( (v_event_blueprint_id IS NULL AND blueprint_id IS NULL) OR (blueprint_id = v_event_blueprint_id) )
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY priority DESC, created_at ASC
    LOOP
        v_conditions_met := FALSE;
        v_start_time := clock_timestamp();

        -- Add debug log BEFORE condition evaluation
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
        VALUES (
            gen_random_uuid(), 
            p_event_id, 
            v_rule.id, 
            v_event.organization_id, 
            'info', 
            'debug', 
            'About to evaluate conditions', 
            jsonb_build_object(
                'rule_name', v_rule.name, 
                'conditions', v_rule.conditions,
                'condition_type', v_rule.condition_type,
                'has_esm_instance', (v_esm_instance IS NOT NULL),
                'entity_id', v_entity_id
            ),
            clock_timestamp()
        );

        BEGIN
            -- Use shared condition evaluation WITH FIX for empty conditions
            IF v_rule.conditions IS NULL OR 
               v_rule.conditions = '{}'::jsonb OR 
               (v_rule.conditions->'rules') IS NULL OR
               (v_rule.conditions->'rules' = '[]'::jsonb) THEN
                v_conditions_met := TRUE;
                RAISE NOTICE '[run_core_process_event] Empty conditions, defaulting to TRUE for rule %', v_rule.id;
            ELSIF v_rule.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
                v_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_rule.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
            ELSE
                RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported condition_type "%"', v_rule.condition_type;
                v_conditions_met := FALSE;
            END IF;
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            v_duration_ms := EXTRACT(MILLISECONDS FROM (clock_timestamp() - v_start_time));
            RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Condition evaluation failed for rule %: %', v_rule.id, SQLERRM;
            v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
            CONTINUE;
        END;

        -- Add debug log AFTER condition evaluation
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
        VALUES (
            gen_random_uuid(), 
            p_event_id, 
            v_rule.id, 
            v_event.organization_id, 
            'info', 
            'debug', 
            'Condition evaluation completed', 
            jsonb_build_object(
                'rule_name', v_rule.name, 
                'conditions_met', v_conditions_met,
                'evaluation_duration_ms', v_duration_ms,
                'condition_type', v_rule.condition_type
            ),
            clock_timestamp()
        );

        -- Add this after rule matching - FIXED STATUS
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
        VALUES (
            gen_random_uuid(), 
            p_event_id, 
            v_rule.id, 
            v_event.organization_id, 
            'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
            'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
            'Rule matched, evaluating conditions', 
            jsonb_build_object('rule_name', v_rule.name, 'conditions_met', v_conditions_met),
            clock_timestamp()
        );

        -- If conditions met, execute actions
        IF v_conditions_met THEN
            FOR v_action IN
                SELECT * FROM automation.wf_actions
                WHERE rule_id = v_rule.id AND is_enabled = TRUE
                ORDER BY action_order ASC, created_at ASC
            LOOP
                v_action_conditions_met := TRUE;
                
                -- Evaluate action-level conditions if they exist (original logic)
                IF v_action.conditions IS NOT NULL AND v_action.conditions != '{}'::jsonb THEN
                    BEGIN
                        IF v_action.condition_type = 'jsonb' THEN
                            v_action_conditions_met := automation.run_util_eval_conditions(v_action.conditions, v_event.trigger_data, to_jsonb(v_esm_instance));
                        ELSE
                            RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] Unsupported ACTION condition_type "%" for action %', v_action.condition_type, v_action.id;
                            v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        END IF;
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        RAISE WARNING '[run_core_process_event] FAILED to evaluate action condition for action %: %', v_action.id, SQLERRM;
                        v_action_conditions_met := FALSE;
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                    END;
                END IF;

                IF v_action_conditions_met THEN
                    -- Add this when actions are found - FIXED STATUS
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, log_level, message, context, executed_at)
                    VALUES (
                        gen_random_uuid(), 
                        p_event_id, 
                        v_rule.id, 
                        v_action.id, 
                        v_event.organization_id, 
                        'info',  -- Valid status: 'info'
                        'debug',  -- Valid log_level: 'debug'
                        'Action found, about to dispatch', 
                        jsonb_build_object('action_name', v_action.name, 'action_type', v_action.action_type),
                        clock_timestamp()
                    );

                    BEGIN
                        -- Build context for action (enhanced with shared builder)
                        PERFORM automation.dispatch_action(
                            v_action.id,
                            v_event.trigger_data,
                            automation.run_helper_build_context(
                                v_event.trigger_data,
                                to_jsonb(v_esm_instance),
                                v_entity_id,
                                v_event.organization_id
                            ) || jsonb_build_object(
                                'event_id', v_event.id,
                                'rule_id', v_rule.id,
                                'organization_id', v_event.organization_id,
                                'trigger_schema', v_event.trigger_schema,
                                'trigger_table', v_event.trigger_table
                            ) || COALESCE(v_rule.metadata, '{}'::jsonb)
                        );
                    EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
                        v_any_action_failed := TRUE;
                        v_dispatch_error := SQLERRM;
                        RAISE; 
                    END;
                ELSE
                    -- Log skipped action (original logic)
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_logs (id, event_id, rule_id, action_id, organization_id, status, message, context, log_level)
                    VALUES (gen_random_uuid(), v_event.id, v_rule.id, v_action.id, v_event.organization_id, 'skipped', 'Action skipped, condition not met.', v_action.conditions, 'info');
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    -- Final error handling (original logic)
    IF v_any_action_failed THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION 'One or more actions failed for event %. Last error: %', p_event_id, v_dispatch_error;
    END IF;

END;$$;


--
-- Name: xzz_run_smart_send_email_v1(jsonb, jsonb, jsonb); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation.xzz_run_smart_send_email_v1(p_action_config jsonb, p_trigger_data jsonb, p_context jsonb) RETURNS jsonb
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$-- DECLARE
--     v_template_id TEXT := p_action_config->>'template_id';
--     v_recipients_config JSONB := p_action_config->'recipients';
--     v_template_details JSONB;
--     v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
--     v_to_recipients TEXT[];
--     v_cc_recipients TEXT[];
--     v_bcc_recipients TEXT[];
--     v_flattened_data JSONB;
--     v_final_subject TEXT;
--     v_final_body TEXT;
--     v_response RECORD;
--     v_supabase_anon_key TEXT;
--     v_send_email_url TEXT;
--     v_from_email TEXT;
-- BEGIN
--     -- DEV NOTE: Minimal changes - only replaced function calls with new names
--     -- Preserves: All original tested logic exactly as-is
    
--     -- 1. PREPARATION (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     SELECT secret INTO v_supabase_anon_key FROM vault.secrets WHERE name = 'supabase_anon_key';
--     SELECT value INTO v_send_email_url FROM public.app_config WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';
--     SELECT value INTO v_from_email FROM public.app_config WHERE name = 'RESEND_FROM_EMAIL';

--     IF v_supabase_anon_key IS NULL OR v_send_email_url IS NULL OR v_from_email IS NULL THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Missing required secrets/config.';
--     END IF;
    
--     IF v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
--          RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Organization ID not found in p_context.';
--     END IF;

--     -- MINIMAL CHANGE: use new flatten function
--     v_flattened_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);

--     -- 2. RESOLVE RECIPIENTS (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
--     v_to_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'to', v_flattened_data);
--     v_cc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'cc', v_flattened_data);
--     v_bcc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'bcc', v_flattened_data);

--     -- ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED
--     IF COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 AND COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 THEN
--         RETURN jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'skipped', 'reason', 'No "to" or "cc" recipients.');
--     END IF;

--     -- 3. RESOLVE CONTENT (WITH FIXES) (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_template_id IS NOT NULL THEN
--         SELECT details INTO v_template_details 
--         FROM public.email_templates 
--         WHERE name = v_template_id AND organization_id = v_organization_id;
--     END IF;
    
--     -- Get base subject/body (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     v_final_subject := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'subject', v_template_details->>'subject', 'No Subject');
    
--     IF v_template_details IS NOT NULL THEN
--         v_final_body := v_template_details->>'body';
--     ELSIF p_action_config->>'body' IS NOT NULL THEN
--         v_final_body := p_action_config->>'body';
--     ELSE
--         -- SMART FALLBACK FIX PRESERVED
--         v_final_body := 'This is an automated notification regarding: <b>' || v_final_subject || '</b>';
--     END IF;

--     -- RECURSIVE PLACEHOLDER FIX (MINIMAL CHANGE: use new function)
--     v_final_subject := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
--         to_jsonb(v_final_subject), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
--     ) #>> '{}';
    
--     v_final_body := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
--         to_jsonb(v_final_body), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
--     ) #>> '{}';

--     -- 4. EXECUTION (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     SELECT * INTO v_response FROM extensions.http_post(
--         v_send_email_url,
--         jsonb_build_array(
--             jsonb_build_object(
--                 'from', COALESCE(p_action_config->>'from', v_from_email),
--                 'to', COALESCE(v_to_recipients, '{}'),
--                 'cc', COALESCE(v_cc_recipients, '{}'),
--                 'bcc', COALESCE(v_bcc_recipients, '{}'),
--                 'subject', v_final_subject,
--                 'html', v_final_body
--             )
--         )::text,
--         jsonb_build_object(
--             'Content-Type', 'application/json',
--             'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_supabase_anon_key
--         )::text 
--     );

--     -- 5. RETURN CONTEXT (ORIGINAL LOGIC PRESERVED)
--     IF v_response.status != 200 THEN
--         RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Failed: HTTP % - %', v_response.status, v_response.content;
--     END IF;

--     RETURN jsonb_build_object(
--         'outcome', 'success',
--         'http_status', v_response.status,
--         'recipient_counts', jsonb_build_object(
--             'to', COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0),
--             'cc', COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0),
--             'bcc', COALESCE(array_length(v_bcc_recipients, 1), 0)
--         )
--     );
-- EXCEPTION
--     WHEN others THEN
--         RAISE NOTICE '[run_smart_send_email] Error: %', SQLERRM;
--         RAISE;
-- END;
-- **** STILL HARD CODING FOR XOWORKS>AI EMAIL)****
DECLARE
    v_template_id TEXT := p_action_config->>'template_id';
    v_recipients_config JSONB := p_action_config->'recipients';
    v_template_details JSONB;
    v_organization_id UUID := (p_context->>'organization_id')::uuid;
    v_to_recipients TEXT[];
    v_cc_recipients TEXT[];
    v_bcc_recipients TEXT[];
    v_flattened_data JSONB;
    v_final_subject TEXT;
    v_final_body TEXT;
    v_response RECORD;
    v_supabase_anon_key TEXT;
    v_send_email_url TEXT;
    v_from_email TEXT;
    v_project_ref TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- 1. PREPARATION — DYNAMIC & RESILIENT
    -- Fetch supabase_anon_key (must be in vault)
    SELECT secret INTO v_supabase_anon_key FROM vault.secrets WHERE name = 'supabase_anon_key';
    IF v_supabase_anon_key IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Missing required secret: supabase_anon_key in vault.secrets.';
    END IF;

    -- Fetch from_email (non-secret — ok in app_config)
    SELECT value INTO v_from_email FROM public.app_config WHERE name = 'RESEND_FROM_EMAIL';
    IF v_from_email IS NULL THEN
        v_from_email := 'support@vkbs.zoworks.ai'; -- ✅ safe fallback
    END IF;

    -- 🔁 DYNAMICALLY DERIVE send_email_url
    -- Try 1: From app_config (existing behavior)
    SELECT value INTO v_send_email_url FROM public.app_config WHERE name = 'SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL';

    -- Try 2: Derive from JWT claims (works in authed/SECURITY DEFINER context)
    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL OR v_send_email_url = '' OR v_send_email_url LIKE '%{PROJECT_REF}%' THEN
        BEGIN
            -- Extract project ref from JWT issuer: "https://xyz.supabase.co/..." → "xyz"
            v_project_ref := (
                SELECT 
                    split_part(
                        replace(
                            (current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true)::jsonb ->> 'iss'),
                            'https://', ''
                        ),
                        '.', 1
                    )
                WHERE current_setting('request.jwt.claims', true) IS NOT NULL
            );

            IF v_project_ref IS NOT NULL AND v_project_ref <> '' THEN
                v_send_email_url := format('https://%s.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email', v_project_ref);
            ELSE
                -- Try 3: Fallback to known ref (dev/staging)
                v_send_email_url := 'https://begqjgsemuiisjznayzx.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
            END IF;
        EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
            -- Fallback if JWT not available (e.g., cron, unauthed trigger)
            v_send_email_url := 'https://begqjgsemuiisjznayzx.supabase.co/functions/v1/send_email';
        END;
    END IF;

    -- Final validation
    IF v_send_email_url IS NULL OR v_send_email_url = '' THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Could not determine SEND_EMAIL_FUNCTION_URL.';
    END IF;

    IF v_organization_id IS NULL THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Organization ID not found in p_context.';
    END IF;

    -- 2. FLATTEN DATA
    v_flattened_data := automation.run_util_eval_conditions_flatten(p_trigger_data);

    -- 3. RESOLVE RECIPIENTS
    v_to_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'to', v_flattened_data);
    v_cc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'cc', v_flattened_data);
    v_bcc_recipients := automation.run_service_resolve_recipients(v_recipients_config->'bcc', v_flattened_data);

    IF COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 
       AND COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0) = 0 THEN
        RETURN jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'skipped', 'reason', 'No "to" or "cc" recipients.');
    END IF;

    -- 4. RESOLVE CONTENT
    IF v_template_id IS NOT NULL THEN
        SELECT details INTO v_template_details 
        FROM public.email_templates 
        WHERE name = v_template_id AND organization_id = v_organization_id;
    END IF;

    v_final_subject := COALESCE(p_action_config->>'subject', v_template_details->>'subject', 'No Subject');

    IF v_template_details IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_final_body := v_template_details->>'body';
    ELSIF p_action_config->>'body' IS NOT NULL THEN
        v_final_body := p_action_config->>'body';
    ELSE
        v_final_body := 'This is an automated notification regarding: <b>' || 
                        v_final_subject || '</b>';
    END IF;

    v_final_subject := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_subject), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';
    
    v_final_body := automation.run_util_resolve_template(
        to_jsonb(v_final_body), v_flattened_data, v_organization_id
    ) #>> '{}';

    -- 5. EXECUTION
    SELECT * INTO v_response FROM extensions.http_post(
        v_send_email_url,
        jsonb_build_array(
            jsonb_build_object(
                'from', COALESCE(p_action_config->>'from', v_from_email),
                'to', COALESCE(v_to_recipients, '{}'),
                'cc', COALESCE(v_cc_recipients, '{}'),
                'bcc', COALESCE(v_bcc_recipients, '{}'),
                'subject', v_final_subject,
                'html', v_final_body
            )
        )::text,
        jsonb_build_object(
            'Content-Type', 'application/json',
            'Authorization', 'Bearer ' || v_supabase_anon_key
        )::text 
    );

    -- 6. RESPONSE HANDLING
    IF v_response.status != 200 THEN
        RAISE EXCEPTION '[run_smart_send_email] Failed: HTTP % - %', v_response.status, v_response.content;
    END IF;

    RETURN jsonb_build_object(
        'outcome', 'success',
        'http_status', v_response.status,
        'recipient_counts', jsonb_build_object(
            'to', COALESCE(array_length(v_to_recipients, 1), 0),
            'cc', COALESCE(array_length(v_cc_recipients, 1), 0),
            'bcc', COALESCE(array_length(v_bcc_recipients, 1), 0)
        )
    );
EXCEPTION
    WHEN OTHERS THEN
        RAISE NOTICE '[run_smart_send_email] Error: % (URL: %)', SQLERRM, v_send_email_url;
        RAISE;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v3-M21(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_after_update_v3-M21"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$DECLARE
-- CHANGES FROM ORIGINAL:
--   1. Added 3 variables: v_trace_id, v_start_time, v_events
--   2. Replaced RAISE NOTICE with tel_event() calls
--   3. Added 1 tel_flush() at each exit path  EXCEPTION block
--   4. Business logic is 100% unchanged
-- ============================================================================
    v_entity_schema  TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type    TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_on_exit_event  TEXT;
    v_blueprint_type TEXT;
 
    -- OTEL telemetry (3 new variables — trace_id = entity's shared UUID)
    v_trace_id   UUID := NEW.id;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp();
    v_events     JSONB := '[]'::JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- Only run if the stage_id actually changed and an instance ID exists
    IF OLD.stage_id IS DISTINCT FROM NEW.stage_id AND NEW.automation_esm_instance_id IS NOT NULL THEN
 
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'stage_change_detected', jsonb_build_object(
            'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
            'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
            'esm_instance_id', NEW.automation_esm_instance_id
        ));
 
        -- 1. Update the stage in the esm_instance table
        UPDATE automation.esm_instances
        SET stage_id = NEW.stage_id, updated_at = NOW()
        WHERE id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;
 
        -- 2. Find the ESM definition for this instance
        SELECT esm.*, (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_type') AS blueprint_type_safe INTO v_esm_definition
        FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
        JOIN automation.esm_instances inst ON inst.esm_definition_id = esm.id
        WHERE inst.id = NEW.automation_esm_instance_id;
 
        IF FOUND THEN
            v_blueprint_type := COALESCE(v_esm_definition.blueprint_type_safe, 'lifecycle');
 
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'esm_definition_resolved', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
                'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type
            ));
 
            -- Only Fire Events for 'lifecycle' types (Approval ESM is light/has NULL events)
            IF v_blueprint_type = 'lifecycle' THEN
 
                -- 3. Fire the EXIT event for the OLD stage
                SELECT s->>'on_exit_event_name' INTO v_on_exit_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = OLD.stage_id;
 
                IF v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_exit_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;
 
                v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exit_event', jsonb_build_object(
                    'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
                    'event_name', v_on_exit_event,
                    'fired', v_on_exit_event IS NOT NULL
                ));
 
                -- 4. Fire the ENTRY event for the NEW stage
                SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
                FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
                WHERE s->>'id' = NEW.stage_id;
 
                IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
                    INSERT INTO automation.wf_events
                        (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id)
                    VALUES
                        (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW), 'old', to_jsonb(OLD)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id);
                END IF;
 
                v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'entry_event', jsonb_build_object(
                    'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
                    'event_name', v_on_entry_event,
                    'fired', v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL
                ));
 
            ELSE
                v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'events_skipped', jsonb_build_object(
                    'reason', 'non-lifecycle blueprint',
                    'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type
                ));
            END IF;
        ELSE
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_esm_definition', jsonb_build_object(
                'esm_instance_id', NEW.automation_esm_instance_id
            ));
        END IF;
 
        -- Flush telemetry: 1 write for the full stage transition
        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_after_update', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object(
                'outcome', 'stage_transition',
                'old_stage', OLD.stage_id,
                'new_stage', NEW.stage_id,
                'blueprint_type', v_blueprint_type,
                'exit_event', v_on_exit_event,
                'entry_event', v_on_entry_event
            ), v_events);
 
    END IF;
    -- no telemetry flush when stage didn't change (no-op, not worth logging)
 
    -- AFTER triggers must return NULL or NEW
    RETURN NULL;
 
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exception', jsonb_build_object(
        'sqlstate', SQLSTATE,
        'sqlerrm', SQLERRM
    ));
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_after_update', v_start_time, 'ERROR',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'exception', 'old_stage', OLD.stage_id, 'new_stage', NEW.stage_id),
        v_events, SQLERRM);
    RAISE;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m17(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m17"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- Ensure NEW.id exists (Shared UUID Anchor)
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); END IF;

    -- 1. Find the single ACTIVE blueprint for this entity type
    SELECT * INTO v_blueprint
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints bp
    WHERE bp.entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND bp.entity_type = v_entity_type
      AND bp.is_active = TRUE
    LIMIT 1;

    -- If no blueprint is active, proceed without orchestration
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 2. Find the compiled ESM definition
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
    LIMIT 1;

    -- 3. Provision the Shared UUID Instances
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (
        id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, 
        entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version,
        v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE'
    ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

    -- Determine stage
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    IF v_esm_definition.id IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (
            id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, 
            entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id
        ) VALUES (
            NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version,
            v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id
        ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

        -- 4. Fire initial stage entry event
        SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
        FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
        WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

        IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
            INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
                organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, 
                trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id
            ) VALUES (
                NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, 
                jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id
            ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
        END IF;
    END IF;

    -- 5. Sync IDs back to the record
    -- Only sync if these columns exist on the target pillar
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN
        NULL;
    END;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m18(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m18"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_found_blueprint BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_match text[];
    v_filter_matches BOOLEAN;
    v_stage_category TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Ensure NEW.id exists (Shared UUID Anchor)
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN 
        NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); 
    END IF;

    -- 2. Dual-Axis Pillar-Agnostic Matcher (Feb 27 Standard)
    FOR v_blueprint IN 
        SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe 
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
        WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema 
          AND entity_type = v_entity_type 
          AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY organization_id NULLS LAST, version DESC
    LOOP
        v_filter_matches := TRUE;
        
        IF v_blueprint.partition_filter IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.partition_filter != '' THEN
            -- Standard Equality Matcher
            FOR v_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*=\s*''([^'']+)''', 'gi')) LOOP
                IF (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_match[1]) IS DISTINCT FROM v_match[2] THEN
                    v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                    EXIT;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
            
            -- Generic IN Clause Matcher
            IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter ~* 'IN\s*\(' THEN
                DECLARE
                    v_in_match record;
                BEGIN
                    FOR v_in_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[1] as col, (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[2] as vals LOOP
                        IF NOT (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_in_match.col = ANY(
                            SELECT trim(both '''' from x) FROM unnest(string_to_array(replace(v_in_match.vals, ' ', ''), ',')) x
                        )) THEN
                            v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                            EXIT;
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        IF v_filter_matches THEN
            v_found_blueprint := TRUE;
            EXIT;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    -- Exit if no automation blueprint matches this state
    IF NOT v_found_blueprint THEN
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Find Compiled ESM Definition
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
    AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version
    LIMIT 1;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'ESM Hybrid: Blueprint % found but NO COMPILED ESM DEFINITION. FK violation likely.', v_blueprint.id;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 4. Provision Shared UUID BP Instance
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (
        id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, 
        entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version,
        v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE'
    ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = 'ACTIVE';

    -- 5. Determine Stage & Category
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_stage_category
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    -- Provision Shared UUID ESM Instance
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (
        id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, 
        entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, 
        blueprint_id, stage_category
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version,
        v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', 
        v_blueprint.id, v_stage_category
    ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET 
        stage_id = EXCLUDED.stage_id,
        stage_category = EXCLUDED.stage_category,
        updated_at = now();

    -- 6. Fire Entry Event
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
            organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, 
            trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id, status
        ) VALUES (
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, 
            jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'pending'
        ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    END IF;

    -- 7. Sync IDs back to the record
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN
        NULL;
    END;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m18-2(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert-m18-2"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_found_blueprint BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_match text[];
    v_filter_matches BOOLEAN;
    v_stage_category TEXT;
    v_p_filter TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- 1. Ensure NEW.id exists (Shared UUID Anchor)
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN 
        NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); 
    END IF;

    -- 2. Dual-Axis Pillar-Agnostic Blueprint Resolution
    FOR v_blueprint IN 
        SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe 
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
        WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema 
          AND entity_type = v_entity_type 
          AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY organization_id NULLS LAST, version DESC
    LOOP
        v_filter_matches := TRUE;
        
        -- SCHEMA RESILIENCE: Safely handle missing partition_filter column
        -- We use to_jsonb to avoid 'no field partition_filter' crash
        v_p_filter := COALESCE(
            to_jsonb(v_blueprint)->>'partition_filter', 
            v_blueprint."intent", -- Fallback to intent column
            ''
        );
        
        IF v_p_filter != '' THEN
            -- Standard Equality Matcher
            FOR v_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_p_filter, '(\w+)\s*=\s*''([^'']+)''', 'gi')) LOOP
                IF (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_match[1]) IS DISTINCT FROM v_match[2] THEN
                    v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                    EXIT;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
            
            -- Generic IN Clause Matcher
            IF v_filter_matches AND v_p_filter ~* 'IN\s*\(' THEN
                DECLARE
                    v_in_match record;
                BEGIN
                    FOR v_in_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_p_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[1] as col, (regexp_matches(v_p_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[2] as vals LOOP
                        IF NOT (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_in_match.col = ANY(
                            SELECT trim(both '''' from x) FROM unnest(string_to_array(replace(v_in_match.vals, ' ', ''), ',')) x
                        )) THEN
                            v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                            EXIT;
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        IF v_filter_matches THEN
            v_found_blueprint := TRUE;
            EXIT;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF NOT v_found_blueprint THEN
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 3. Find Compiled ESM Definition
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition
    FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
    AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version
    LIMIT 1;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        RAISE WARNING 'ESM Hybrid: Blueprint % found but NO COMPILED ESM DEFINITION. FK violation likely.', v_blueprint.id;
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;

    -- 4. Provision Shared UUID BP Instance
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (
        id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, 
        entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version,
        v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE'
    ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = 'ACTIVE';

    -- 5. Determine Stage & Category
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_stage_category
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    -- Provision Shared UUID ESM Instance
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (
        id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, 
        entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, 
        blueprint_id, stage_category
    ) VALUES (
        NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version,
        v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', 
        v_blueprint.id, v_stage_category
    ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET 
        stage_id = EXCLUDED.stage_id,
        stage_category = EXCLUDED.stage_category,
        updated_at = now();

    -- 6. Fire Entry Event
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s
    WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (
            organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, 
            trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id, status
        ) VALUES (
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, 
            jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'pending'
        ) ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    END IF;

    -- 7. Sync IDs back to the record
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN
        NULL;
    END;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_M21(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_M21"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$DECLARE
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_found_blueprint BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_match text[];
    v_filter_matches BOOLEAN;
    v_stage_category TEXT;
    v_record_intent TEXT;
BEGIN
    -- [SHARED UUID ANCHOR]
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); END IF;

    -- [AXIS 1: RESOLVE RECORD PILLAR]
    -- Try to extract 'intent' or 'intent_category' from the record dynamically
    v_record_intent := COALESCE(to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent', to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent_category', '');

    -- [DUAL-AXIS MATCHER]: Universal Resolution
    FOR v_blueprint in 
        SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe 
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints 
        WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema 
          AND entity_type = v_entity_type 
          AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY organization_id NULLS LAST, version DESC
    LOOP
        v_filter_matches := TRUE;

        -- Axis 1: Pillar Match (if record provides an explicit intent)
        IF v_record_intent != '' AND v_blueprint.intent IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.intent != '' THEN
            IF v_blueprint.intent != v_record_intent THEN
                v_filter_matches := FALSE;
            END IF;
        END IF;
        
        -- Axis 2: Discriminator Match (High-Performance Column Matcher)
        IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.partition_filter != '' THEN
            -- Standard Equality Matcher
            FOR v_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*=\s*''([^'']+)''', 'gi')) LOOP
                IF (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_match[1]) IS DISTINCT FROM v_match[2] THEN
                    v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                    EXIT;
                END IF;
            END LOOP;
            
            -- Generic IN Clause Matcher
            IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter ~* 'IN\s*\(' THEN
                DECLARE
                    v_in_match record;
                BEGIN
                    FOR v_in_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[1] as col, (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[2] as vals LOOP
                        IF NOT (to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_in_match.col = ANY(
                            SELECT trim(both '''' from x) FROM unnest(string_to_array(replace(v_in_match.vals, ' ', ''), ',')) x
                        )) THEN
                            v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                            EXIT;
                        END IF;
                    END LOOP;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;

        IF v_filter_matches THEN
            v_found_blueprint := TRUE;
            EXIT;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;

    IF NOT v_found_blueprint THEN RETURN NEW; END IF;

    -- [ESM PROVISIONING]
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
    AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version LIMIT 1;

    IF NOT FOUND THEN RETURN NEW; END IF;

    -- Provision Instances
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = 'ACTIVE';

    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);

    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_stage_category
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id, stage_category)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id, v_stage_category)
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET stage_id = EXCLUDED.stage_id, stage_category = EXCLUDED.stage_category;

    -- [WF EVENTS]
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;

    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id, status)
        VALUES (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'pending')
        ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    END IF;

    -- [BINDING]
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN NULL; END;

    RETURN NEW;
END;$$;


--
-- Name: zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v3-M26(); Type: FUNCTION; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE FUNCTION automation."zz_esm_orchestration_before_insert_v3-M26"() RETURNS trigger
    LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER
    SET search_path TO 'public', 'automation', 'identity'
    AS $$DECLARE
-- CHANGES FROM ORIGINAL:
--   1. Added 3 variables: v_trace_id, v_start_time, v_events
--   2. Replaced every INSERT INTO ut_debug_logs with v_events := automation.tel_event(...)
--   3. Added 1 PERFORM automation.tel_flush(...) at end  in EXCEPTION
--   4. Business logic is 100% unchanged
-- ============================================================================
    v_entity_schema TEXT := TG_TABLE_SCHEMA;
    v_entity_type   TEXT := TG_TABLE_NAME;
    v_blueprint     RECORD;
    v_esm_definition RECORD;
    v_start_stage_id TEXT;
    v_final_stage_id TEXT;
    v_on_entry_event TEXT;
    v_found_blueprint BOOLEAN := FALSE;
    v_match          text[];
    v_filter_matches BOOLEAN;
    v_stage_category TEXT;
    v_record_intent  TEXT;
    v_blueprint_count INT;
    v_blueprint_checked TEXT := '';

    -- OTEL telemetry (3 new variables — trace_id set after shared UUID anchor)
    v_trace_id   UUID;
    v_start_time TIMESTAMPTZ := clock_timestamp();
    v_events     JSONB := '[]'::JSONB;
BEGIN
    -- [SHARED UUID ANCHOR]
    IF NEW.id IS NULL THEN NEW.id := gen_random_uuid(); END IF;
    v_trace_id := NEW.id;  -- trace_id = entity's shared UUID
 
    -- Log start
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'start', jsonb_build_object(
        'organization_id', NEW.organization_id,
        'leave_type_id', NEW.leave_type_id,
        'stage_id', NEW.stage_id,
        'entity_schema', v_entity_schema,
        'entity_type', v_entity_type
    ));
 
    -- [AXIS 1: RESOLVE RECORD PILLAR]
    v_record_intent := COALESCE(to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent', to_jsonb(NEW)->>'intent_category', '');
 
    -- Count available blueprints
    SELECT COUNT(*) INTO v_blueprint_count
    FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
    WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema
      AND entity_type = v_entity_type
      AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
      AND is_active = TRUE;
 
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'blueprints_available', jsonb_build_object(
        'count', v_blueprint_count,
        'organization_id', NEW.organization_id
    ));
 
    -- [DUAL-AXIS MATCHER]: Universal Resolution
    FOR v_blueprint IN
        SELECT *, COALESCE(blueprint_type, 'lifecycle') AS blueprint_type_safe
        FROM automation.bp_process_blueprints
        WHERE entity_schema = v_entity_schema
          AND entity_type = v_entity_type
          AND (organization_id = NEW.organization_id OR organization_id IS NULL)
          AND is_active = TRUE
        ORDER BY organization_id NULLS LAST, version DESC
    LOOP
        v_filter_matches := TRUE;
 
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'checking_blueprint', jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
            'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version,
            'partition_filter', v_blueprint.partition_filter,
            'intent', v_blueprint.intent
        ));
 
        -- Axis 1: Pillar Match
        IF v_record_intent != '' AND v_blueprint.intent IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.intent != '' THEN
            IF v_blueprint.intent != v_record_intent THEN
                v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'intent_mismatch', jsonb_build_object(
                    'record_intent', v_record_intent,
                    'blueprint_intent', v_blueprint.intent
                ));
            END IF;
        END IF;
 
        -- Axis 2: Discriminator Match (High-Performance Column Matcher)
        IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter IS NOT NULL AND v_blueprint.partition_filter != '' THEN
            -- Standard Equality Matcher
            FOR v_match IN SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*=\s*''([^'']+)''', 'gi')) LOOP
                DECLARE
                    v_actual_value TEXT := to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_match[1];
                    v_matches BOOLEAN := v_actual_value = v_match[2];
                BEGIN
                    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'equality_filter', jsonb_build_object(
                        'column', v_match[1],
                        'expected', v_match[2],
                        'actual', v_actual_value,
                        'matches', v_matches
                    ));
                    IF NOT v_matches THEN
                        v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                        EXIT;
                    END IF;
                END;
            END LOOP;
 
            -- Generic IN Clause Matcher
            IF v_filter_matches AND v_blueprint.partition_filter ~* 'IN\s*\(' THEN
                DECLARE
                    v_in_match record;
                BEGIN
                    FOR v_in_match IN
                        SELECT (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[1] as col,
                               (regexp_matches(v_blueprint.partition_filter, '(\w+)\s*IN\s*\(([^)]+)\)', 'gi'))[2] as vals
                    LOOP
                        DECLARE
                            v_actual_value TEXT := to_jsonb(NEW)->>v_in_match.col;
                            v_allowed_values TEXT[];
                        BEGIN
                            v_allowed_values := ARRAY(
                                SELECT trim(both '''' from x)
                                FROM unnest(string_to_array(replace(v_in_match.vals, ' ', ''), ',')) x
                            );
                            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'in_filter', jsonb_build_object(
                                'column', v_in_match.col,
                                'allowed_values', v_allowed_values,
                                'actual', v_actual_value,
                                'matches', v_actual_value = ANY(v_allowed_values)
                            ));
                            IF NOT (v_actual_value = ANY(v_allowed_values)) THEN
                                v_filter_matches := FALSE;
                                EXIT;
                            END IF;
                        END;
                    END LOOP;
                END;
            END IF;
        END IF;
 
        IF v_filter_matches THEN
            v_found_blueprint := TRUE;
            v_blueprint_checked := v_blueprint.name;
            v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'blueprint_matched', jsonb_build_object(
                'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
                'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
                'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version
            ));
            EXIT;
        END IF;
    END LOOP;
 
    IF NOT v_found_blueprint THEN
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_blueprint_found', jsonb_build_object(
            'organization_id', NEW.organization_id,
            'leave_type_id', NEW.leave_type_id,
            'blueprints_checked', v_blueprint_checked
        ));
 
        -- Flush telemetry: 1 write, status = OK (graceful skip)
        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'no_blueprint'), v_events);
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;
 
    -- [ESM PROVISIONING]
    SELECT * INTO v_esm_definition FROM automation.esm_definitions esm
    WHERE esm.blueprint_id = v_blueprint.id
      AND (esm.metadata->>'blueprint_version')::INT = v_blueprint.version LIMIT 1;
 
    IF NOT FOUND THEN
        v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'no_esm_definition', jsonb_build_object(
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_version', v_blueprint.version
        ));
 
        PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
            NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
            jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'no_esm_definition'), v_events);
        RETURN NEW;
    END IF;
 
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'esm_definition_found', jsonb_build_object(
        'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
        'esm_version', v_esm_definition.version
    ));
 
    -- Provision Instances
    INSERT INTO automation.bp_instances (id, organization_id, blueprint_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, status)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_blueprint.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'ACTIVE')
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET status = 'ACTIVE';
 
    v_start_stage_id := v_esm_definition.definitions->>'startStateId';
    v_final_stage_id := COALESCE(NEW.stage_id, v_start_stage_id);
 
    SELECT s->>'category' INTO v_stage_category
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;
 
    INSERT INTO automation.esm_instances (id, organization_id, esm_definition_id, blueprint_version, entity_schema, entity_type, entity_id, stage_id, status, blueprint_id, stage_category)
    VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.organization_id, v_esm_definition.id, v_blueprint.version, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, v_final_stage_id, 'Active', v_blueprint.id, v_stage_category)
    ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET stage_id = EXCLUDED.stage_id, stage_category = EXCLUDED.stage_category;
 
    -- [WF EVENTS]
    SELECT s->>'on_entry_event_name' INTO v_on_entry_event
    FROM jsonb_array_elements(v_esm_definition.definitions->'stages') s WHERE s->>'id' = v_final_stage_id;
 
    IF v_on_entry_event IS NOT NULL THEN
        INSERT INTO automation.wf_events (organization_id, trigger_schema, trigger_table, trigger_type, trigger_data, context_entity_schema, context_entity_type, context_entity_id, status)
        VALUES (NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, v_on_entry_event, jsonb_build_object('new', to_jsonb(NEW)), v_entity_schema, v_entity_type, NEW.id, 'pending')
        ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    END IF;
 
    -- [BINDING]
    BEGIN
        NEW.automation_bp_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.automation_esm_instance_id := NEW.id;
        NEW.stage_id := v_final_stage_id;
    EXCEPTION WHEN undefined_column THEN NULL; END;
 
    -- Flush telemetry: 1 write, full trace
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'OK',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object(
            'outcome', 'success',
            'blueprint_id', v_blueprint.id,
            'blueprint_name', v_blueprint.name,
            'esm_definition_id', v_esm_definition.id,
            'bp_instance_id', NEW.id,
            'esm_instance_id', NEW.id,
            'final_stage_id', v_final_stage_id
        ), v_events);
 
    RETURN NEW;
 
EXCEPTION WHEN OTHERS THEN
    -- Flush telemetry even on error: 1 write
    v_events := automation.tel_event(v_events, 'exception', jsonb_build_object(
        'sqlstate', SQLSTATE,
        'sqlerrm', SQLERRM
    ));
    PERFORM automation.tel_flush(v_trace_id, 'esm_orchestration_before_insert', v_start_time, 'ERROR',
        NEW.organization_id, v_entity_schema, v_entity_type,
        jsonb_build_object('outcome', 'exception'), v_events, SQLERRM);
    RAISE;
END;$$;


SET default_tablespace = '';

SET default_table_access_method = heap;

--
-- Name: bp_fragments; Type: TABLE; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE automation.bp_fragments (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    key text NOT NULL,
    name text NOT NULL,
    category text,
    definition jsonb NOT NULL,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT true,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now()
);


--
-- Name: bp_instances; Type: TABLE; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE automation.bp_instances (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid NOT NULL,
    blueprint_id uuid NOT NULL,
    blueprint_version integer NOT NULL,
    entity_schema text NOT NULL,
    entity_type text NOT NULL,
    entity_id uuid NOT NULL,
    status text DEFAULT 'ACTIVE'::text NOT NULL,
    context jsonb,
    started_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    completed_at timestamp with time zone,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    correlation_id text,
    CONSTRAINT bp_instances_status_check CHECK ((status = ANY (ARRAY['ACTIVE'::text, 'COMPLETED'::text, 'CANCELLED'::text, 'ERROR'::text])))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY automation.bp_instances FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;


--
-- Name: TABLE bp_instances; Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON TABLE automation.bp_instances IS 'Tracks active process instances linked to a specific entity and blueprint version.';


--
-- Name: COLUMN bp_instances.blueprint_version; Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON COLUMN automation.bp_instances.blueprint_version IS 'The specific version of the blueprint this instance is running against.';


--
-- Name: COLUMN bp_instances.context; Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON COLUMN automation.bp_instances.context IS 'Runtime data specific to this process instance (e.g., variables).';


--
-- Name: COLUMN bp_instances.correlation_id; Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

COMMENT ON COLUMN automation.bp_instances.correlation_id IS 'Cross-process correlation key. Example: Lead-to-Cash flow: Opportunity -> Project -> Delivery all share one correlation_id. Enables process mining across business boundaries.';


--
-- Name: bp_process_blueprints; Type: TABLE; Schema: automation; Owner: -
--

CREATE TABLE automation.bp_process_blueprints (
    id uuid DEFAULT gen_random_uuid() NOT NULL,
    organization_id uuid,
    name text NOT NULL,
    description text,
    entity_schema text NOT NULL,
    entity_type text NOT NULL,
    definition jsonb NOT NULL,
    version integer DEFAULT 1 NOT NULL,
    is_active boolean DEFAULT false NOT NULL,
    metadata jsonb DEFAULT '{}'::jsonb NOT NULL,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now() NOT NULL,
    created_by uuid,
    updated_by uuid,
    blueprint_type text DEFAULT 'lifecycle'::text NOT NULL,
    intent text,
    partition_filter text,
    CONSTRAINT bp_process_blueprints_blueprint_type_check CHECK ((blueprint_type = ANY (ARRAY['lifecycle'::text, 'approval'::text, 'orchestration'::text, 'agentic'::text]))),
    CONSTRAINT valid_blueprint_definition CHECK (public.jsonb_matches_schema('{
            "type": "object",
            "required": ["name", "entity_schema", "entity_type", "blueprint_type", "lifecycle"],
            "properties": {
                "name": {"type": "string"},
                "entity_schema": {"type": "string"},
                "entity_type": {"type": "string"},
                "blueprint_type": {
                    "type": "string",
                    "enum": ["lifecycle", "approval", "orchestration", "agentic"]
                },
                "lifecycle": {
                    "type": "object",
                    "required": ["startStateId", "stages"],
                    "properties": {
                        "startStateId": {"type": "string"},
                        "stages": {
                            "type": "array",
                            "minItems": 1,
                            "items": {
                                "type": "object",
                                "required": ["id", "name", "category"],
                                "properties": {
                                    "id": {"type": "string"},
                                    "name": {"type": "string"},
                                    "category": {
                                        "type": "string",
                                        "enum": ["NEW", "IN_PROGRESS", "CLOSED_WON", "CLOSED_LOST", "CANCELLED"]
                                    }
                                }
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }'::json, definition))
);

ALTER TABLE ONLY automation.bp_process_blueprints FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;


--
-- Name: TABLE bp_process_blueprints; Type: COMMENT; Schema: automation; Owner: -

COMMENT ON TABLE automation.bp_process_blueprints IS 'Defines the high-level business process blueprints (Source of Truth). Contains stages, automations, SLAs.';

    success_threshold numeric(10,4),

    treatment_metrics jsonb,
    p_value numeric(6,5),
    winner text,
    created_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    updated_at timestamp with time zone DEFAULT now(),
    CONSTRAINT pm_experiments_control_percentage_check CHECK (((control_percentage >= 0) AND (control_percentage <= 100))),
    CONSTRAINT pm_experiments_status_check CHECK ((status = ANY (ARRAY['draft'::text, 'active'::text, 'paused'::text, 'completed'::text, 'rolled_back'::text]))),
    CONSTRAINT pm_experiments_winner_check CHECK ((winner = ANY (ARRAY['control'::text, 'treatment'::text, 'inconclusive'::text])))
);
